shows.name: The Lives of Others
shows.description_short: Keira Knightley stars in a new stage adaptation of Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck’s story of censorship, surveillance, art and politics.
shows.description_full:
An extraordinary story of artistic censorship, state surveillance, and the relationship between art and politics is heading to the West End! Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck’s Academy Award-winning “triumph of nuanced tension” (Radio Times) is adapted for the stage for the first time by Robert Icke at the Adelphi Theatre, a short walk from the Victoria Embankment. Book your tickets to experience The Lives of Others in London for a limited time this autumn.

Transport to 1984 East Berlin, where Stasi officer Gerd Wiesler is spying on playwright Georg Dreyman and actress Christa-Maria Sieland from a nearby attic. Whilst Wiesler covertly monitors them for signs of opposition, he becomes absorbed in their lives... and ultimately conflicted by the surveillance and corruption carried out by the GDR regime he serves. Will the power of art be able to change the Stasi officer’s perspective on morality?

Inspired by Florian Henckel von Donnersmarck’s childhood visits to East Germany, The Lives of Others was created in response to a fear of a totalitarian state. The film was released in 2006 in Germany, where it was praised for how accurately it recreated the paranoia and clandestine rebellion experienced by people living in the shadow of the Berlin Wall.

It won several awards, including the Academy Award for Best International Feature Film and the BAFTA for Best Film Not in the English Language. The play is the first time it has been adapted for the stage.

The lead cast for The Lives of Others :

Keira Knightley is an Academy Award-nominated actress known for her extensive work in film and TV, as well as on stage, where she made her Broadway debut in 2015. Her credits include Atonement, Love Actually , The Pirates of the Caribbean, and The Misanthrope .

Luke Thompson plays the role of Georg. He is best known for his role in the hit Netflix series Bridgerton , as well as theatre credits including A Little Life , Hamlet and Oresteia .

Stephen Dillance stars as Gerd Wiesler. He has a well-established career on stage and screen, with credits including Game of Thrones , The Crown , A Long Day’s Journey Into Night and When We Have Sufficiently Tortured Each Other .

Produced by Sonia Friedman, The Lives of Others is directed by Robert Icke, who has collaborated with the writer and director of the original film to adapt this drama for the West End stage. Known for creating emotionally intense adaptations of classics, his recent credits include Uncle Vanya , The Doctor, and Player Kings .

Icke is joined on the creative team by designer Hildegard Bechtler, lighting designer Jon Clark and sound designer Giles Thomas. The Lives of Others features original music by Max Richter.

Arriving in the West End this autumn for a limited time, this brand-new psychological surveillance thriller reveals the power of listening. Brought to the stage by an exceptionally talented cast, including Academy Award-nominated actress Keira Knightley, this will showcase just how gripping theatre can be. Book your tickets for The Lives of Others at Adelphi Theatre today!

shows.name: Jesus Christ Superstar
shows.description_short: Tim Sheader's exhilarating revival of Andrew Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice's iconic musical, Jesus Christ Superstar, is back in the West End.
shows.typical_age_range: TBC. Children under 4 not permitted. Under 15s must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Following phenomenal demand for Jesus Christ Superstar at the London Palladium , Tim Sheader's electrifying production transfers to Theatre Royal Drury Lane in the heart of Covent Garden this autumn. Sam Ryder stars in the “palpably energetic” (LondonTheatre1) revival of Andrew Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice’s iconic rock-musical, which charts the last days of Jesus Christ’s life. Secure your tickets for this must-see theatre event of the year today!

Jesus Christ Superstar retells the final days of Jesus, from the perspective of his disciple Judas Iscariot. The production is set against a backdrop of political unrest and growing public fascination with Jesus Nazareth, who continues to challenge the Roman authorities with his growing influence over people. Whilst the growing tension between his followers, religious leaders, and the Roman authorities fizzes underneath, Jesus Christ Superstar will ultimately lead to betrayal and crucifixion.

The musical features the timeless rock anthems, including “I Don’t Know How to Love Him”, “Gethsemane”, “Heaven on Their Minds”, and the title number “Superstar”.

Sam Ryder reprises his role as Jesus as the show transfers to Drury Lane. The British singer-songwriter rose to international fame after representing the United Kingdom at the Eurovision Song Contest in 2022, where he placed second. His power ballad "Space Man" was the UK's best result in over two decades. Jesus Christ Superstar is his West End debut.

Further casting is to be announced.

Olivier Award winner Tim Sheader directs the production ( Hello, Dolly!, To Kill A Mockingbird ), alongside choreography from Olivier winner and Regents Park Open Air Theatre Artistic Director Drew McOnie ( In the Heights, Carousel ). They reunite after working on the 2016/17 Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre production of Jesus Christ Superstar. Tony winner Tom Scutt ( Cabaret, Fiddler on the Roof ) designs.

The creative team also includes Musical supervision by Tom Deering ( Standing at the Sky’s Edge ), lighting design by Lee Curran ( A Streetcar Named Desire ), sound design by Adam Fisher ( Sunset Boulevard ), and fight direction from Kate Waters ( War Horse ).

If you missed out on the event this summer at the London Palladium, now is your time to experience one of musical theatre’s electrifying productions. This timely resurrection for a classic will “really rock” (Guardian) in the West End this autumn - secure your tickets for Jesus Christ Superstar at Theatre Royal Drury Lane today.

shows.name: Jesus Christ Superstar
shows.description_short: Eurovision star Sam Ryder leads this rock‑powered revival of Regents Park's critically-acclaimed production of the iconic musical.
shows.description_full:
The five‑star rock‑musical phenomenon Jesus Christ Superstar comes to the London Palladium in the heart of Soho, following critically‑acclaimed runs at the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre in 2016 and 2017. This “gorgeous, thrilling, heavenly” (Guardian) staging of Andrew Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice’s sung-through rock-musical about the last days of Jesus Christ’s life, and his betrayal by Judas Iscariot, comes to the West End for a strictly limited run. Eurovision star Sam Ryder makes his West End debut in the lead role, before transferring the show for Jesus Christ Superstar at Theatre Royal Drury Lane later in the year.

Set in the final days of Jesus’s life, Jesus Christ Superstar follows the growing tension between Jesus of Nazareth and his disciple Judas Iscariot, who fears that Jesus’s message has been distorted and that his growing influence will provoke Roman retaliation. As crowds gather and expectations rise, Jesus struggles with his role as both spiritual leader and political figure. The story, told largely from Judas’s perspective, charts betrayal, sacrifice, and the weight of destiny.

This new staging brings the 2017 open‑air triumph into the West End, featuring all your favourite emotionally charged rock anthems, including “I Don’t Know How to Love Him”, “Gethsemane”, “Heaven on Their Minds”, and the title number “Superstar”.

Jesus Christ Superstar began life as a concept album in 1970, created by composer Andrew Lloyd Webber and lyricist Tim Rice. Its bold fusion of rock music and biblical storytelling quickly captured public imagination, with the album topping charts in several countries. Following its success, the show made its stage debut on Broadway in 1971 and premiered in London’s West End at the Palace Theatre in 1972, where it ran for over eight years.

It has since become one of the world’s most enduring and frequently revived musicals, inspiring numerous international tours, film adaptations and reimaginings - including most recently a concert staging at the Hollywood Bowl starring Cynthia Erivo and Adam Lambert. This 2026 revival revisits the critically acclaimed Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre 2016 production, which received a second staging at the Open Air Theatre in 2017 and received both an indoor staging at the Barbican and a US tour. The magic is brought indoors once again for a West End limited season this summer.

Sam Ryder plays the lead role of Jesus. Sam Ryder is a British singer-songwriter who rose to international fame after representing the United Kingdom at the Eurovision Song Contest 2022, where he finished in second place with the power ballad “Space Man” - the UK’s best result in over two decades. Ryder first gained attention through social media, sharing powerful covers during lockdown, and his debut album, There’s Nothing But Space, Man!, topped the UK Albums Chart in 2022. Known for his signature falsetto and positive energy, Jesus Christ Superstar at the London Palladium marks Ryder’s highly anticipated West End debut. Further casting includes:

Across the run, the role of King Herod will be shared by six performers:

The production reunites the Olivier-winning creative team from the 2016/2017 Regent’s Park Theatre Open Air Theatre production. Olivier winner Tim Sheader ( Hello, Dolly!, To Kill A Mockingbird ) directs, with choreography from Olivier winner and Regents Park Open Air Theatre Artistic Director Drew McOnie ( In the Heights, Carousel ). Tony winner Tom Scutt ( Cabaret, Fiddler on the Roof ) designs.

Musical supervision is by Tom Deering ( Standing at the Sky’s Edge ) with lighting design by Lee Curran ( A Streetcar Named Desire ), sound design by Adam Fisher ( Sunset Boulevard ), and fight direction from Kate Waters ( War Horse ).

There should be just one thing on your mind - grabbing tickets for the summer’s most unmissable theatrical event. Sam Ryder makes his West End debut in this award-winning staging that “really rocks” (Guardian). So don’t miss out, because in the Palladium, you might just feel like a “Superstar” - secure your tickets for Jesus Christ Superstar at the Palladium today.

shows.name: Kinky Boots
shows.description_short: The fabulous and feel-good Tony, Olivier and Grammy-winning musical struts back into the West End, starring Strictly’s Johannes Radebe.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 8+. Under 4s not permitted.
shows.description_full:
Everybody say yeah - Kinky Boots is back in London! Fresh from a critically acclaimed UK tour, Strictly Come Dancing star Johannes Radebe leads the cast as the larger-than-life drag queen Lola, who teams up with mild-mannered Charlie Price to help save his father's shoe factory. This joyous, award-winning musical is a “sizzling five-star masterpiece” (Chronicle Live). Kinky Boots is strutting into the London Coliseum for a strictly limited run - so don’t miss out, secure your tickets today.

When Charlie Price’s father unexpectedly dies, he inherits the family business - Price and Son, a shoe factory in Northampton that is down at heel. As Charlie struggles to revive the failing business, his relationship with pushy fiancée Nicola becomes strained when she encourages him to sell the factory to a corporation that wants to turn it into flats, and Charlie is at risk of losing not only his relationship but the livelihood that meant so much to his father. Downtrodden, Charlie can think of no other solution... then in sashays Lola, a drag artist in need of a sturdy pair of heels. With the help of Lola and assembly line worker Lauren, who is harbouring a secret crush, Charlie sets to work designing the ultimate “kinky boot”, hoping to tap into a niche market and save the factory. But not everyone is happy about working with a drag queen, and Lola has troubles of her own… Will Charlie and Lola be able to save Price and Son, or will their differences cause them to stumble?

Kinky Boots is based on the 2005 film of the same name, which starred Joel Edgerton and Chiwetel Ejiofor and drew inspiration from a remarkable true story. The show premiered on Broadway in 2013 with Pose star Billy Porter playing the fabulous Lola. It snagged six Tony wins, including Best Musical, and later transferred to London’s West End, where it won three Olivier Awards. In 2014 the production won a Grammy for Best Musical Theatre Album. This fierce new revival comes to the West End following a fabulous UK tour in 2025.

South African dancer and choreographer Johannes Radebe stars as Lola. Best known as a professional on Strictly Come Dancing, in 2021 Radebe was a runner-up on the show with his partner John Whaite, as part of the first male same-sex pairing in the history of UK Strictly . Radebe made his musical theatre debut in 2025 as part of the UK tour of Kinky Boots and has previously toured with dance show Burn the Floor . He has also appeared on Doctor Who , RuPaul's Drag Race: UK vs. the World , Celebrity Masterchef and The Great British Sewing Bee .

Further casting for the West End transfer is to be confirmed.

Kinky Boots has a book by Harvey Fierstein, based on the screenplay by Geoff Deane and Tim Firth, with music and lyrics by Cyndi Lauper. Nikolai Foster directs.

Harvey Fierstein is a Tony Award-winning American actor, playwright, and screenwriter. One of the first openly gay celebrities in Hollywood, Fierstein is a trailblazer and LGBTQ+ activist. His credits include Torch Song Trilogy , La Cage aux Folles and Newsies . In 2007, Fierstein was inducted into the American Theatre Hall of Fame, and in 2025 he was awarded a Lifetime Achievement Tony Award.

Cyndi Lauper is an American singer, songwriter and actress, best known for her iconic hits "Girls Just Wanna Have Fun" and "Time After Time". Lauper is a Grammy, Emmy and Tony Award winner and inductee of the Rock’n’Roll and Songwriters Hall of Fame. In 2013, she made history when she won the Tony for Best Original Score for Kinky Boots , as the first woman to win the category by herself, rather than as part of a songwriting team.

Nikolai Foster is an award-winning British-Danish theatre director and the current Artistic Director of the Curve Leicester. Foster’s credits include Billy Elliot the Musical at the Curve, Grease at the Dominion Theatre, The Wizard of Oz at the London Palladium and UK tour and A Chorus Line at Sadler’s Wells.

Fall head over heels for this energetic, feel-good musical that our Kinky Boots review describes as full of “heart and joy” It returns to London for a special limited run. Johannes Radebe is truly “mesmerising” (Chronicle Live) as Lola, so grab your glitter and get ready to strut down to the West End this Spring 2026! Don’t miss out – book your tickets for Kinky Boots at the London Coliseum today!

shows.name: Les Miserables
shows.description_short: Hear the people sing in the world's longest-running musical, which has been in the West End since 1985.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 7+. Children under 3 not permitted. Please be advised that children under the age of 16 may be refused entry if they are not sitting next to an accompanying adult.
shows.description_full:
Les Miserables is the world’s longest-running musical, seen by over 120 million people globally. It flies its flag over London at the Sondheim Theatre in sight of Soho and Chinatown.

The current production of Boublil and Schönberg’s classic has been hailed as ‘Les Mis for the 21st Century’ with new sets, costumes and video design. Its heart-wrenching, life-affirming story of redemption and revolution makes it one of the best musicals in the West End .

The show follows former convict Jean Valjean, who spent 19 years in jail for stealing a loaf of bread. Revolution is in the air in France as Valjean breaks his parole to start a new life, while the merciless police inspector Javert hunts him down.

Adapted from French author Victor Hugo’s novel, there are many iconic songs in the Les Miserables musical including “One Day More”, “I Dreamed a Dream” and “Bring Him Home”.

Les Miserables tickets are some of the hottest in London, and this award-winning musical continues to rouse audiences to their feet for a storming ovation night after night.

Based on the novel of the same name by Victor Hugo, Les Miserables tells an epic story that covers almost two decades. Starting in a remote area of France in 1815, chain-gang convict Jean Valjean is granted parole by staunchly law-abiding prison guard Javert. Finding that nobody will treat him fairly as a convict, Valjean steals some precious silverware from a bishop who shelters him. When he is caught by police, the bishop tells them that the silverware was a gift and Valjean is free to go. The bishop tells him to take this gift and live a good life devoted to God.

Years later Valjean has become a successful factory owner and mayor. When single mother Fantine is harassed into leaving the factory, she is forced into prostitution, leaving her daughter Cosette in the keeping of the greedy and cunning innkeeper couple the Thénardiers. Valjean finds the child and takes her into his care. More years pass and Valjean and Cossette find themselves in the wake of rising trouble as the 1832 Paris Uprising is about to commence. Meanwhile, the vengeful Javert still hunts Valjean’s every move and wishes to see him back in chains.

Claude-Michel Schönberg wrote the music and co-wrote the book with Alain Boublil. Boublil also co-wrote the original French lyrics with Jean-Marc Natel. Herbert Kretzmer adapted the lyrics for the English-language production.

The musical is based on the 1862 novel by Victor Hugo, which is widely considered to be one of the greatest works of 19th-century literature.

The London production, which was the first English-language version of the musical, first opened on 8th October 1985 at the Barbican Centre. The show transferred to the Palace Theatre later that year and in 2004 moved to the Queen’s Theatre, now called the Sondheim Theatre. It is the longest-running musical in West End history and the 2nd longest-running show after The Mousetrap. It closed only briefly in 2019 while the Sondheim Theatre was refurbished but an All-Star Concert version played at the Gielgud Theatre during this time. The production re-opened with updated staging in December 2019.

Main cast:

Find out more about the stars in their multitudes with our Les Mis West End cast guide.

This crowning glory of the West End is still captivating audiences after almost 40 years, and there is no show as resonant and emotionally stirring. From the highs of love to the lows of war, sacrifice and loss, Les Mis proves time and time again why it is one of the greatest musicals and greatest stories ever told.

faq.question: How can I get cheap Les Misérables tickets?
faq.answer: Discount tickets are rare, which makes the show a very popular feature of seasonal offers such as London Theatre Week and Winter Sales. These are often the best times to book tickets as you will find selected Stalls and Dress Circle tickets at lower prices than any other time in the year. If you are looking for great value, book for a weeknight rather than the popular weekend performances, and try to find a hidden gem seat that offers a better view than expected. For example, Grand Circle E1 is sold as restricted view, with prices starting at £30, but only misses a tiny, mostly unused amount of the stage. Meanwhile, the very back of the Dress Circle may seem undesirable, but seats such as M15 and M16 have received 5-star View scores from SeatPlan users.

shows.name: Oliver!
shows.description_short: Matthew Bourne directs a thrilling revival of Lionel Bart’s musical, featuring “Consider Yourself”, “I’d Do Anything” and more.
shows.description_full:
Oliver! can consider itself part of the West End family once more! Lionel Bart’s “musical masterpiece” (Sunday Times) is breathtakingly reconceived by producer Cameron Mackintosh and award-winning director and choreographer Matthew Bourne ( Nutcracker! ) in this intimate and imaginative new production. Whether you have seen it before or are ready to experience it for the very first time, the story of the boy who dared to ask for more is pure magic at Shaftsbury Avenue's Gielgud Theatre.

When young orphan Oliver escapes the harsh Victorian workhouse, he is taken under the wing of one of London’s most lively gangs. Led by wily Fagin and the Artful Dodger, Oliver joins a team of light-fingered pickpockets and finds a friend in warm-hearted Nancy. But when he is wrongly arrested for stealing, Oliver’s new life and rag-tag family come under threat… until an unexpected saviour shows him that true happiness might be just around the corner.

Based on Charles Dickens’ Oliver Twist , Lionel Bart’s Oliver! is one of the most beloved shows of all time, and its sensational score - including “Food Glorious Food”, “Consider Yourself”, “You’ve Got to Pick-a-Pocket or Two”, “I’d Do Anything”, “Oom Pah Pah” and “As Long As He Needs Me” - and vivid storytelling have made it the gateway to musical theatre for generations of children. Since debuting at Wimbledon Theatre in 1960, it has played to sold-out crowds in London and on Broadway, toured the UK and been adapted into an Academy Award-winning film.

Powerhouse producer Cameron Mackintosh and Olivier Award-winning director/choreographer Matthew Bourne have collaborated on this thrilling revival of Lionel Bart’s musical, which is in London direct from its premiere at Chichester Festival Theatre. Read our review of Oliver at the Gielgud Theatre .

The duo are responsible for some of UK theatre’s biggest productions: Mackintosh has driven the success of Les Miserables , The Phantom of the Opera and Mary Poppins , whilst Bourne’s bright and bold ballets including Nutcracker! and The Red Shoes are genre-defining spectacles that audiences return to again and again.

Main cast:

Who will buy Oliver musical tickets? Everyone! It is one of the greatest shows of all time and a crowd-pleaser that never fails to make your heart swell and tears flow. Rediscover the magic or experience it as a family as it returns to London for an unforgettable new season.

shows.name: The Lion King
shows.description_short: Disney’s spectacular musical features stunning puppetry and an unforgettable score by Elton John. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 3s not permitted. Please be advised that children under the age of 16 may be refused entry if they are not sitting next to an accompanying adult.
shows.description_full:
Disney’s The Lion King is a spectacle like nothing else – exploding onto the stage in a mirage of vibrant colour, rhythm and life at the Lyceum Theatre in the heart of London's West End.

Julie Taymor’s visionary adaptation of the classic Disney animated film was game-changing when it premiered in 1997. Fusing puppetry and design with traditional African dance, costume and song, this groundbreaking production brings every character to life.

The Lion King transports audiences to the plains of the Serengeti as the story charts Simba’s journey from a naive cub to fulfilling his destiny as King of the Pridelands.

The Lion King tells the story of Simba, who is forced into exile after the death of his father and must embark on a journey to reclaim his place as King of the Pridelands. The musical transforms Disney's beloved film - that is based loosely on Shakespeare's Hamlet - into an unforgettable theatrical event that sees the sights and sounds of the Serengeti brought to life with puppetry, set design, spectacular costumes and dance.

The Lion King is based on Disney's award-winning film of the same name. The musical's book is by Roger Allers and Irene Mecchi, who worked on the film as a co-director and screenwriter respectively. Tim Rice and Elton John wrote the show's music and lyrics, including the hit songs "Circle of Life" and "Hakuna Matata".

Main cast:

There are over 50 cast members in the West End production, including a number of child performers in the roles of young Simba and young Nala. Some of the cast have been in The Lion King for many years, including Shaun Escoffery and George Asprey , who have performed as Mufasa and Scar since 2008.

The production first opened at the Lyceum Theatre on 19 October 1999, and is one of the longest-running West End musicals. In 2019, the musical celebrated its 20th anniversary with a special gala in support of the Elton John AIDS Foundation and the Royal Academy of Music. Director Julie Taymor's puppetry and costumes remain incredibly innovative and spectacular more than 20 years since its premiere, ensuring that The Lion King remains a hugely popular, must-watch show for audiences of all ages.

This enduring musical has long reigned as one of the best London shows for kids and adults alike. Get The Lion King tickets for all the family to gasp in awe as the animals of Pride Rock swoop, prowl, roar and so much more in front of your eyes!

shows.name: The Devil Wears Prada
shows.description_short: The iconic film becomes a fun and fabulous new musical, with songs by Elton John. Dress to impress because Miranda Priestly is on her way!
shows.description_full:
All right theatre fans, gird your loins! The Devil Wears Prada musical is making the West End the most stylish place to be as it struts past the shops of Oxford Street and into the Dominion Theatre.

Based on the book and film starring Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway, this hilariously biting story of fashion’s cut-throat industry and the lengths we’ll go to achieve our dreams gets a musical makeover with songs by Elton John. It’s going to be groundbreaking!

Fresh college graduate Andy longs to get a foot in the door to start her journalism career, so when an opening comes up at the prestigious Runway magazine, she rushes to apply. Soon, she’s scored a job working for the terrifying but iconic editor-in-chief Miranda Priestly, and it’s a thankless task!

As Andy sacrifices her personal life to please Miranda and keep up with her gruelling schedule, she is seduced by the glitz and glamour she thought she hated. How far will she go to win at her career, and is selling her soul worth it?

The Devil Wears Prada is one of the most popular comedy films of the 21st century and acidic, tyrannical Miranda Priestly is a character that everyone loves to hate - and loves to quote! The musical adaptation has been in the works for years, and after a trial run in Chicago, it’s the debut of the season for London theatregoers in autumn 2024.

Main cast:

With a book by Kate Wetherhead, Broadway director Jerry Mitchell helms The Devil Wears Prada in London, as both director and choreographer. Tony Award-winner Mitchell is no stranger to taking films and turning them into stage magic - his credits include Legally Blonde: The Musical, Pretty Woman: The Musical and Kinky Boots . Expect some fabulous dancing, sharp humour and exhilarating fun as he gets his hands on Andy, Miranda and co.!

This fun and fabulous show has a score by one of the greatest living musicians, Elton John. John’s musical credits include The Lion King and Billy Elliot: The Musical , and for The Devil Wears Prada he has worked with lyricist Shaina Taub to transform the story into an amazing new musical.

Songs in The Devil Wears Prada musical’s Chicago season included “Dress Your Way Up" and "When a Legend Is Born”, but for the full experience and some brand new aspects, book tickets to see its full run in the West End.

This new musical is hotter than hell and ready to shake up the West End! Fun, fabulous and with a great story of self-discovery, The Devil Wears Prada is a show you definitely don’t want to miss.

shows.name: The Phantom of the Opera
shows.description_short: Andrew Lloyd Webber’s sensational musical has been enchanting audiences since 1986 with its haunting tale of love and obsession.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Under 4s not permitted.
shows.description_full:
Playing for almost 39 years at His Majesty’s Theatre (formerly Her Majesty’s Theatre) just around the corner from Pall Mall, Andrew Lloyd Webber's global smash-hit The Phantom of the Opera has become the second longest-running musical in the West End, and the longest-running show on Broadway. With an iconic score, stunning sets, gorgeous costumes and dazzling special effects, this unmissable production is essential viewing, earning a reputation as one of London's best musicals .

Based on Gaston Leroux’s early 20th-century gothic novel, The Phantom of the Opera is a story of love and obsession set within the grand scenery of the Paris Opéra House. The titular Phantom, a musical genius who lurks beneath the building, sets his sights on beautiful and talented young soprano Christine. But when Christine falls in love with the handsome Vicomte Raoul, the Phantom’s love sours into rage and jealousy as he stops at nothing to possess the object of his desires.

This is a musical that will take your breath away with its clarity of vision and stunning vocal performances that rival some of the biggest opera sopranos. After watching Christine and the Phantom's tragic tale unfold, you may even want to book opera tickets in London for a taste of the genre that inspired so much of this famous story.

Andrew Lloyd Webber collaborated with musician Richard Stilgoe and lyricist Charles Hart to adapt The Phantom of the Opera into the hugely popular, award-winning West End musical. Its score - including "The Music of the Night" and the iconic title song "The Phantom of the Opera" - is known worldwide. The Phantom of the Opera is based on French author Gaston Leroux’s novel, which was published in the early 1900s.

No, The Phantom of the Opera is not an opera. It is a musical set within a Parisian opera house in the early 20th century. The word "opera" in the title refers to the setting and not the genre of the show.

While the show is not an opera, it is a mostly 'sung-through' musical, meaning there is very little spoken dialogue. The music in the show has some operatic themes but follows the musical format throughout and also includes influences from other genres such as rock, pop and choral.

Andrew Lloyd Webber’s unforgettable score is known around the world and some of the most instantly recognisable in all of theatre. Andrew Lloyd Webber developed the libretto alongside Tony Award nominee Richard Stilgoe ( Starlight Express; Cats ), with lyrics from Academy Award nominee Charles Hart ( Aspects of Love ) and Stilgoe.

Songs in The Phantom of the Opera include the mighty and ominous title number, the famous duet “All I Ask of You”, “Masquerade” and “The Music of the Night”. It was nominated for the Tony Award for Best Original Score back in 1988, and won Best Musical at the Tonys and Best New Musical at the Olivier Awards in 1986. Most impressive however is the music’s enduring relevance in the public’s imagination; no one can hear the opening notes of “The Phantom of the Opera” without being transported to the drama and unique atmosphere of the live show.

Main cast:

The musical had its world premiere in London in 1986. There have been more than 13,000 performances in London, including a special 25th Anniversary filmed production at the Royal Albert Hall in 2011.

All visitors to the West End should book The Phantom of the Opera tickets for a truly magical theatrical experience that must be seen to be believed.

One of the West End’s longest-serving and most beloved shows, The Phantom of the Opera is an unmissable musical that has thrilled and inspired generations of theatregoers. Descend into the depths of the Parisian Opera House and discover the secrets of the Phantom for yourself at His Majesty’s Theatre.

shows.name: Grace Pervades
shows.description_short: Ralph Fiennes stars in this vibrant new play about theatrical legends Henry Irving and Ellen Terry from Tony-winning playwright David Hare.
shows.description_full:
Ralph Fiennes stars in the London transfer of Grace Pervades , a new play from Olivier and Tony Award-winning playwright David Hare, about the lives of acclaimed Victorian actors Henry Irving and Ellen Terry. Fresh from a critically acclaimed transfer at the Theatre Royal Bath, this “exceptional” (Guardian) work comes to the Theatre Royal Haymarket, just a stone's throw from Piccadilly Circus. Set an alert to book today!

Grace Pervades follows the lives of groundbreaking Victorian actors Henry Irving and Ellen Terry, interweaving their personal and professional lives in an “elegant love letter to theatre” (Guardian). Irving is an established performer and manager whose wife and children come second to his career and his aim to make theatre respectable; Terry is an acclaimed actress and, scandalously, an unwed mother, who longs to step into the leading roles. As their relationship deepens from mentee and mentor to something more, Irving and Terry must navigate the changing face of the art form they have fought to shape, and contend with what their theatrical legacy might be.

Based on the remarkable true stories of Henry Irving, who was the first actor to be knighted, and Ellen Terry, whose decades-long career was highly influential, Grace Pervades sheds a new light on these theatrical icons. Titled after a scathing review of a performance of Terry’s, in which a reviewer stated “Grace pervades the hussy”, and made disparaging remarks about her children, who were born out of wedlock, Grace Pervades premiered at the Theatre Royal Bath in summer 2025 to critical acclaim. The production comes to the Theatre Royal Haymarket for a strictly limited run - make sure to book early for this “fascinating, absorbing and very funny” (Financial Times) play.

Ralph Fiennes and Miranda Raison reprise their roles as Henry Irving and Ellen Terry from the Theatre Royal Bath run.

Ralph Fiennes is a British actor, best known for his roles in Conclave, The English Patient, Schindler’s List, The Menu and as Lord Voldemort in Harry Potter . Fiennes is also an acclaimed stage actor who has performed with the RSC and at the National Theatre, and has won Tony Awards for his roles in Bryan Friel’s Faith Healer and Hamlet , directed by Jonathan Kent. As well as his Tony Awards, Fiennes is a BAFTA, Emmy, Golden Globe and Academy Award winner. Miranda Raison is a British actor, best known for her role as Jo Portman in BBC One spy drama Spooks . Her credits also include roles in Doctor Who , HBO’s Warrior, and Silk for BBC One. Her stage roles include originating the role of Anne Boleyn in Howard Brenton's Anne Boleyn at Shakespeare’s Globe, Jez Butterworth’s The River at The Royal Court and starring as Hermione in The Winter’s Tale at the Garrick Theatre, directed by Kenneth Branagh.

They will be joined in the West End by:

Grace Pervades is written by David Hare. Hare is an acclaimed British playwright and screenwriter. He is a three-time Tony winner, a BAFTA and Olivier Award winner, and Golden Globe and Academy Award nominee. Hare’s credits include Academy Award-nominated screenplays for The Hours , starring Meryl Streep and The Reader with Fiennes and Kate Winslet, and plays Racing Demon at the National Theatre, Amy's View at the National Theatre and on Broadway, starring Judy Dench, and Straight Line Crazy at the Bridge Theatre, also starring Fiennes. In 1998, Hare was knighted in the Queen's Birthday Honours List for his services to theatre.

Jeremy Herrin directs. Herrin is a British director, a co-founding director of Second Half Productions and a former Artistic Director of acclaimed theatre company Headlong. He is an Evening Standard Theatre Award and South Bank Sky Arts Award winner and three-time Olivier Award winner. Herrin’s credits include the critically acclaimed stage adaptations of Hilary Mantel’s novels Wolf Hall and Bringing Up the Bodies for the RSC and West End, People Places and Things at the National Theatre and Long Day's Journey into Night at the Wyndham's Theatre, starring Brian Cox.

Don’t miss your opportunity to see Ralph Fiennes and Miranda Raison’s “mesmerising” (Times) performances in this powerful new play from award-winning playwright David Hare. Grace Pervades is “luminous, magnetic” (What’s On Stage) and comes to London for a strictly limited run in April 2026. Set an alert today and make sure to secure your tickets for Grace Pervades at the Theatre Royal Haymarket as soon as they go on sale!

shows.name: Wicked
shows.description_short: Discover the untold story of the witches of Oz, with spellbinding songs, a powerful story and incredible visual spectacles. Best for age 7+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 7+. Under 5s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Wicked is the smash-hit musical that has been casting its magical spell over the Apollo Victoria Theatre, close to Buckingham Palace, since 2006. “One of the most successful musicals of all time” (BBC News) and the winner of more than 100 awards worldwide including three Tony Awards and a Grammy Award, it is the 11th longest-running production in West End history.

Stephen Schwartz and Winnie Holzman’s must-see musical phenomenon, based on Gregory Maguire’s acclaimed novel, reveals the untold story of how the Wicked Witch of the West and Glinda the Good fulfilled their destinies. Discover a beguiling backstory and future possibilities for the characters from L. Frank Baum’s The Wonderful Wizard of Oz in this visually stunning musical.

Backed by an unforgettable score, including modern musical theatre staples such as "Defying Gravity" and "The Wizard and I", find out why it is “London’s #1 show” (LBC) and book tickets now.

The story serves as a prequel to The Wizard of Oz that explores the origins of Elphaba, the Wicked Witch of the West, and Glinda the Good Witch. Starting years before Dorothy sets foot on the yellow brick road, the story opens at Shiz University when Elphaba and Glinda are assigned as roommates on their first day.

Although the pair couldn't be more different at first glance, they soon strike up a friendship, which is tested by the arrival of the handsome Fiyero and their opposing views on politics and authority. When Elphaba's talent for magic takes her all the way to the Emerald City and a meeting with the Wizard, Glinda joins her. But they soon discover that the Wizard is not all he seems, with consequences that leave Elphaba branded wicked throughout the whole of Oz.

Academy Award-winning composer Stephen Schwartz ( The Prince of Egypt ) and screenwriter Winnie Holzman adapted the musical from Gregory Maguire's popular fantasy novel of the same name. Maguire's book is itself inspired by L. Frank Baum's The Wizard of Oz .

Wicked is best known for its incredible songs, including "Defying Gravity", "The Wizard and I", "Popular" and "No Good Deed". After almost 20 years, these have become musical theatre standards that are loved just as much as some of the classics!

Main cast:

Idina Menzel, who originated the role of Elphaba on Broadway, also starred in Wicked in the West End when it first opened in 2006.

The production premiered in London on 27 September 2006, after a hugely successful Broadway season. It has been seen by over 10 million people and is the 5th longest-running musical currently playing in the West End.

Wicked tickets remain one of the most popular choices for theatregoers, young and old. Since premiering in London in 2006, this award-winning musical has sold over 9 million tickets and is guaranteed to leave you spellbound.

shows.name: Sinatra The Musical
shows.description_short: This swingin’ new musical, from a Tony and Olivier Award-winning creative team, tells the story of Frank Sinatra’s remarkable career.
shows.description_full:
Ride high this summer as Sinatra the Musical comes to London! This swingin’ new musical from an award-winning creative team tells the story of one of America’s greatest singers - from humble beginnings as the son of Italian Immigrants, to international stardom, to an epic comeback in the aftermath of a career-shattering affair. Sinatra the Musical comes to the Aldwych theatre, just off Drury Lane, following a smash hit premiere in Birmingham in 2023 - secure your tickets today!

It’s New Year's Eve, 1942, and as a singer steps onto the stage of the Paramount Theatre, New York, he has no idea he’s about to give a performance that will change the face of music forever. That skinny, 27-year-old Italian-American is Frank Sinatra, and as his career suddenly goes stratospheric, Frank must balance the love of his wife, Nancy, with the demands of fame.

But with fame comes temptation, and when Frank’s affair with movie star Ava Gardner comes to light, the press turns on their former golden boy, and Frank’s career is sent into a tailspin. Desperate to make things right with his family and to earn back the public’s love, Frank uses his trademark charm, his unparalleled artistry and his scrappy determination to stage what promises to be the biggest comeback in the history of showbiz.

Sinatra The Musical premiered at the Birmingham Rep in 2023, and has long been anticipated for a West End Transfer. The musical features some of Sinatra’s biggest hits, including “One For My Baby“, “That’s Life“, and “The Best Is Yet to Come”.

Initial casting for Sinatra the Musical has been announced, including:

Sinatra the Musical is written by Tony Award-winner Joe DiPietro ( What’s New Pussycat?, Memphis ), and directed and choreographed by Olivier and Tony-winner Kathleen Marshall ( Anything Goes, The Pajama Game ). Music and Lyrics are by Frank Sinatra.

Let yourself be dazzled by old blue eyes this summer with this brand new musical from a Tony and Olivier award-winning team. Featuring your favourite Sinatra tunes and based on the incredible true story of this musical icon, this new musical has plenty of “glitter and dazzle” (Birmingham Pres) and is perfect for Sinatra fans and musical theatre buffs alike. Book your tickets for Sinatra the Musical at the Aldwych Theatre today!

shows.name: War Horse
shows.description_short: A boy searches for his horse during the First World War as the award-winning smash hit, featuring breathtaking puppetry, returns to London.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
The National Theatre’s landmark play War Horse gallops back to its original home, the Olivier Theatre on London’s South Bank, in a major revival of the world-famous production. Blending astonishing stagecraft, life-sized puppets and a sweeping musical score that carries you from rural Devon to the battlefields of France, War Horse is unmissable, and returns to London for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets today.

At the outbreak of the First World War, a Devonshire farm boy, Albert, is separated from his beloved horse, Joey, when Joey is sold to the cavalry and sent overseas. Too young to enlist, Albert can’t let go and instead sets out on a daunting journey that pulls him into a changing world, where ordinary lives are swept up by extraordinary events, determined to bring his friend home. Across village fields, training grounds, and the brutal churn of the trenches, War Horse follows the bond between boy and horse as it’s tested by distance, duty, and survival.

War Horse first premiered at the National Theatre’s Olivier Theatre in October 2007. Based on Michael Morpurgo’s beloved children's novel, the production is known for its innovative, incredibly life-like puppetry work from the Handspring Puppet Company - the puppeteers spent time at stables during rehearsals, studying how horses move so the onstage behaviour felt authentic.

Critically acclaimed, the production returned for a further run at the National, transferred to the West End and later expanded internationally, including a major Broadway run. War Horse has earned numerous awards, snagging several Olivier and Tony Awards, including a Special Tony Award recognising the puppet work.

Casting for this revival includes:

War Horse is based on the novel by Michael Morpurgo, adapted for the stage by Nick Stafford ( Battle Royal, The Snow Queen ).

Tom Morris ( Dr Semmelweis, Touching the Void ), who co-directed the original production alongside Marianne Elliot, returns to direct alongside revival director Katie Henry.

Set and costume design is by Rae Smith ( The Importance of Being Earnest, Translations ). The horse puppets are by Handspring Puppet Company, with puppet design credited to Adrian Kohler and Basil Jones. Music and sound for the production is by Adrian Sutton with John Tams.

Saddle up: Joey’s heading home. War Horse is truly unforgettable theatre - this epic story of love, friendship and courage features breathtaking puppetry and a stirring score. Returning to the Olivier Theatre for a strictly limited run, you won’t want to miss this iconic, Tony and Olivier award-winning play. Ready to Ride? Book tickets for War Horse at the National Theatre today!

shows.name: Disney's Hercules
shows.description_short: Experience Disney’s beloved film on stage in this dazzling new musical, featuring “Zero to Hero” and more. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 4s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
A young demi-god’s journey from Zero to Hero becomes a spectacular new musical comedy in Disney’s Hercules at Theatre Royal Drury Lane, off Covent Garden's piazza. Inspired by the 1997 Academy Award-nominated animated film, this West End premiere is a nostalgic crowd-pleaser for all ages, with Alan Menken’s songs - including “A Star is Born” and “The Gospel Truth” - performed alongside brand-new numbers.

Grab your lightning bolts, put on your togas and join Herc, Pegasus and Meg as they journey from Ancient Greece to Mount Olympus and beyond!

High on Mount Olympus, Zeus and Hera welcome a precious baby boy. But destiny interferes, and with vengeful Hades plotting to kill Hercules, he is sent to live on earth as a mortal. It’s not easy being a demi-god surrounded by humans, though, and Hercules struggles to fit in. His outlandish strength and boundless enthusiasm get him in trouble, and soon he sets off to discover his true identity - and stop Hades from overthrowing Olympus once and for all.

Bursting at the seams with colourful settings, a hero to root for, and true love’s kiss, Hercules is one of Disney’s best-loved films, and the perfect West End show to enjoy as a family. Headstrong Megara, witless Panic and Pain, and cantankerous Phil all appear in the production, which creates more Disney magic in Theatreland after the huge success of Frozen and Aladdin .

Main cast:

Relive the magic of Alan Menken and David Zippel’s exhilarating score as top songs including “Zero to Hero”, “The Gospel Truth”, “Go the Distance” and “I Won’t Say (I’m in Love)” all feature. Audiences can also enjoy new songs written exclusively for the musical.

Hercules is transformed from screen to stage by Olivier Award nominee Kwame Kwei-Armah and Tony Award winner Robert Horn, and directed by Tony Award winner Casey Nicholaw, who also directed the Broadway hit Aladdin. Nicholaw and Tanisha Scott’s choreography ensures the musical is as vibrant and dynamic as the film.

A star is born at Drury Lane and we think it will go the distance! Take your kids and watch their faces light up at the incredible stage effects, or treat your inner child with Hercules musical tickets, and enjoy this heroic tale on stage.

shows.name: Matilda the Musical
shows.description_short: Tim Minchin's mischievous musical adaptation is a hilarious, heart-warming modern classic for the whole family. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 4s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult. Booster seats are available at the theatre.
shows.description_full:
Matilda is the perfect example of children's theatre in London that adults will love too, boasting mischief, mayhem and a huge heart. Get your little miracles engrossed in the magic of theatre with tickets to this modern family favourite at the Cambridge Theatre in the heart of Seven Dials and Covent Garden.

Roald Dahl’s much-loved children’s book is given a boisterous musical makeover, with comedian Tim Minchin’s songs - including “When I Grow Up” and “Revolting Children” - injecting a witty breath of fresh air into the tale.

Since bursting into London in 2011 with a technicolour set and incredible cast of young actors, Matilda has caught the imaginations of audiences worldwide, earning 7 Olivier Awards and a celebrated Broadway season.

Matilda the Musical is the story of a young girl who learns to use her remarkable, supernatural talents to find the loving family she deserves. Matilda's parents dislike their daughter, who'd much rather read a book than watch television all day, and at school the child-hating Miss Trunchbull keeps students and teachers in line with terrifying threats. When Matilda meets the gentle Miss Honey, who encourages her love of reading and keen curiosity, she decides that being a little bit naughty is worth it to get back at the bullies.

Playwright Dennis Kelly and comedian and musician Tim Minchin collaborated to write the show, which is adapted from Roald Dahl's popular children's book. Kelly wrote the book, with Minchin creating original music and lyrics for the show.

The songs in the show include "When I Grow Up", "Naughty", "The Chokey Chant" and "Miracle". They are written by comedian and musician Tim Minchin, and combine his witty lyricism with original author Roald Dahl's mischievous storytelling to great effect.

Main cast:

The cast also includes more than 25 talented young performers, including four actresses who alternate the leading role of Matilda Wormwood.

Snatch up Matilda tickets and you’ll be utterly enchanted, whether you’re 6 or 60. Borrowing the best parts of Dahl’s dark humour and adding some hilarious new twists, this musical adventure is a truly irresistible treat.

shows.name: The Book of Mormon
shows.description_short: From the creators of South Park this outrageous, multi-award winning musical tells the hilarious story of two naïve Mormon missionaries.
shows.typical_age_range: Recommended 18+. Please be advised that children under the age of 16 may be refused entry if they are not sitting next to an accompanying adult.
shows.description_full:
South Park creators Trey Parker and Matt Stone join forces with Avenue Q ’s Robert Lopez for controversial hit musical The Book of Mormon , which promises to outrage and entertain in equal measure at the Prince of Wales Theatre at the edge of Leicester Square and Chinatown. This story of Mormon missionaries who travel to Uganda is an Olivier Award-winning audience favourite in the West End!

The Book of Mormon plot centres on two Mormon missionaries, Elder Price and Elder Cunningham, who are sent to war-torn Uganda for their two-year mission to bring new people into their church. They find the poverty-stricken local populace to be very angry with God, with a hilariously vulgar song to express it! With a warlord causing atrocities and a temporary home far from the sunny Florida that they hoped for, the pair will need to draw on all their faith and truly “man up” to face the challenges presented to them.

The now familiar The Book of Mormon musical score includes stand-out songs “Hello”, “I Believe" and “Baptize Me”, performed by a talented ensemble cast who ensure the laughs keep flowing.

Trey Parker and Matt Stone, the creators of South Park and outrageous comedy films such as BASEketball and Cannibal! The Musical paired up with Robert Lopez, co-creator of Avenue Q and one of the songwriters on Frozen to write the book, songs and lyrics for the show.

Parker and Stone grew up in Colorado, where they encountered the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints and its members often and have made references to the faith in many of their other works. The story of The Book of Mormon is told in Parker and Stone’s trademark, in-your-face style with no shortage of vulgarity and crass humour, but also shows a warmth for the Church and is much more a satirical satire than a damning parody.

Main cast:

Considered by many to be the funniest musical ever written and equipped with a genuinely uplifting story, you'll definitely want to say "Hello" to The Book of Mormon ! Over a decade since it opened, it is still one of the biggest musicals for the not-so-faint of heart, so book now if you want to laugh until you cry.

faq.question: Is The Book of Mormon really as shocking as people say?
faq.answer: It definitely pushes the boundaries, but that’s part of its charm. The humour is bold, fast and sometimes jaw-dropping, but it’s never mean-spirited. Most people end up laughing harder than they expected, even at the bits they thought they shouldn’t.

faq.question: Do I need to know anything about religion to enjoy it?
faq.answer: Not at all. While the story pokes fun at organised religion, it’s more about people and belief than any specific faith. Even if you’ve never set foot in a church, you’ll get the jokes - and probably recognise the themes.

faq.question: Who is The Book of Mormon best suited for?
faq.answer: People who enjoy clever storytelling, energetic performances and humour that doesn’t play it safe. If you enjoy South Park, The Life of Brian, Avenue Q or The Producers, this musical should be on your list. It’s not for everyone, and that’s exactly why so many people love it.

faq.question: Do you have to be a musical fan to enjoy it?
faq.answer: Not at all. This is definitely a musical for non-musical fans. Even people who don’t usually go for showtunes and chorus lines end up loving The Book of Mormon. The jokes land so hard and so often that you’ll forget you’re watching a musical at all!

shows.name: Mamma Mia!
shows.description_short: Dance the night away to ABBA’s greatest hits at the hilarious West End musical that inspired the film.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Under 3s not permitted. Ages 16 and under must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult. Children sitting separately from adults may be refused entry.
shows.description_full:
Swedish pop legends ABBA join forces with director Phyllida Lloyd for a jukebox musical like no other. Perfect for dancing queens young and old, discover the joyful show that inspired the hit film!

Mamma Mia! at the Novello Theatre in Covent Garden weaves ABBA’s back catalogue into a witty original story. Audiences who couldn't get enough of Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan and Colin Firth on the big screen will adore the West End show, which features ABBA’s best-loved hits, including "Gimme, Gimme, Gimme", "Dancing Queen" and "SOS".

Sophie and her mother Donna have lived peacefully on a Greek island since she was a child. But as Sophie’s wedding approaches, she becomes restless. Who is her father, and would he want to walk her down the aisle?

A peek inside her mum’s diary from 19 years earlier gives her three potential names: Harry Bright, Bill Austin and Sam Carmichael. Throwing caution to the wind, she decides to invite all of them to her wedding without Donna’s knowledge.

This original story was inspired by some of Swedish pop group ABBA’s greatest hits, which are woven throughout the narrative to create cheeky, romantic and heart-rending moments as Sophie comes face-to-face with her dads and Donna has to face up to her past!

ABBA members Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus had an active role in bringing this fantastic show to the stage, which features music and lyrics from the group’s most famous songs. The story was written by playwright Catherine Johnson. Johnson later adapted her script for the successful film version which starred Meryl Streep, Colin Firth and Pierce Brosnan.

Main cast:

It is the West End’s longest-running jukebox musical, having played over 9,000 performances, and Mamma Mia remains a strong crowd-pleaser for all ages. Discover why it’s such a hit for yourself - gather together your pals, dress like you’re ready to party in Greece, and enjoy the iconic sounds of ABBA all over again.

shows.description_short: A daring revival of the hit musical welcomes audiences to 1930s Berlin, via the immersive Kit Kat Club.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 13+. The venue recommends that under 13s not attend the Kit Kat Club.
shows.description_full:
”Come hear the music play” with Cabaret at the Playhouse Theatre! One of London’s hottest musicals beckons you from Embankment into the Kit Kat Club, where the party starts as soon as you step through the doors.

Enjoy the hedonism and tragedy of this classic musical with a hassle-free booking experience at the box office, and e-tickets delivered straight to your inbox. With prices starting at just £30 and group bookings and special dinner packages available to buy online, tickets for Cabaret promise an unforgettable night at one of the West End's most popular shows.

The Master of Ceremonies welcomes you to the decadent world of the dingy, free-spirited cabaret in which English dancer Sally Bowles and American writer Cliff Bradshaw strike up a relationship. Meanwhile, on the streets outside, the Nazi Party is on the rise and the city’s freedom is under threat.

Director Rebecca Frecknall’s Olivier Award-winning revival of Kander and Ebb’s musical invites you into a strikingly reconfigured Playhouse Theatre. Soak in the sights and sounds of the Prologue Company's unmissable performance, before heading inside to see a show that mixes politics, hedonism, dazzling choreography and iconic songs including “Money”, “Don’t Tell Mama” and "Maybe This Time".

Main Cast:

From 25 May 2026, Jamie Muscato and Joy Woods star as the Emcee and Sally Bowles.

Cabaret transports audiences to the heart of Germany’s tumultuous past and regales you with unforgettable characters and songs. Don’t miss this poignant, steamy and sensational show: book your tickets now and get ready to be captivated by the Kit Kat Club.

shows.name: Moulin Rouge! The Musical
shows.description_short: Immerse yourself in a world of spectacle and faded glamour at this unmissable jukebox musical, based on Baz Luhrmann’s film.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 12+. Under 5s not permitted.
shows.description_full:
Come what may, London! Based on the iconic 2001 film starring Nicole Kidman and Ewan McGregor, the Olivier Award-nominated Moulin Rouge! The Musical brings faded Parisian glitz and glamour to the Piccadilly Theatre, just off Regent Street, with an incredible mash-up of songs from hitmakers including Adele, Beyonce, Elton John and David Bowie.

Secure your spot at this lavish musical with hassle-free booking direct from the box office and e-tickets delivered to your inbox. Whether you’re attending for date night, hosting a group or just want to experience the iconic film live on stage, you will find the best prices by booking online today!

Set in the Montmartre Quarter of Paris as the 20th century stirs to life, Moulin Rouge! The Musical beckons audiences into a buzzing world of excess. Drinks flow freely, bohemians rub shoulders with aristocrats, and beneath the glaring spotlight and splendour, a courtesan and a composer fall in love – and fall into tragedy.

This is a true feast for the senses, with gorgeous set design by Derek McLane that spills out into the auditorium. Velvet, elephant, bright lights and sumptuous red backgrounds make this one of the most beautiful shows in the West End. It is so dazzling that it was nominated for five Olivier Awards including Best Set Design, and won for Best Costume Design.

Inspired by Baz Luhrmann’s revolutionary film, Moulin Rouge! The Musical remixes songs from the movie with 21st-century chart-toppers including Lady Gaga and U2.

Fans of the film can enjoy all of the songs that made it a modern classic, including “Your Song” and “Material Girl”, “Lady Marmalade” and “Heroes”, along with a dizzying number of modern additions from Lorde’s “Royals” to Katy Perry’s “Firework”. Most spectacular of all is the updated “Elephant Love Medley”, a showstopping centrepiece that elevates the romance to new heights. Songs in the medley include “Don’t Speak”, “Love Hurts”, “Torn” and “Take On Me”.

Featuring a mix of pop hits and sumptuous, show-stopping sets, Moulin Rouge! The Musical is perfect for anyone wanting to paint the town red with an evening of music and heart-breaking romance. Book your tickets online today and get ready to enter the biggest whirlwind world of romance, tragedy and showbiz in Theatreland.

shows.name: Hamilton
shows.description_short: Lin-Manuel Miranda’s hugely popular musical Hamilton follows the life of Alexander Hamilton, one of the Founding Fathers of America.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Under 3s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
If there’s a record to be broken, or an award to be won, the chances are Lin-Manuel Miranda’s smash-hit musical Hamilton has achieved it. Since premiering in London at the Victoria Palace Theatre, a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, in 2017, tickets for this incredible show have remained the hottest in town.

With the sold-out excitement of Hamilton around the globe, the West End production continues to be one of the most in-demand shows. Don’t give away your shot by missing out on Hamilton tickets.

Hamilton is about one of America’s lesser-known founding fathers, Alexander Hamilton, a baseborn orphan from a West Indies immigrant background who travelled to the New York to study at King’s College in 1776 and rose through the ranks of American Politics to become George Washington’s right-hand man during the Revolutionary War and the first Secretary of the Treasury once the nation gained independence.

Far from a dry history lesson, this hit musical tells its story with a fresh mix of hip-hop, blues and R&B fused showtunes that have taken Broadway and the West End by storm. In fact, the Hamilton Broadway cast album has been the number one seller on Billboard’s US Cast Albums Chart every year from 2015 to 2022.

Lin-Manuel Miranda wrote the book, music and lyrics for Hamilton . Not only that, but he also starred as Alexander Hamilton in the musical from its early workshops in 2013 up until its Broadway debut in 2015, which earned him a Tony Award nomination for Best Actor in a Musical. The same year, Hamilton won Best Book of a Musical and Best Original Score.

Miranda has become a near household name for his contribution to musical theatre and film, as well as his acting roles on stage and television. His first Broadway musical In the Heights won the Tony for Best Original Score in 2008 and was adapted into a successful film in 2021. He has contributed hit songs to Disney films Moana and Encanto . Recently Miranda played aeronaut Lee Scoresby in hit TV series His Dark Materials .

Main cast:

The company is a dynamic group of actors, singers and dancers who bring the show's unforgettable, high-energy musical numbers to life.

Hamilton is one of the most successful musicals in both Broadway and West End history. When it premiered off-Broadway at The Public Theatre in February 2015, it sold out almost immediately. When it transferred to Broadway, it took $30 million in ticket sales before it even opened and was the second-highest-grossing show on Broadway for the Labour Day week in 2015. In November 2016 it set a record for the most money grossed in a single week on Broadway, taking $3.3 million in one week.

Hamilton holds the record for most Tony Award nominations, with 16 nominations at the 2016 ceremony, of which it won 11. The only musical to have won more Tony Awards is The Producers , which won 12.

Hamilton West End has its fair share of awards, with 7 Olivier Awards from 13 nominations at the 2018 ceremony.

Hamilton tickets are the most sought-after in the West End, and with so many accolades and awards to its name, it's easy to see why. Don’t throw away your shot - book now and find out why it is an essential, historic London theatre experience that is not to be missed.

shows.name: Avenue Q
shows.description_short: The raunchy and hilarious Tony-winning puppet musical  -  “Sesame Street for grown-ups” (EW) - returns to the West End.
shows.description_full:
Come home to Avenue Q as the Tony-winning, boundary-pushing musical returns to London’s West End this Spring. This raunchy, raucous musical is “Sesame Street for grown-ups” (Entertainment Weekly) and tells the story of bunch of dissatisfied friends, both puppet and human, who live together in the titular borough of NYC. Avenue Q is an equal opportunity offender - with love, sexuality, and privilege all in the firing line. The musical comes to the Shaftesbury Theatre, in the heart of Covent Garden, for the first time in over two decades - you won’t want to miss it, read our review of Avenue Q secure your tickets today!

Set in a shabby outer borough of New York City, Avenue Q is a raucously funny and surprisingly heartfelt coming-of-age musical where humans and puppets live side by side. The story follows Princeton, a bright-eyed (puppet) college graduate who moves into Avenue Q with big dreams and a tiny bank account. As he tries to figure out his purpose in life, he meets a host of colourful neighbours, including Kate Monster, an earnest teaching assistant; Rod, a closeted banker and Nicky, his slacker roommate; landlord and former child-star Gary Oldman (yes, the Gary Oldman) and Trekkie Monster, who’s definitely not your average children’s puppet!

As the characters navigate adulthood, love, friendship, and disappointment, Avenue Q balances outrageous humour with relatable moments. From job-hunting to heartbreak, it captures the awkward hilarity of trying to grow up when you’re not quite sure how. With songs like “It Sucks to Be Me”, “There’s a Fine Fine Line”, “Schadenfreude”, “If You Were Gay”, and “Everyone’s a Little Bit Racist,” the show isn’t afraid to tackle taboo topics with tongue firmly in cheek.

Avenue Q premiered off-Broadway in 2003, winning a Lucille Lortel and Outer Critics Circle award, before transferring to the John Golden Theatre on Broadway later that same year. The musical went on to win three Tony awards in 2004, including the award for Best Musical, which the edgy, underdog production controversially won over the smash-hit Wicked. The musical ran until 2009 on Broadway, when it transferred back off-Broadway and ran for a further ten years. The show has since toured internationally and across the United States. In 2006, Avenue Q premiered in the West End and ran for five years at various different venues. While an anniversary concert was held in 2024 to celebrate the show’s 18th anniversary, this brand new revival marks the first full run of the show in London in almost two decades.

Casting includes:

Avenue Q has music and lyrics by Robert Lopez and Jeff Marx, with a book by Jeff Whitty.

Lopez and Marx are long-term collaborators who have worked together on the music and lyrics for the musical Book of Mormon , the musical episode of Scrubs , the songs for Disney films Frozen 1 and Frozen 2 as well as music for Bear in the Big Blue House and The Book of Pooh .

Lopez is an American songwriter and playwright. He is one of only 27 people in the world to hold an EGOT, meaning he has won an Emmy, Tony, Oscar and Grammy. Lopez is the youngest person to have won an EGOT, as well as the only person to have won all four awards more than once. As well as his collaborations with Marx, his credits include work on Disney’s Coco , South Park and Marvel series’ WandaVision and Agatha All Along .

Jeff Marx is an American composer and lyricist. Aside from his collaborations with Lopez, his credits include Rick & Steve: The Happiest Gay Couple in All the World, Home Street Home and How To Win A High School Election .

Whitty is an American playwright, actor, and screenwriter. He is a Tony, Independent Spirit and Writers Guild of America Award winner, and an Academy Award and BAFTA nominee. His credits include the Libretto for Bring it On the Musical , The Further Adventures of Hedda Gabler , and Oscar-nominated film Can You Ever Forgive Me?.

All your fantasies can come true with Avenue Q ! This outrageous, hilarious, “savvy, sassy and eminently likeable” (The New Yorker) musical returns to the West End for the first time in almost two decades. Book your tickets for Avenue Q today and check out this memorable musical for yourself at the Shaftesbury Theatre!

shows.name: Titanique
shows.description_short: Climb aboard the funniest, campest musical in town, girlfriends! Forget Jack and Rose… Céline Dion is the star of this Off-Broadway hit.
shows.description_full:
Near, far, wherever you are… make sure you’re heading for the West End, where Off-Broadway hit Titanique is docked in Piccadilly Circus at the Criterion Theatre! Academy Award-winning film Titanic meets Canadian songstress Céline Dion in this camp, chaotic show that has been regaling audiences in New York since 2022. One of the greatest love stories of all time transforms into a hysterical parody featuring Dion’s greatest hits, so climb aboard, girlfriends, for this “sensational, hilarious and deranged” (The New York Post) jukebox musical!

The devastating story of Jack and Rose’s love affair aboard the Ship of Dreams is turned upside down, as iconic singer Céline Dion hijacks a Titanic Museum tour and decides to give her version of events. This wild parody brings a new, unique perspective to some of the film’s most beloved scenes, as Dion - claiming to have survived the sinking herself - camps it up, sets her diva scale to 100 and belts out her greatest hits, including “My Heart Will Go On”, “All by Myself” and “To Love You More”.

If you think you know Titanic , think again and get ready for a show packed with pop culture references, ‘90s nostalgia, swoon-worthy romance, and a whole new iceberg that gets in between our favourite lovers… one of Canada’s most famous faces!

Titanique has been an Off-Broadway favourite since 2022, winning the 2023 Lucille Lortel Award for Outstanding Musical. With its mix of crowd-pleasing music, ironic humour and pop culture references to everything from RuPaul’s Drag Race to Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt , critics have raved as much as the theatregoers watching it every night.

Co-created by writers Tye Blue, Marla Mindelle and Constantine Rousouli, you’re “guaranteed to laugh your iceberg off” (People Magazine) at this “camp reimagining of the maritime blockbuster” (New York Times) - this isn’t just a jukebox musical, it’s “the silliest, smartest, gayest, FEVER-DREAM MIRACLE [...] created to manifest pure joy” (The Daily Beast).

Main cast:

Get ready for a musical voyage you won’t forget in a hurry with Titanique tickets! Brimming with wit, humour and a slay-fest full of top showstopper tunes, set sail with the most entertaining - and chic - new musical in town.

shows.name: My Neighbour Totoro
shows.description_short: The Olivier Award-winning adaptation of Studio Ghibli’s film is a magical West End experience. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 4s not permitted. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Step into the astonishing world of Studio Ghibli as the Olivier Award-winning My Neighbour Totoro play transfers to the West End for its third season in London. The Royal Shakespeare Company's “dazzling” (The Guardian) tribute to the imagination of childhood is a theatrical delight, with gorgeous stagecraft and puppetry by Jim Henson’s Creature Shop. After two sold-out seasons at the Barbican Theatre, don’t miss your chance to see the classic story transformed on stage at the Gillian Lynne Theatre on Covent Garden's iconic Drury Lane this year.

My Neighbour Totoro is the story of sisters Satsuki and Mei, who move closer to their sick mother while she receives treatment in hospital. As the long summer months stretch ahead of them, they escape into the forests near their home and befriend incredible spirits including the larger-than-life forest protector, Totoro.

Hayao Miyazaki’s beloved 1988 film is reimagined by the Royal Shakespeare Company ( Matilda the Musical ) and original composer Joe Hisaishi. The film’s famous score is performed live with new orchestrations by Will Stuart.

My Neighbour Totoro was a sold-out sensation during its world premiere in 2022 and won six Olivier Awards including Best New Play - it was so successful, that it returned for another season over Christmas 2023. With its brilliant combination of music, beautiful set design and jaw-dropping puppets built by Jim Henson’s Creature Shop, theatregoers are transported directly into the whimsical world of the film.

Main cast:

“Utterly charming” (Culture Whisper), “awe-inspiring” (Time Out), “beguiling” (The Stage), “beautiful” (Financial Times) and “dazzling” (The Guardian): critics love Totoro and it has become a mega-hit that audiences want to see again and again! This five-star adaptation is event theatre, creating unforgettable moments of awe for fans of the film and families who want to be inspired by the magic of live performance.

Unleash your imagination with the “enchanting” (The Guardian) show that is unlike anything else in the West End. Nothing says “theatre magic” as well as My Neighbour Totoro tickets, so find your spirit and join the adventure this year.

shows.description_full:
Before the world turned upside down, Hawkins was an average town… Enter the realm of Stranger Things: The First Shadow in the world premiere of this spectacular new play, from the team behind the seminal Netflix series.

This incredible story takes you deeper than ever before into the world of the Netflix series. Enter Charing Cross Road's Phoenix Theatre, and exit with some mindblowing revelations… and many more questions!

Jim Hopper’s car won’t start and Joyce Maldonado is counting down the days ‘til she graduates and skips town. Hawkins in 1959 is a normal place with normal worries, until new student Henry Creel arrives. His family is hoping for a fresh start, but the shadows of the past are hard to outrun, and they might just hold the key to the future.

Dive back into the Upside Down with jaw-dropping storytelling and effects that will immerse you in the uncanny world of the award-winning series.

Main Cast:

This brand-new story is written by Kate Trefry, based on an idea by the Duffer Brothers, Trefry and Jack Thorne. Fans of Stranger Things will recognise the Duffers and Trefry’s names instantly, as they are the creators and writers of the original series. It is a real treat to have them putting brand new parts of the story on the stage, and this is set to be one of the most ambitious productions of recent years.

Visionary director Stephen Daldry ( Billy Elliot; The Inheritance ) brings the mythology of Stranger Things: The First Shadow at the Phoenix Theatre to thrilling life, as the universe expands further than ever before.

Get ready to question everything you thought you knew at this epic theatrical event! Stranger Things: The First Shadow is a breathtaking new dimension to explore if you can’t get enough of the Netflix series, or simply can’t wait for the next installment to arrive on your screens.
