shows.name: High Society
shows.description_short: The vibrant and hilarious Cole Porter returns to London for the first time in over a decade, starring Felicity Kendal.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 8+. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Get ready to misbehave as Cole Porter’s Golden Age musical High Society returns to London for the first time in over a decade. This brand new production stars The Good Life’s Felicity Kendal and Call The Midwife's Helen George, and comes to the Barbican, just a short walk from Moorgate, for a strictly limited run this spring. Don’t miss this hilarious, witty and sharply satirical musical all about the romantic misadventures of a stuck-up socialite.

Set in a wealthy enclave near New York City, High Society follows the romantic entanglements of Tracy Lord, a poised and independent socialite preparing for her second wedding. When her charming ex-husband, Dexter Haven, returns unexpectedly, with a pair of undercover reporters in tow, old flames reignite, and complications ensue. As the wedding approaches, Tracy must untangle her feelings and choose between her polished fiancé, her persistent ex, and a journalist who sees past her upper-crust exterior. Packed with Cole Porter classics like ”Ridin’ High”, “Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?”, and “Let’s Misbehave,” the musical blends screwball comedy, class satire, and heartfelt romance into a sparkling tale of love, second chances, and social spectacle.

High Society is based on Philip Barry’s comedy play The Philadelphia Story, as well as the 1956 film adaptation, High Society . The musical blends Porter's original songs with a new book by Arthur Kopit and additional lyrics by Susan Birkenhead. The show first premiered at the American Conservatory Theater in San Francisco in 1997 before transferring to Broadway. The Broadway cast featured a 12-year-old Anna Kendrick in the role of Dinah Lord, a performance that earned Kendrick a Theatre World Award and a Tony nomination. The production ran for 144 performances and earned two Tony nominations before closing in 1998.

Across the pond, a different version of High Society, using Cole Porter’s songs but a separate book by Richard Eyre, premiered in the West End at the Victoria Palace Theatre in 1987, running for over 400 performances. The UK premiere of the Arthur Kopit version came later, with a summer 2003 production at Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, followed by a UK tour, and a West End transfer, running from 2005 to 2006.

One of the most acclaimed revivals came in 2015 at The Old Vic in London. Directed by Maria Friedman, this production presented High Society in the round, creating an intimate and immersive atmosphere. This brand new 2026 production marks the show's return to London for the first time in over a decade.

Strictly Come Dancing stars Felicity Kendal ( Arcadia, Rosemary & Thyme, The Good Life ) and Helen George ( Call The Midwife, The Three Musketeers ) lead the cast, with further casting including:

Music and lyrics are by Cole Porter. Porter (1891-1964) was one of the most celebrated American composers and lyricists of the 20th century, renowned for his sophisticated, witty, and urbane songs. He composed dozens of Broadway scores during the 1920s through the 1950s, and his best-known works include Anything Goes, Kiss Me, Kate, and Can-Can . Kiss Me, Kate earned Porter the first-ever Tony Award for Best Musical in 1949, along with awards for Best Composer and Lyricist. Arthur Kopit (1937 - 2021) was a Tony- and Pulitzer-nominated American playwright and librettist. He rose to prominence in the early 1960s with his absurdist satire Oh Dad, Poor Dad, Mamma’s Hung You in the Closet and I’m Feelin’ So Sad , which premiered Off-Broadway and quickly became a critical success. His other notable works include plays Indians, Wings, End of the World with Symposium to Follow, and the book for Broadway musical Nine , adapted from Fellini’s film 8½. Directed by Rachel Kavanaugh, who has previously directed at the Royal Shakespeare Company, Chichester Festival Theatre, and Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, choreogrpahy by Anthony Van Laast, and Musical supervision and direction from Stephen Ridley.

You’ll be “Ridin’ High” this spring as this brand new production of High Society twirls onto the stage at the Barbican for a strictly limited run. Strictly star Helen George and TV Legend Felicity Kendal lead the cast as this fizzing, hilarious and vibrant music returns to London for the first time in over a decade. You won't want to miss it - book tickets for High Society at the Barbican today!

shows.name: Dracula
shows.description_short: Cynthia Erivo stars in a stunning one-woman version of the vampire classic, from the award-winning team behind The Picture of Dorian Gray.
shows.description_full:
Wicked superstar Cynthia Erivo returns to the West End this February in a sensational one-woman production of Dracula at the Noel Coward Theatre. Erivo plays all 23 roles, including the titular vampire himself, in this brand new one-person play adaptation of the iconic gothic horror novel. Be sure to secure your tickets now - this production is bloody brilliant!

Jonathan Harker, a newly qualified solicitor, is hired by mysterious nobleman Count Dracula to help with the purchase of a London property and travels to Transylvania to visit the count in his home. But when Jonathan arrives, all is not as it seems. The terrified locals warn Harker about the enigmatic Count, wolves disrupt his journey, and Jonathan is unsettled by the sinister and intimidating castle. Though the Count seems genial and welcoming, if not a little strange, Harker soon realises he is being kept prisoner - and the trio of beautiful but unsettling women who live in the castle with the Count have developed a fascination with him…

Disoriented, delirious and fearing for his life, Harker is determined escape and make it back to London, and to his fiancée Mina Murray. But will Jonathan make it home safely, or will the vampiric Count haunt him all the way back to England?

Dracula is based on the 1897 Gothic horror novel by Irish novelist Bram Stoker. One of the most famous books in English literature, the novel is originally told in epistolary form - through letters, diary entries and newspaper articles. Highly influential, Dracula reshaped the way vampires are seen in popular culture. Dracula has been adapted into many different forms, including for stage, TV, into operas, musicals and has received several iconic film adaptations, which include a 1931 adaptation starring Bela Lugosi as Dracula, a 1958 film starring Christopher Lee and the 1992 film starring Gary Oldman as Dracula and Keanu Reeves as Jonathan Harker.

In this brand new adaptation, which premiered at the Sydney Theatre Company in 2024, all 23 characters are brought to life by just one actor in a radical reimagining of the seminal vampire story. The production is the third in director Kip Williams’ “game-changing gothic cine-theatre trilogy” (Time Out), which includes The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde and the award-winning adaptation of The Picture of Dorian Gray .

Cynthia Erivo stars in this one-woman production.

Erivo is a British actor and singer. She is an Emmy, Grammy, and Tony Award winner and Oscar, Golden Globe and BAFTA nominee. Best known for her critically acclaimed portrayal of Elphaba in the recent Wicked film adaptations, Erivo’s other credits include Chewing Gum for Channel 4, Luther: The Fallen Sun for Netflix, The Color Purple at the Menier Chocolate Factory and on Broadway, and an Oscar-nominated portrayal of Harriet Tubman in 2019 film Harriet .

Dracula is adapted and directed by Kip Williams. Williams is an award-winning Australian director and writer. The former artistic director of the Sydney Theatre Company, Williams is best known for his innovative adaptations of classic works, which often incorporate technology in an experimental fashion. His credits include a gender blind production of Lord of the Flies starring Mia Wasikowska, The Resistible Rise of Arturo Ui starring Hugo Weaving, and an Olivier-winning, Tony-nominated one-person production of The Picture of Dorian Gray , starring Succession ’s Sarah Snook.

This sensuous, thrilling, “clever and playful production” (Guardian) is unmissable theatre. Critically acclaimed director Kip Williams and powerhouse performer Cynthia Erivo collaborate for the first time to create an utterly fang-tastic show! So don't miss an innovative new take on a Victorian Gothic classic - secure your tickets for Dracula at the Noel Coward Theatre today.

shows.name: The Mousetrap
shows.description_short: Agatha Christie’s classic whodunnit is the world's longest running show. Can you keep the secret?
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 12+. Under 5s not permitted.
shows.description_full:
Agatha Christie’s Mousetrap is a London institution, having been performed continuously in the West End since 1952.

It started life as a radio play written at the request of the BBC for Queen Mary, and holds the distinction of being the longest-running show in the world. The Mousetrap has performed through 14 different prime ministers’ terms in office, from Winston Churchill to Rishi Sunak, and over 460 actors have appeared in the show.

The Mousetrap tickets provide an authentic taste of theatre history. Just make sure you don’t spoil the ending for any future theatregoers!

Young couple Mollie and Giles Ralston have set up a country guest house at Monkswell Manor, but their grand opening is upstaged by a grizzly murder nearby. Trapped by a snowstorm and with the killer heading their way, the residents and guests of Monkswell must figure out who is guilty.

With everyone a suspect, this ultimate whodunit has plenty of twists and turns to trap even the canniest crime fiction fans - and a surprise ending that has remained a secret for decades.

This now-iconic play has a small but brilliant cast of characters, all with secrets to keep. Can you work out whodunit?

Mollie and Giles Ralston are a newly married couple and the country estate owners that form the setting of The Mousetrap . As the snowstorm hits, they are accommodating five guests: eccentric and flighty young architect Christopher Wren; acerbic retiree Mrs. Boyle; well-mannered and eagerly helpful Major Metcalf; stand-offish and secretive young professional Miss Casewell; and last-minute guest Mr Paravicini, a charming rogue who seems to delight in the fear and uncertainty weighing down on everyone else.

Detective Sergeant Trotter arrives at Monskwell Manor to keep the guests safe and find the killer in their midst. Will he be able to work out who is responsible before it is too late… and more importantly, can you beat him to it?

The Mousetrap first opened in London in 1952, and it thrilled West End audiences for the entirety of Queen Elizabeth's reign, and into King Charles'. Its original West End home was the Ambassadors Theatre, and in 1974 it transferred to its current home, St Martins Theatre.

Queen of Crime Agatha Christie wrote the whodunit as a gift for Queen Mary, airing on BBC radio on 30 May 1947 under the name Three Blind Mice . Christie is possibly the world’s most famous crime writer, having written over 60 murder mysteries and created the iconic characters of Poirot and Miss Marple. The Mousetrap had its world premiere at Theatre Royal, Nottingham in 1952, before touring the UK ahead of its West End debut on 25 November 1952. Christie famously stated in her autobiography that she initially expected The Mousetrap play to run for no more than eight months.

Now in its 70th year, The Mousetrap is the longest-running show in the West End and in the world, beating Les Misérables, The Phantom of the Opera, The Woman in Black and The Lion King .

The Mousetrap sets a new record at every curtain up. When you enter St Martins Theatre, you will see a large wooden tally that displays the exact performance number you are attending. The play currently exceeds 28,000 West End performances and its only extended closure was in 2020, when the Covid-19 pandemic forced all West End venues to shut.

In its impressively long history, The Mousetrap has also hit several cast records. One of its original cast members, the late Deryk Guyler, still “performs” each night in a recorded radio news bulletin, whilst David Raven earned a place in the Guinness Book of Records as “Most Durable Actor” for his role as Major Metcalf at 4,575 performances between 1957 and 1968. Nancy Seabrooke also made it into the record books, racking up 15 years as an understudy in The Mousetrap .

Main cast:

Casting for The Mousetrap traditionally changes every year, with many famous faces appearing since its first West End performance. Richard Attenborough starred as the original Detective Sergeant Trotter and stars including Patrick Stewart and Julie Walters performed in a special charity performance to mark the play’s 60th anniversary - and 25,000th performance - in 2012.

The Mousetrap has a brilliantly eccentric history, with its most well-known feature being the shock twist right at the end of the show. Audiences are asked to “preserve the tradition by keeping the secret locked in your hearts”, ensuring The Mousetrap’s intricate plot and compelling surprises remain just as exciting now as they were on opening night.

There has never been a film adaptation of The Mousetrap , and it is unlikely to happen any time soon! The play’s contract states that no film can be made until it has been closed in the West End for at least six months. The production famously managed to move theatres in 1974, and install a brand new set in 2000, without losing a single performance - so the only way to see this thrilling murder mystery is by booking The Mousetrap London tickets.

An icon of the West End, The Mousetrap is one of the best London plays , and a must-see for any theatre-lover. Full of surprises, chills and thrills that remain just as sharp today as they were in 1952, this is an unmissable classic that continues to attract audiences from across the world.

shows.name: Oh, Mary!
shows.description_short: This deliciously dark Tony Award-winning comedy turns history on its head and puts Mary Todd Lincoln in the spotlight.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 14+. Under 4s not permitted.
shows.description_full:
Oh, Mary! is a hilarious dark comedy that puts First Lady, Mary Todd Lincoln, firmly in the spotlight, telling the story of this famous couple as you’ve never seen them before. Written by Tony Award winner, Pulitzer finalist and self-proclaimed idiot Cole Escola, this hilarious spoof history play dazzled audiences in New York, and London audiences can finally enjoy this “rude, crude, darkly funny, deeply silly” (SeatPlan) show as Oh, Mary! comes to the West End's Trafalgar Theatre, just off Whitehall, at last. Check out our review of Oh,Mary! starring Catherine Tate and book your tickets today!

Think you know the story of Abraham Lincoln, his wife Mary Todd Lincoln, and the assassination that rocked a nation? Think again! While everyone might know the story of the American President, who exactly was Mary? Known in the history books as Abraham Lincoln’s wife and mother to his children, the IRL MLT was a troubled and somewhat controversial figure whose life was marred by grief and mental illness.

This riotous black comedy puts a satirical slant on the troubles of this lesser-known First Lady, and runs fast and loose with the facts to shed a queer new light on a not-quite-so-honest Abe. Deliciously dark and sharply satirical, Oh, Mary! uncovers the unrequited yearnings, alcoholism and buried desires of Mrs Lincoln in the weeks leading up to her husband’s assassination, and asks - what were Mary’s dreams when she wasn’t standing dutifully by as First Lady?

Oh Mary! is “the best kind of stupid: a broad, wildly funny comedy that spits in the face of American history” (Variety). The show premiered off-Broadway at the Lucile Lortell Theatre in February 2024 to critical acclaim, receiving several extensions and later transferring to Broadway’s Lyceum Theatre. The show won an Outer Critics Circle Award and an Obie for its off-Broadway run and went on to snatch two Tonys, a GLAAD Media Award and a Drama League Award during its (still ongoing) Broadway run, and was a finalist for the 2025 Pulitzer Prize for Drama.

Main cast:

Catherine Tate stars as Mary Todd Lincoln until 18 July 2026. Her credits include Doctor Who and The Catherine Tate Show on television, and Much Ado About Nothing, opposite David Tennant, on stage.

Performers who have donned MLT’s iconic “brat curls” wig in New York include writer and creator Cole Escola, who originated the role (and won the Tony Award for Best Leading Actor in a Play), star of The Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt and Emmy nominee Titus Burgess, two-time RuPaul's Drag Race w inner and Broadway sensation Jinkx Monsoon, Emmy nominee and star of Netflix’s GLOW Betty Gilpen, and Tony winner and 30 Rock star Jane Krakowski.

Oh, Mary! is written and created by Cole Escola. Escola is a Tony and Writers Guild of America Award-winning writer and performer, and a Pulitzer finalist. Their credits include Difficult People, Hacks, At Home With Amy Sedaris and The Other Two .

The original New York production was directed by Tony Award-winning director and choreographer Sam Pinkleton. Pinkleton’s credits include Natasha, Pierre & The Great Comet of 1812, La Cage Aux Follies, Can I Be Frank , and choreography on Stephen Sondheim’s final show Here We Are .

Oh, Mary! is a “smartly stupid riot” (Variety). Enjoy the story of the Lincolns told from a hilarious, farcical and bold new perspective - just don’t expect historical accuracy! Oh, Mary! pushes President Lincoln out of the limelight and turns it on to one of US history’s most overlooked figures. Don’t miss out on this witty, wild and “gleefully irreverent comedy” (Time Out) - Book your tickets for Oh, Mary! at the Trafalgar Theatre today!

shows.name: The Producers
shows.description_short: This major revival of Mel Brooks' hilariously irreverent musical about a strategically planned Broadway flop transfers to the West End.
shows.description_full:
The Menier Chocolate Factory’s acclaimed production of The Producers transfers to the West End’s Garrick Theatre this year! Mel Brooks' irreverent, boundary-pushing musical about two men who try to commit fraud by mounting a guaranteed-to-fail Broadway show is “irresistible, absurd and joyful, both celebrating and sending up the power of theatre” (The Guardian). The Producers sold out its run off the West End so “don’t be stupid, be a smartie”  - check out our Review of The Producers , and book your tickets today!

Once the “King of Broadway”, producer Max Bialystock is down on his luck, a washed-up hack who now earns a living conning little old ladies into investing for shows that never happen. When nervous accountant Leo Bloom comes to audit Bialystock and discovers he’s been cooking the books, Bloom has the revelation that a show that flops could earn more than one that succeeds. So... the pair team up to produce the worst show on Broadway. They land upon the perfect script, Springtime for Hitler: A Gay Romp with Adolf and Eva at Berchtesgaden : a "love letter to Hitler" written by pigeon-obsessed ex-Nazi Franz Liebkin, and the perfect director - campy cross-dresser Roger de Bris and his partner and assistant Carmen Ghia, who live by the motto “Keep It Gay”.

Swedish bombshell Ulla rounds out their gang of misfits, joining as both chorus girl and office assistant, and Leo quickly falls for her. Once the funds are raised, Leo and Max are ready to make a show that’s sure to be a disaster - and when the show closes early, they’ll skip town with the money and head to Rio de Janeiro. But when “Opening Night” changes everything, Bialystock and Bloom must figure out what to do when the show is an unexpected smash hit.

Based on Mel Brooks' 1967 satirical cult classic film, which starred Zero Mostell and Gene Wilder, The Producers is a provocative and hilarious “equal-opportunity offender” (The Standard). The original Broadway production opened in 2001, winning a record-breaking 12 Tony Awards, and was later adapted into a movie musical starring Nathan Lane and Matthew Broderick, who originated the lead roles on stage. Featuring side-splitting songs including “Der Guten Tag Hop Clop”, “Keep it Gay”, “We Can Do It” and “Springtime for Hitler” - a song dubbed “a contender for the funniest showtune ever written” by TimeOut - The Producers remains as risqué and riotous as ever.

The Menier Chocolate Factory hosted the first major production of The Producers in London for almost 20 years and delighted audiences and critics alike, with its “vigour, sparkle and controlled wildness” (The Guardian). Don’t miss out on a gay old time when this “grimy, funny and well-judged” (TimeOut) production transfers to the West End - book today!

Main cast:

The Producers is based on the 1967 film of the same name, which was written and directed by Mel Brooks. He also adapted his own work for the stage, writing the music and lyrics and co-writing the book for the musical. Brooks is part of the exclusive EGOT club, holding Emmy, Grammy, Tony and Academy Awards. His numerous other accolades include winning a Kennedy Center Honor, a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, the AFI Life Achievement Award, a BFI Fellowship, a National Medal of Arts, a BAFTA Fellowship, and an Honorary Academy Award in 2024. Phew!

The book was written by Brooks and three-time Tony Award winner Thomas Stroman ( Annie, Hairspray, Young Frankenstein ), and the musical features original choreography by five-time Tony Award winner Susan Stroman ( Oklahoma!, Crazy for You, Smash ).

This production is directed by Olivier Award-winning comedian, playwright and director Patrick Marber, best known for his directing work on Leopoldstadt at Wyndham’s Theatre, The Old Neighbourhood at the Royal Court and Blue Remembered Hills at the National Theatre

You’ll want to be a producer this autumn! This “buoyantly vulgar” (Standard) musical is an uproarious delight, so grab a ticket for the best show in London about the worst show on Broadway. Book today for The Producers at the Garrick Theatre and get ready to do “Der Guten Tag Hop Clop” all the way home.

shows.name: Hadestown
shows.description_short: Tony Award winner Anaïs Mitchell's genre-defying take on Greek mythology takes audiences on an unforgettable trip to the Underworld.
shows.description_full:
Can a song change your fate? Climb aboard and journey on down to Hadestown to find out. Following critically acclaimed engagements at the National Theatre and in New York, Anaïs Mitchell’s Broadway phenomenon - and 8-time Tony Award winner - is back in London in 2024. Read our review of Hadestown and get ready to see “your next musical theatre obsession” (Vogue) at the Lyric Theatre on London's Shaftesbury Avenue.

Based on two classic Greek myths - young lovers Orpheus and Eurydice, and King Hades and his wife Persephone - Hadestown journeys to the underworld, as faith battles doubt, love fights fear, and industry struggles against nature. Orpheus and Eurydice are struggling through a barren winter, and when Eurydice is tempted by King Hades’ offer of a better life, Orpheus must travel down below to bring her home, with only his music to save him.

With a folk and jazz-inspired score of incredible songs including “Hey, Little Songbird”, “Wait For Me” and “Our Lady of the Underground”, this is a musical to raise a toast to and feel inspired by - and it is also hellraising fun!

Hadestown boasts an incredible creative team who have made it one of the biggest new musicals of the last decade. Singer-songwriter Anaïs Mitchell’s concept album was developed with Tony Award-winning director Rachel Chavkin ( Natasha, Pierre and the Great Comet of 1812 ) into a stunning musical that has had a journey as exciting as the one you will see on stage.

It was last in London at the National Theatre in 2018, and since 2019 has hooked audiences on Broadway. With numerous Olivier and Tony Awards to its name and a Grammy Award for Best Musical Theater Album, this entrancing folk opera is a one-of-a-kind musical that brings an ancient story to a whole new audience.

Main cast:

It might invite audiences on a hell-raising journey to the underworld, but the result is a theatrical slice of heaven that is “Sumptuous. Gorgeous. As good as it gets” (The New York Times). Book Hadestown tickets to experience the phenomenon yourself when it makes its long-awaited West End debut.

shows.name: Six the Musical
shows.description_short: Henry VIII’s embattled ex-wives reign over the West End with their fist-pumping histo-remix.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
Henry VIII’s ex-wives continue their reign over London theatre in a fist-pumping musical concert. Join Boleyn, Seymour and company for 90 minutes of uplifting pop hits at the Vaudeville Theatre minutes away from Covent Garden piazza.

Belting out hilarious, empowering songs inspired by modern pop royalty including “Ex Wives”, “Haus of Holbein'' and “Don’t Lose Ur Head”, the Tudor queens grab the mic and remix over five hundred years of historical heartbreak into 90 minutes of 21st-century girl power.

This comedy pop concert musical is inspired by the six wives of Henry VIII. Reimagining the Tudor queens as pop princesses, the wives compete to decide who suffered the most during their marriage, with the winner becoming the lead singer of their band. The musical features each queen performing a song about their life in different styles inspired by Beyoncé, Adele and other modern chart-topping artists.

This homegrown phenomenon has had audiences losing their heads since 2018 with the irresistible story of Henry’s sidelined wives taking centre stage. Six first burst onto the scene at Edinburgh Festival, created by Toby Marlow and Lucy Moss. Moss also directs the show alongside Jamie Armitage.

Marlow and Moss first created the show when they were students at the University of Cambridge, and the musical premiered at Edinburgh Fringe Festival before its West End premiere in 2019. It has enjoyed a swift ascent, with previous sell-out runs at the Arts Theatre and Lyric Theatre, and is now one of the biggest musicals in London with its own Queendom fandom. If you want a show that feels modern, fun, sassy and great for teenagers, this is the one to book tickets for!

Songs from the show include "Ex-Wives", "No Way", "Don't Lose Ur Head", "Heart of Stone", "Haus of Holbein", "Get Down", "All You Wanna Do", "I Don't Need Your Love" and "Six". At the end of the show, the cast perform "Megasix", which is a mashup of the queens' individual songs. "Megasix" does not feature on the official cast recording, and can only be seen live at the theatre.

Six the Musical songs are inspired by a range of modern recording artists. Catherine of Aragon's song "No Way" is based on Beyoncé, Anne Boleyn's "Don't Lose Ur Head" takes influence from Avril Lavigne and Lily Allen, and Katherine Howard's musical number "All You Wanna Do" is inspired by Ariana Grande and Britney Spears.

“Get Down” to the concert everyone’s talking about with Six the musical tickets. Securing their place as West End royalty, the Queens are divorced, beheaded, and live on stage!

Main cast:

shows.name: ABBA Voyage
shows.description_short: The Swedish pop group are projected into the ABBA Arena in a cutting-edge digital performance, promising a concert experience like no other.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 3s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult. Dance Floor best for age 12+.
shows.description_full:
Dance the night away to ABBA’s greatest hits in a concert like no other. Mixing the physical, the digital, and some of pop’s most irresistible hits, join Agnetha, Benny, Bjorn and Anni-Frid for the spectacular ABBA Voyage at ABBA Arena close to London's Olympic Park. If you're new to the show, our complete guide to everything you need to know is the perfect place to start.

Named after their first new album in 40 years, ABBA Voyage is an unforgettable live experience designed with all four members of ABBA, for their fans. Using cutting-edge motion capture technology, the pop quartet will appear as digital avatars on stage, accompanied by a 10-piece band made up of today’s best musicians.

The purpose-built ABBA Arena in East London was constructed especially for this never-before-seen concert and will immerse audiences in the magic of ABBA whether they’re on the Dance Floor or enjoying the music from their seats.

Fans of the chart-topping Swedish group can channel their inner dancing queens as they sing along to a setlist of ABBA’s most popular songs, taken from albums across the decades.

Get ready to dance and jive to “Dancing Queen”, sing along to the melody of “Thank You For The Music”, dance up a storm to “Waterloo” and get your friends together to enjoy the irresistible “Mamma Mia”!

Other songs included in ABBA Voyage include “The Visitors”, “SOS”, “Knowing Me, Knowing You”, “Chiquitita”, “Fernando”, “Does Your Mother Know”, Lay All Your Love On Me”, “The Winner Takes It All”, and “Voulez-Vous”. Find out more with our ultimate guide to the ABBA Voyage setlist .

While ABBA themselves do not appear in ABBA Voyage , the ABBAtars have been modelled from them using motion capture technology that is so cutting-edge, you will hardly believe that you aren’t seeing the real deal on stage!

To achieve the uncanny effect, the group performed wearing motion capture suits for five weeks, and 160 cameras scanned every inch of their body movements and facial expressions to create the ultimate likeness of themselves from the 1970s.

For a deep dive into the groundbreaking technology, read our full guide on how ABBA Voyage works .

Whether you choose to sit, stand or party in a Dance Booth, you will have the time of your life at ABBA Voyage . Get ready to enjoy one of the world’s biggest pop acts as you’ve never seen them before.
shows.running_time_text: ABBA Voyage running time is 1 hour and 40 minutes.

faq.answer: ABBA Voyage tickets are priced from £48 to £221. Fridays and Saturdays are the most popular days so tend to be more expensive.Top Tip: Sundays are an excellent choice for tickets. Both performances on Sundays tend to be the same price as weekday performances. A Sunday 1pm performance is the perfect way to cap off your weekend and still have time to rest your dancing feet afterwards!

Other expensive tickets are in Block H, located behind Block C, Blocks B and D, and the front rows of Blocks G and J.These areas are the most central in the entire arena, and from seats such as Block C C3 you can get a fantastic full view of the stage, screens, lighting and visual effects - and most importantly, the jaw-dropping ABBAtars.One fan sitting in Block H B17 even said they were in the “best seat in the house”.Dance Booth tickets are also some of the most expensive in ABBA Arena and are perfect for private groups celebrating a special occasion.

faq.question: What is the age recommendation for ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: ABBA Voyage is best for age 6+. Under 3s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult. Dance Floor best for age 12+.

faq.question: How long is ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: The running time for ABBA Voyage at ABBA Arena is 1 hour and 40 minutes.

faq.question: How do I collect my tickets for ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: Tickets for ABBA Voyage are e-tickets. These will be sent to you before the performance. If you have not received your e-tickets, please contact your point of purchase. They will re-send these to you before your performance date.

faq.question: What are ABBA Voyage Dance Booths and how do I book them?
faq.answer: The Dance Booths at ABBA Voyage London are a fantastic ‘best-of-both-worlds' experience that provides seating as well as a dance floor. ABBA Arena features eight Dance Booths; four for up to 10 people and four for up to 12 people. Find out more with our guide to the ABBA Voyage Dance Booth Experience.The Dance Booths are very popular and can sell out many months in advance so be prepared to search a few months ahead.

faq.question: Can I buy tickets for ABBA Voyage at the box office?
faq.answer: Yes you can buy from the ABBA Arena box office where you will pay the face value ticket price, sometimes with a small booking fee. Search on SeatPlan to get the best prices and deals in one search including discounts not available at the box office.

faq.question: Is there an interval during ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: No, ABBA Voyage doesn't have an interval.

faq.question: Is it best to sit or stand at ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: Like any concert, ABBA Voyage can be enjoyed seated in traditional auditorium seating, or on your feet, grooving away on the dance floor in front of the stage. The best option for you will depend on personal preference, but for a real party atmosphere, we highly recommend the Dance Floor.The general admission Dance Floor is a fantastic and cost-effective way to enjoy getting your boogie on with a group of friends, and you can find out exactly what it's like in our review of the Dance Floor experience. It does not have the privacy or seating of the far pricier Dance Booths, but puts you closer to the stage where the incredible visual effects will have you believing that you are actually near the front row of a real ABBA Concert.

faq.question: What is the refund / cancellation policy?
faq.answer: Tickets for ABBA Voyage are generally non-refundable and non-exchangeable.SeatPlan works with multiple suppliers to find the best deal for you. Each supplier has their own refund and cancellation policy, which you can review on the payment page before you complete your purchase.

faq.question: What are the best ABBA Voyage tickets for children?
faq.answer: ABBA Voyage is recommended for ages 6+, and children under 3 will not be admitted. There are also some restrictions for children attending depending on where you choose to stand or sit, and anyone under the age of 16 must be accompanied by, and next to, an adult.The Dance Floor is recommended for ages 12+ because it can get crowded; this is both for children’s comfort and safety and to ensure they have a great view of the stage.The seated areas are best for children of all ages. Central seating sections have a more head-on view, but rows across the auditorium are tiered so there is a clear view of the stage no matter where you sit.

faq.question: Are accessible seats available?
faq.answer: ABBA Arena has dedicated Accessible sections an assortment of wheelchair spaces and ambulatory seating. Companion seats for all accessible seats can be booked free of charge. For more information, see our ABBA Arena Accessibility page.

faq.question: My group’s tickets have different entrances. What does this mean?
faq.answer: ABBA Voyage tickets recommend which door you should enter the auditorium from, for example Entry B Arena Left or Entry D Arena Right. This is to make sure everyone gets inside easily and queues aren’t too long.If you’re with a group and you have different entrances on your tickets, don’t worry! You can meet up with them once you’re inside.

faq.question: Can I take drinks to my seat at ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: Drinks at ABBA Voyage that have been purchased on site can usually be taken to your seat or to the standing area. They will be decanted into plastic containers, but please always be aware of other audience members and follow any advice from staff at the venue.

faq.question: Can I dance in my seat?
faq.answer: Yes, dancing is usually allowed during ABBA Voyage from seated areas and the audience often gets on their feet for some songs! However, please be mindful of the people around and behind you and don’t block the aisles.

faq.question: Can I sit down on the Dance Floor?
faq.answer: You might be able to sit down at the back of the Dance Floor, but please don’t sit down in the middle. This is a safety hazard because of the crowd around you. It's best to check with staff, who are available to help just outside the auditorium throughout the show.

faq.question: Can I move between the standing and seated areas?
faq.answer: No, tickets are for one area only and you won’t be allowed to move from your seats to the standing area or vice versa. This is for safety reasons to prevent overcrowding. If you want to exchange your ticket for a different area, please contact your ticket provider.

faq.question: Is ABBA Voyage general admission?
faq.answer: The seating areas aren't general admission. Tickets are all assigned a section and seat number which you choose when you book. You will need to remain in that seat for the duration of the performance.The standing area is general admission. You can stand wherever you want and can move around during the show. If you want to grab the perfect spot, we recommend getting to the venue a little early. Lots of people have reported that the middle to back of the dancefloor is best for enjoying the sky high projections which really add to the experience. Some fans take their places an hour before the start time but thirty minutes before should secure you a good view.

faq.question: Can I leave the auditorium during the performance?
faq.answer: Yes, you can. ABBA Voyage doesn’t have an interval but the doors are open. You can leave during the show to use the toilets, buy another drink or just take a break. Staff are available to help if you need to sit down or have any questions.

faq.question: Are latecomers allowed at ABBA Voyage?
faq.answer: Latecomers will not be admitted until a suitable point in the concert. ABBA Voyage recommends that you aim to arrive an hour in advance of the advertised start time to allow for ticket and bag checks on entry to the venue and to allow for any travel delays en route.

shows.name: Witness for the Prosecution
shows.description_short: The landmark production of Agatha Christie's courtroom thriller is set inside the magnificent council chamber of London County Hall.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Babes in arms not admitted
shows.description_full:
Would you be able to tell if a man is innocent or guilty? Experience Agatha Christie's gripping courtroom drama Witness for the Prosecution in a unique, landmark production set against the backdrop of London County Hall in Westminster. This venue’s historic grandeur immerses audiences in the story of accused murderer Leonard Vole, and by the end you will feel as if you are part of the jury, deciding his fate.

Leonard Vole has been accused of murdering a widow in order to inherit her wealth. As he takes to the stand to protest his innocence and convince the jury that he did not commit this crime, expect shocking twists, powerful testimonies and tales of passion and betrayal.

First published by Agatha Christie in 1925 as the short story, 'Traitor's Hands', the Witness for the Prosecution play debuted in 1953. The current London County Hall production, directed by Lucy Bailey, opened in 2017 and drew critical acclaim for its unique and imaginative staging in the Hall's former Council Chamber.

It is the second of Agatha Christie’s plays to be whipping up a storm in London; her mind-bending whodunit The Mousetrap has been a West End mainstay for more than 70 years, and the ending has still remained a secret…

Witness for the Prosecution is the perfect show for anyone who wants to enjoy top London theatre in a completely unique environment, and get their brain working at the same time! Since opening it has received rave reviews praising its “imaginative” (The Guardian) staging, and calling it a “deliciously enjoyable” (Time Out) show that is “guilty of being a good night out” (The Arts Desk).

With all the twists, turns and revelations we have come to expect from Queen of Crime Agatha Christie, audiences have been thrilled by this tricky and entertaining show that delights in making you second-guess up until the final moments.

Main cast:

In this tense production, the audience and cast are almost entwined thanks to County Hall’s site-specific features. Theatregoers sitting in the Jury Box have a unique view down to the Witness Box and barristers as they fight their corner and persuasively put forward their version of events, while other company members walk up and down the aisles as if entering and exiting a real courtroom.

With seats available in the heart of the courtroom as well as on the jury bench itself, Witness for the Prosecution tickets will fully immerse you in the action and place Leonard's fate firmly in your hands.

shows.name: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
shows.description_short: This uplifting, moving musical tells the remarkable true story of an innovative boy who dreams of saving his Malawain village from drought.
shows.work_performed_name: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
shows.description_full:
Fresh from a world premiere in Stratford-upon-Avon, The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind makes its West End debut at Soho Place, in the heart of Soho, for a strictly limited run this spring. This bold moving and uplifting musical, about a young Malawian boy who fights to save his village from a devastating drought is based on the incredible true story, and the film adaptation by Olivier Award winner Chiwetel Ejiofor. Joyous, moving and telling a vitally important story, The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind promises to be unmissable theatre. Secure your tickets today!

In the midst of a scorching summer in Malawi and a devastating drought, 13-year-old William has a dream - he wants to save his village. As the drought worsens day by day, causing crops to fail and hope to run dry, nobody in William’s community believes he can find the solution to their problems, with even his family and friends doubting him. Armed only with library books, some scrap machinery and his own imagination and grit, William is determined to prove them all wrong and bring the village some relief. As William begins to build a windmill, challenges arise - can he harness both the power of his creativity and the wind to bring hope back to his people?

The musical is based on the 2019 film, which was written, directed and starred in by Chiwetel Ejiofor, and was based on the memoir of the same name by William Kamkwamba and Bryan Mealer. The film premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in January 2019, and later received a wide release on Netflix. The musical makes its world premiere at the Swan Theatre in Stratford-upon-Avon in February 2026 before transferring to London for a strictly limited run in April.

Adapted from the book by William Kamkwamba and Bryan Mealey, as well as the film by Chiwetel Ejiofor, who serves as executive producer, The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind has a book and lyrics by Richy Hughes ( Superhero, Mr Popper's Penguins ) and music and lyrics by Tim Sutton ( Restless Natives, The Merry Wives of Windsor ).

The production is directed by former Bush Theatre Artistic Director and BAFTA nominee Lynette Linton ( Shifters, Sweat, Intimate Apparel, Blues for an Alabama Sky ).

This joyous, deeply moving new musical is a celebration of ingenuity and the courage to dream, even whilst facing impossible odds. With a West End transfer confirmed before the world premiere has even opened in Stratford, we know this is going to be a deeply special show. Book your tickets for The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind at Soho Place today and allow yourself to be blown away by this remarkable story.

shows.name: To Kill A Mockingbird
shows.description_short: Academy Award winner Aaron Sorkin’s Tony-winning adaptation of Harper Lee’s novel about racial tensions in rural Alabama returns.
shows.description_full:
This landmark revival of To Kill A Mockingbird comes to the Wyndham’s Theatre, just off Leicester Square, following a huge UK and Ireland tour. Based on Harper Lee’s iconic Pulitzer Prize-winning novel about racial tensions in Southern America, Oscar-winner Aaron Sorkin’s critically acclaimed stage adaptation has taken Broadway and the West End by storm and now returns to London for a strictly limited run. This “powerful. Important. Deeply moving” (Sunday Express) play is unmissable - secure your tickets today.

Alabama, 1934, and successful lawyer Atticus Finch is a pillar of his community, determined to encourage empathy and kindness in his children, Scout and Jem. But when Finch finds himself at the centre of a shocking court case, in which a Black man may have been falsely accused, he is pushed to the very limits of his own capacity for understanding. Can Finch uncover the truth, when the people of the town seem determined to obscure it?

To Kill a Mockingbird is based on the novel by Harper Lee, which was inspired by experiences from the author’s own childhood. The book was first published in 1960, to immediate acclaim, winning the Pulitzer Prize a year later. Widely considered to be one of the Greatest American Novels, the book is not without controversy, with the novel topping lists of banned books due to its themes of racial injustice and the use of racial slurs within the text.

Sorkin’s adaptation was written in 2018, following a previous stage adaptation by Christopher Sergel written in 1990, and reframes the story to make Finch the central character - as both the previous production and the novel are narrated from Scout, Finch’s six-year-old daughter’s point of view. Sorkin’s adaptation debuted on Broadway in 2018, earning nine Tony nominations and winning one, and was followed by a West End production in 2022, which was nominated for five Olivier awards. The play embarks on a huge UK and Ireland tour in 2026, before returning to the West End for a strictly limited run.

Richard Coyle will be reprising his role as Atticus Finch from the UK tour.

Further casting for the West End production of To Kill A Mockingbird is to be announced.

To Kill a Mockingbird is adapted from Harper Lee’s novel by Aaron Sorkin. Sorkin is an American writer, best known for creating and writing TV shows The West Wing and The Newsroom, and for films The Social Network and A Few Good Men . Sorkin is an Academy Award, BAFTA and Golden Globe winner.

Bartlett Sher directs. Sher is a Tony Award-winning American director and the former director of New York’s Lincoln Centre Theater. His credits include My Fair Lady, Oslo, The Light in the Piazza, The King and I , and South Pacific .

This award-winning revival of one of the greatest American stories of all time is “radical and pulsing with relevance” (Chicago Tribune), adapted by Academy Award winner Aaron Sorkin. “ Mockingbird soars anew in Sorkin’s blistering adaptation” (Daily Mail) - you won’t want to miss it. Secure your tickets for To Kill A Mockingbird at Wyndham’s Theatre today.

shows.name: Now You See Me Live
shows.description_short: The highly anticipated UK premiere of the stage illusion show, Now You See Me, plays at the London Coliseum for a limited summer run.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Under 5s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Experience an explosive mix of high-tech illusions, solo stunts, and jaw-dropping spectacles as Now You See Me Live arrives at the London Coliseum, the largest theatre in London’s West End. Located on St Martin's Lane in the heart of theatreland, Now You See Me is inspired by the blockbuster franchise and delivers a “high-energy blend of wonder and humour” (Broadway World) this summer.

Now You See Me is presented by a world-class music ensemble inspired by the film’s characters, the ‘Four Horsemen’. During the cutting-edge production, the performers will stun audiences with their unbelievable skills but also with the addition of some shocking twists and grand feats of illusion.

The film franchise which this show is influenced by was first released in 2013. Although it was met with mixed reviews initially, it became a box office success and earned the People’s Choice Award for Favorite Thriller Movie. A sequel was released in 2016, followed by a third installment, Now You See Me: Now You Don’t which came out in November 2025.

The Now You See Me live production has been hugely successful, having performed thousands of shows across the world. They have sold out shows in Australia, Asia, Las Vegas and Broadway. Now it heads to the UK for a limited five-week summer season at the London Coliseum.

Now You See Me is brought to the stage by the team behind Broadway’s record-breaking hit The Illusionists . Simon Painter and Adam Trent are co-creators, whilst Painter is joined by Tim Lawson as Executive Producer. Ash Jacks McCready is the creative and staging director, Kirsty White is the creative director, and Enzo Wenye is the illusion director.

The show is co-produced by Runaway Entertainment, BOS Productions and Timothy Laczynski.

If you’re looking for a production to blow your mind this summer, the UK premiere of Now You See Me Live at the London Coliseum is must-see. See the boundaries of state magic get pushed to the absolute edge and be left in awe by all that this show has to offer.

shows.name: The Truth
shows.description_short: Ardal O’Hanlon and Stephen Mangan lead this revival of Florian Zeller’s sharp relationship comedy about truth, lies and infidelity.
shows.description_full:
Oscar Winner Florian Zeller’s acclaimed play The Truth arrives at the Apollo Theatre, in the heart of Leicester Square, for a strictly limited season. This major revival of the razor-sharp comedy explores love, lies and self-deception, and comes to London for a strictly limited run, starring Tony nominee Stephen Mangan, comedy icon Ardal O’Hanlon, Olivier winner Janie Dee and Miranda star Sarah Hadland. Are you ready to learn the truth? Secure your tickets to this “ingenious puzzle of a play” (London Theatre) today.

Michel is having an affair. Or at least, that’s the truth, as far as he’s concerned. Married to Laurence, he is also involved with Alice, the wife of his best friend, Paul. When Alice suggests confessing to spare their spouses further deception, Michel resists, convinced that honesty would cause more harm than good.

As the couples’ relationships intertwine, truths are revealed, concealed and reshaped. Each character has their own version of events, and what seems certain in one moment is cleverly overturned in the next. With swift dialogue and escalating misunderstandings, The Truth examines how lies can protect, destroy or even sustain relationships, asking whether complete honesty is always the best policy.

Originally titled La Vérité , the play premiered in Paris in 2011 and quickly became a hit, cementing Zeller’s reputation as one of Europe’s most exciting contemporary playwrights. Zeller's acclaim grew further with his play The Father , which won the 2014 Molière Award for Best Play and was later adapted into an Academy Award-winning film starring Anthony Hopkins, and he  is now one of the most internationally performed French playwrights of his generation. The Truth debuted in London in 2016, and now returns in the first major UK revival of the production in over ten years.

The star-studded, award-winning cast of The Truth includes:

Roles are to be confirmed.

The Truth is written by Academy Award-winning French playwright Florian Zeller ( The Father, The Son, The Mother ). The English-language version is translated by Tony winner and Academy Award nominee Christopher Hampton ( Sunset Boulevard, Les Liaisons Dangereuses , Atonement ).

Lindsay Posner directs. Posner is an Olivier-winning British theatre director. His credits include Noises Off for the Old Vic, Death and the Maiden at the Royal Court, The Rivals starring David Tennant for the RSC, Sexual Perversity in Chicago starring Matthew Perry at the Harold Pinter Theatre, and A View from the Bridge for Duke of York’s Theatre.

Directed by Olivier winner Lindsey Posner, with a script by Oscar winner Florian Zeller, translated by Tony Winner Christopher Hampton, and a star-studded cast including comedy icons Stephen Mangan and Ardal O’Hanlon, The Truth is truly unmissable theatre. As secrets unravel and loyalties are tested, this sharply witty play invites audiences to question just how honest we really want our relationships to be - and whether there ever is one objective truth. Book your tickets for The Truth at the Apollo Theatre today.

shows.description_short: This bold new play transfers to the West End. Three women. One summer in 1536. A field in Essex. A king’s betrayal ripples through time.
shows.description_full:
Book tickets for 1536 at the Ambassadors Theatre, in the heart of Leicester Square, and experience a gripping new play that places female voices at the heart of Tudor history. Following a celebrated, sell-out premiere at the Almeida Theatre, Ava Pickett’s “wonderfully vibrant, cracking debut” (The Guardian) transfers to the West End for a strictly limited season - read our review of 1536 and secure your tickets today.

Set in a marshy field in Essex, far from the grandeur of royal courts, 1536 follows three women grappling with friendship, survival, and the rippling impact of Anne Boleyn’s downfall. This bold and darkly comic play offers a modern lens on a pivotal moment in history.

Though the play is set in 1536 - the year Anne Boleyn was executed - it doesn’t focus on the Tudor court. Instead, it centres on three ordinary women, exploring how seismic events at the top of society impact those at the bottom. The characters speak in a contemporary, colloquial style, bridging past and present through a darkly hilarious, anachronistic script.

Ava Pickett won the prestigious Susan Smith Blackburn Prize in 2024 for 1536 , an honour awarded to outstanding English-language plays written by women. The play was then developed through the Genesis Almeida New Playwrights, Big Plays Programme and premiered at the Almeida Theatre in 2025 to critical acclaim.

Casting for this production includes:

1536 is written by Ava Pickett, The play marks her professional debut and firmly establishes her as one of the most exciting new voices in British theatre.

Lyndsey Turner directs. Turner is a National Theatre associate. Her previous credits include the multi-award-winning Chimerica for the Almeida, Under Milk Wood for the National Theatre, Girls & Boys , and Posh for the Royal Court.

1536 is an urgent and unflinching portrait of power, patriarchy and protest. This “funny, saucy, and totally gripping” (Daily Mail) production blends humour, heartbreak and political bite. Whether you're a history lover, a fan of fearless new writing, or simply looking for something refreshingly original in the West End, 1536 is a must-see. Book your tickets for 1536 at the Ambassadors Theatre today.

shows.name: Beetlejuice
shows.description_short: The smash-hit Broadway comedy, based on Tim Burton’s cult classic film, finally makes its London debut.
shows.description_full:
Beetlejuice... Beetlejuice… Beetlejuice ! The new musical comedy based on Tim Burton’s iconic film is set to give London the fright of its life at last! The Tony-nominated smash hit has long been rumoured to come and haunt the West End, and now comes to the Prince Edward Theatre, just off Old Compton Street, from Spring 2026. Beetlejuice the Musical is “screamingly good fun!” (Variety) - secure your tickets today!

Beetlejuice the Musical tells the story of Lydia Deetz, a strange and unusual teenager whose whole life changes when she meets mild-mannered, recently deceased couple, the Maitlands, and a wildcard demon with a thing for stripes. Luckily, Lydia is totally obsessed with “the whole being dead thing” and befriends Beetlejuice, the ghost-with-the-most from six feet under. Together, Lydia and Beetlejuice hatch a plan to scare Lydia’s absent dad into paying attention to her, but when the Juice is loose, surely only chaos can ensue…

Tim Burton’s much-loved 1988 film was a huge critical and commercial success, starring Michael Keaton as Beetlejuice, as well as Alec Baldwin, Geena Davis as Adam and Barbara Maitland and Winona Ryder as Lydia Deetz. A firm Halloween classic, the film is one of Burton’s most successful, alongside Edward Scissorhands and The Nightmare Before Christmas . Beetlejuice the Musical opened on Broadway in 2019, earning eight Tony nominations, before heading out on a US tour in late 2020. The show has since returned to Broadway briefly in 2023, and comes to Broadway again, again, again this spooky season for a limited run. Beetlejuice has been highly anticipated for a London transfer - so make sure to “jump in the line” and snap up your tickets today!

Casting for Beetlejuice's London debut has started creeping out. With David Fynn ( School of Rock ) taking on the madness of the iconic titular role, Tom Xander ( Mean Girls) will play as alternate. Making her West End debut is Euphoria's Hanna Nordberg as the strange and unusual Lydia Deetz.

Further casting includes:

Beetlejuice the Musical features a frighteningly catchy score by Eddie Perfect ( Australia’s Got Talent, King Kong the Musical ), and a book by Scott Brown ( Sharp Objects, Manhattan ) and Anthony King ( Broad City, Wet Hot American Summer: First Day of Camp ). As well as all new songs, like “The Whole ‘Being Dead Thing’” and viral sensation “Say My Name”, the musical also includes two of the film’s iconic musical numbers – “Day-O (The Banana Boat Song)” and “Jump in the Line (Shake, Senora)”.

Buckle up, London, it’s showtime! Beetlejuice the Musical promises a must-see spectacular, with a story that is heart-warming, hideous and hilarious. Beetlejuice the Musical has been rumoured for a West End transfer since it premiered on Broadway in 2019 so emo kids and musical theatre fans alike can rejoice as it comes to London at last! Missing out might give you the “Fright of Your Life” so book your tickets for Beetlejuice the Musical today!

shows.name: A Midsummer Night's Dream
shows.description_short: Love’s logic unravels, identities shift, and magic reigns in this sunlit summer dream, as Shakespeare’s most beloved comedy returns.
shows.typical_age_range: Suitable for all ages. All under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
A Midsummer Night’s Dream returns to the Globe Theatre, in the heart of London’s Bankside. Step into a world where lovers chase desires through a moonlit forest and playful spirits stir magical mischief under open skies. The play’s rich tapestry of love stories, supernatural mischief and meta‑theatrical humour makes Dream one of Shakespeare’s most widely performed works, and this new staging embraces the theatre’s iconic open‑air design and communal spirit, inviting audiences into a shared experience of mischief and wonder. Secure your tickets today!

Amid moonlit woods just beyond the city, love’s logic unravels, identities shift, and magic reigns. With a sprinkling of fairy dust, a wild web of stories is woven together: the impending wedding of Theseus and Hippolyta, the romantic entanglements of four young Athenians, a band of amateur players rehearsing a rustic play, and the capricious antics of a fairy host led by Oberon and his sprite, Puck.

In this forest, desire is both unpredictable and irresistible. The edge between dream and waking life blurs, and laughter springs from both chaos and connection. So let yourself be spellbound, and who knows who you’ll be by morning…

A Midsummer Night’s Dream is one of William Shakespeare’s most celebrated and enduring comedies, believed to have been written between 1595 and 1596. Scholars suggest it may have been composed for a noble wedding celebration, possibly that of Elizabeth Carey, a member of Queen Elizabeth I’s court. Its intricate structure, poetic language, and fantastical themes mark it as one of Shakespeare’s most imaginative works.

A Midsummer Night’s Dream was first published in quarto in 1600, with a more reliable version included in the 1623 First Folio. It became a popular choice for performances during Shakespeare’s lifetime and has remained a staple of theatrical repertoire ever since. The play’s popularity surged in the 19th century with spectacular Victorian productions that emphasised its fairy-tale elements.

Over the centuries, it has inspired numerous adaptations in opera, ballet, film, and contemporary theatre. From Benjamin Britten’s 1960 opera to the modern screen adaptations by directors like Michael Hoffman in 1999 and Julie Taymor in 2014, A Midsummer Night’s Dream continues to captivate audiences with its dreamlike blend of romance, comedy, and magic.

Casting for this production includes:

A Midsummer Night’s Dream is written by William Shakespeare. Emily Lim ( The Odyssey, Brighter Still ) directs, with set design by Aldo Vázquez ( Romeo and Juliet ), and costume design by Fly Davis ( Othello ). Music and composition are by Jim Fortune, whose credits include Pericles and A Christmas Carol .

Step into the forest and experience a sunlit summer dream where love’s enchantments dance beneath open skies, and laughter mingles with poetry. Illusion, desire and transformation meet in Shakespeare’s best-known comedy returning to the Globe this summer season in an exciting new production that emphasises the community spirit of the play. So don’t be a fool, mortal - secure your tickets for A Midsummer Night’s Dream at Shakespeare’s Globe today.

shows.name: The Play That Goes Wrong
shows.description_short: Mischief Theatre’s hilarious comedy of errors will leave you in stitches, as an am-dram group's latest murder mystery goes awry.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 8+. Children under 5 not permitted.
shows.description_full:
What happens when you combine a murder mystery and a comic farce? Mischief Theatre’s catastrophic, but brilliant, The Play That Goes Wrong ! The West End’s longest-running comedy is Fawlty Towers meets Noises Off, as a fictional am-dram society desperately try to stage a brand new murder mystery without any mishaps.

The winner of the 2015 Olivier Award for Best New Comedy is still the top pick for rib-tickling comedy years later, laughing up a storm in Covent Garden at the Duchess Theatre.

The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society is staging its latest production, The Murder at Haversham Manor . But in the hands of these accident-prone actors, everything that can go wrong does! The cast battles through faulty sets, fluffed lines and more in a desperate bid to bring the play to its thrilling - and hilarious - conclusion.

This must-see hit show is an absolute riot from start to finish, with plenty of surprises, a little bit of audience interaction, and hilariously clever send-ups of the Agatha Christie-style whodunnits. This acting troupe take “break a leg” a bit too literally, and the results are side-splitting as audiences get to see the backstage team frantically fixing set pieces as the actors valiantly battle their way through, come hell or high water.

Main cast:

This rib-tickling masterpiece is the first show from Mischief Theatre, who have taken theatreland by storm with several award-winning comedies. Other hit shows from the team include The Comedy About a Bank Robbery and Magic Goes Wrong .

Mischief Theatre burst onto the scene with The Play That Goes Wrong in 2012, and it has been running at the Duchess Theatre since 2014; the longest-running play the venue has seen since it opened nearly 100 years ago. An instant hit with theatregoers and critics, the play has embarked on UK tours, transferred to New York, and won several awards including the Olivier Award for Best New Comedy in 2015.

Everything goes wrong for the unfortunate characters, but something has gone very right for the show’s creators in the real world; they’ve gone on to enjoy enormous acclaim, and have applied their winning blend of comic capers, slapstick and perfectly-pitched chaos to Peter Pan , Penn and Teller and even a BBC TV series, The Goes Wrong Show .

This laugh-out-loud comedy has been in the West End for almost ten years, and was an immediate hit with critics and audiences when it premiered in 2014!

The Telegraph called it “delightful”, while The Stage gave it four stars and raved about its “outrageous, sometimes courageous inventiveness” and “brilliantly sustained” joke from start to end. Mark Shenton even wrote in London Theatre that Mischief Theatre “may have a substantial little hit on their hands” - and he certainly wasn’t wrong, because this show is still one of the most enjoyable and popular in London!

Agatha Christie meets local am-dram in the most hilarious way! Guaranteed to leave you giggling all the way home, book tickets to find out why theatregoers keep coming back for more laughs, more chaos and more pitch-perfect escapist comedy.

shows.name: Sherlock Holmes
shows.description_short: This brand new play features the world's most famous Victorian detective and his faithful companion in a thrilling original mystery.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 12+. Children under 4 will not be admited. All under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
Begin the summer season sleuthfully, with the world premiere of Sherlock Holmes at Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, in the heart of Regent’s Park. This play presents a brand new mystery starring the legendary detective and his companion, Dr Watson, who are drawn into a conspiracy that spans the city by an unknown woman and a mysterious jewel. Sherlock Holmes is an immersive theatrical journey into suspense and deduction beneath the open sky. Check out our review of Sherlock Holmes and book your tickets today!

London, 1890; Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson are fresh from the success of Holmes’s first major case. The pair find themselves waiting for the next big case when a mysterious woman and a precious jewel arrive at 221B Baker Street, pulling them into a fresh and dangerous investigation. What follows is a pursuit across the bustling streets of Victorian London, from the foggy inner streets to the banks of the River Thames, all the way to Regent’s Park, testing the duo’s deductive skills as they confront a potentially deadly conspiracy.

This brand new original mystery is inspired by the stories of Sir Arthur Conan Doyle and presented in agreement with the Conan Doyle Estate

Confirmed casting includes:

Sherlock Holmes is written by Joel Horwood ( The Ocean at the End of the Lane, I Want my Hat Back ), based on the novels by Arthur Conan Doyle. Sean Holmes ( Pinocchi, The Merry Wives of Windsor ) directs.

Lisa Aitken ( Brigadoon, The Boy that Harnessed the Wind ) serves as co-costume designer and supervisor, with co-costume design and set design by Grace Smart ( Guess How Much I Love You, Cyrano de Bergerac ), movement direction by Charlotte Broom ( The Seagull, The Hunger Games: On Stage ), and lighting design by Ryan Day ( Hamlet, Never Have I Ever ).

The show has an original score by Jherek Bischoff ( The Ocean at the End of the Lane, Eureka Day ). Sound design is by Elena Peña ( Bengal Tiger at the Baghdad Zoo, Sweat ) and wigs, hair and make-up design by Carole Hancock ( Shucked, Brigadoon ), who also serves as supervisor in these areas.

Should you book tickets to see Sherlock Holmes this summer? The answer is elementary! Sherlock Holmes offers a fresh mystery crafted for the stage, combining a celebrated literary figure with a brand new narrative adventure. Solve the puzzle alongside Holmes and Watson in the heart of a Victorian‑inspired London - book your tickets for Sherlock Holmes at the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre today!

shows.name: Arcadia
shows.description_short: A landmark in-the-round revival of Tom Stoppard's Olivier-winning play about time, science and desire heads to the West End.
shows.description_full:
Immerse yourself in the extraordinary, where brilliant ideas and burning hearts fuse, in Arcadia , coming to the West End this summer. This revival of Tom Stoppard’s seminal play takes place at the Duke of York's theatre, just a short walk from the Trafalgar Theatre. Directed by acclaimed British director Carrie Cracknell, Arcadia “pits the classical against the romantic, science against poetry, the past against the present” (Guardian). You won't want to miss this breathtaking new staging for a limited time; secure your tickets today.

Set in a country house in Derbyshire, Arcadia follows two intertwined storylines across time. In the early 19th century, a teenage prodigy named Thomasina Coverly discovers mathematical truths about chaos and thermodynamics, while the adults around her - tutors, guests, and the household - attempt to discipline her passion with reason. Meanwhile, in the 1990s, scholars descend on the same estate, chasing the ghosts of that earlier era, seeking proof, revelation - and perhaps each other.

Pulsing with themes of order and disorder, the irreversible flow of time, the intersection of intellect and emotion, poetry and physics, Arcadia is brilliant, funny and deeply moving. Making its West End premiere after its initial staging at The Old Vic, the in‑the‑round staging of this revival heightens the play’s sense of immersion and immediacy.

Arcadia premiered at the National Theatre Lyttelton stage in London on 13 April 1993, directed by Trevor Nunn. The play received widespread critical acclaim upon its premiere and went on to win the Laurence Olivier Award for Best New Play. Arcadia later received its first Broadway transfer at the Lincoln Centre Theatre in New York in 1995, and has been revived several times, including a major 2009 production at the Duke of York’s Theatre in London and a 2011 Broadway revival. This new revival at the Duke of York’s Theatre is a result of a triumphant debut at The Old Vic at the start of 2026.

Arcadia is widely regarded as one of the most significant plays of the 20th Century, and in 2006, the Royal Institution recognised the play as one of the greatest science-related literary works ever written. This honour reflects the play’s extraordinary ability to weave complex scientific concepts - such as thermodynamics, chaos theory and fractals - into a compelling, emotionally resonant narrative.

“A truly marvellous play, full of wit, wisdom and a rare warmth” (WhatsOnStage), “A stellar, lust-filled revival” (The Independent): critics were transformed by Arcadia at The Old Vic. Now that the West End transfer has been announced, more people will be able to witness a pulsating relationship between past and present unfold on the big stage this summer.

Casting for this production is to be announced.

Carrie Cracknell directs the production. Cracknell became the youngest artistic director of a professional UK theatre when she co-led the Gate Theatre from 2007 to 2012. Her acclaimed work includes A Doll’s House at the Young Vic and West End, Medea and Julie both at the National Theatre, and the Netflix film Persuasion .

Arcadia is written by Tom Stoppard. Tom Stoppard is a Czech-born British playwright and screenwriter, widely regarded as one of the most influential dramatists of his generation. His major stage credits include Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead , The Real Thing , The Invention of Love , Rock ’n’ Roll and Leopoldstadt . Stoppard has received numerous prestigious awards, including four Tony Awards for Best Play and an Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay for Shakespeare in Love . He was knighted in 1997 for his services to drama.

This landmark revival of acclaimed playwright Tom Stoppard’s most critically acclaimed work comes to London for a strictly limited run, directed by one of the UK's hottest young directors. Arcadia is where minds meet, loves blaze, and time unwinds and a “dazzling meditation on our endless search for understanding” (What’s on Stage). This production promises to be unmissable – book your ticket for Arcadia at the Duke of York’s Theatre today.

shows.name: Come Alive! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular
shows.description_short: Step under the big top! Songs from The Greatest Showman meet breathtaking circus acts in this immersive family spectacle. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 5s, inc. babes in arms, not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.work_performed_name: Come Alive! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular
shows.description_full:
This is the greatest show! Step inside the Empress Museum in Earl's Court, where the Big Top and immersive foyer performances transport you into the spectacle from the moment you arrive. Based on the blockbuster 2017 film, Come Alive! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular invites you into a vibrant world of music, storytelling, and jaw‑dropping circus artistry. You won’t want to miss this breathtaking experience, suitable for the whole family. Secure your tickets for this immersive circus show full of “incredible energy” (Daily Express) and “impressive acrobatics” (TimeOut) today!

A vibrant reinvention of the world of The Greatest Showman , but not a direct stage adaptation, Come Alive! tells a contemporary, original story inspired by the beloved film’s themes and soundtrack as plucky newcomer Max must learn to step into the spotlight and out of the shadows, with a little help from the circus’s owner, The Showman.

This “eye-popping theatrical spectacle” (WestEnd Theatre) features new characters, fresh narration, and all of Benj Pasek and Justin Paul’s hit songs from the film—including “This Is Me”, “Rewrite the Stars”, “A Million Dreams”, “Tightrope” and “The Greatest Show”. As well as enjoying the spectacular vocals, acrobatic feats and moving storytelling, audiences are welcomed into an immersive pre‑show experience, where performers mingle among the crowd and they can explore mini‑tents, meet performers, and enjoy food and drinks from the themed bars and vendors.

The Greatest Showman was a 2017 musical film by Jenny Bicks and Bill Condon, directed by Michael Gracey, starring Hugh Jackman, Zendaya and Zac Efron. Loosely based on the life of entertainer and circus ringleader PT Barnum, the film won the Golden Globe for Best Original Song for "This Is Me", and the award for Best Compilation Soundtrack for Visual Media at the 2018 Grammys. “This Is Me” was also nominated for Best Original Song at the 2018 Academy Awards.

Main cast:

The show also features singer, dancers, and over 30 internationally acclaimed and award-winning circus performers from Cirque du Soleil and global ensembles, who bring spectacular acrobatics, aerial acts, teeterboard routines, Cyr wheel performances and human pyramids to life.

Come Alive! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular is the brainchild of visionary director Simon Hammerstein - grandson of famed lyricist Oscar Hammerstein II - who conceived the production as a reimagined celebration of The Greatest Showman’s spirit of inclusion and spectacle. Hammerstein’s other projects include hip-hop artist Usher’s sold-out Las Vegas Residency My Way , and immersive dinner theatre show Queen of the Night at New York's The Paramount Hotel. Hammerstein is a Drama Desk Award winner and co-founder of The Box nightclub in Soho. The show’s music is produced by Matthew Brind, an Olivier Award nominee whose previous work includes Paddington 2 and Live Aid jukebox musical Just For One Day . Choreography is by Jerry Reeve, known for his work with pop icon Taylor Swift and the Platinum Jubilee celebration, and Lukas McFarlane, whose credits include Strictly Come Dancing and The X Factor.

Lighting design is by Emmy Award nominee Adam Bassett, whose spectacular work lit up the London 2012 Olympics, with set design by Ruby Law, noted for her work on Warrior Queens . Costumes are by Susan Kulkarni, whose previous credits include The Peripheral and work for Secret Cinema. The production’s immersive art direction is led by Mika Handley, bringing experience from Secret Cinema and the Aladdin/Galaxy Experience.

Step under the Big Top and let the spirit of The Greatest Showman ignite your night with soaring acrobatics, live vocals, and a show full of wonder. Whether you're bringing family, treating visitors, or celebrating a special occasion, Come Alive! delivers spectacle, song and joyful discovery in every moment. Unmissable, unforgettable - and booking fast! Experience one-of-a-kind showmanship and book tickets for Come Alive! The Greatest Showman Circus Spectacular at the Empress Museum. Get in the ring - and prepare to be dazzled!
