shows.name: The Comedy About Spies
shows.description_short: MI5? More like LOL! Mischief Theatre’s spy comedy, set in a world of espionage, returns, featuring spies, mistaken identity and more!
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 10+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Theatre fans, your mission - if you choose to accept it - is simple: enjoy some of the biggest laughs in the West End with The Comedy About Spies ! This riotous play from Mischief Theatre, the team behind the hilarious The Play That Goes Wrong , returns to London this August for a strictly limited run following a sell-out summer season in 2025. “Bigger, better and more brilliantly bungling than ever” (Daily Mail), The Comedy About Spies dives into the murky world of espionage in 1960s London. With stolen plans for a top-secret weapon, hurried meetings in hotels, and a hefty dose of mistaken identity, this Cold War farce is a little less suave than Bond, but much funnier! Don’t find yourself out of the loop: catch this five-star comedy play as it comes to the Adelphi Theatre, just off the Strand, this summer.

It’s London in the 1960s, and there’s fashion, rockin’ music and intrigue at every turn. When a rogue British agent steals top-secret plans for a new weapon, spies from the CIA and KGB meet at the Piccadilly Hotel to smoke out the treacherous mole. This is a Mischief Theatre play, though, so nothing’s simple… and the hotel is coincidentally welcoming a young British couple and an actor auditioning for the first ever James Bond film at the same time!

A straightforward mission for the world’s most daring spies quickly turns into a mess of mistaken identity and miscommunication. As the stakes get higher, will the traitor be found in time? This side-splitting Cold War farce brings together the best tropes from your favourite spy films and flips them on their head. The result? A madcap spoof full of bumbling agents, confused guests, valiantly misguided heroism… and one of the funniest nights out in London.

The Comedy About Spies premiered at the Noel Coward Theatre in summer 2025, and returns for a second missing this August - for eight weeks only!

Multi-award-winning theatre troupe Mischief Theatre have been tickling audiences’ funny bones since 2008 with some of London’s biggest comedy plays. From Olivier Award winner The Play That Goes Wrong to The Comedy About a Bank Robbery and Groan Ups , Mischief proves that it doesn’t have to be all about drama and tragedy in the West End - there’s plenty of room for laughter as well!

Mischief Theatre was formed by Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer, and Henry Shields, with a trusty company of actors regularly appearing in their shows. The Comedy About Spies is co-written by Lewis and Shields and directed by Matt Di Carlo ( The Play That Goes Wrong Off-Broadway, Christmas Carol Goes Wrong ). Fans will be pleased to hear that the original Mischief members will be returning for this silly summer revival, with further casting to be confirmed.

MI5? More like LOL! This new Mischief Theatre comedy is perfect for fans of James Bond, Austin Powers, and every agent in between. This hilarious comedy play will “leave you crying helpless tears of laughter” (Guardian) and sold out its previous run, so move quickly and book your tickets for The Comedy About Spies at Adelphi Theatre today… Our surveillance says it’s a hit!

shows.name: The Misanthrope
shows.description_short: Sandra Oh headlines Martin Crimp’s contemporary take on Moliere’s sharp satire of honesty, hypocrisy and society.
shows.description_full:
You certainly won’t hate this news - Sandra Oh makes her National Theatre debut in The Misanthrope . This brand new production reimagines Molière’s satire for the 21st century in a new version by Martin Crimp, directed by the National’s Artistic Director Indhu Rubasingham. This bitingly witty play, about a brutally honest writer who speaks her mind despite the social ramifications, comes to the National Theatre’s Lyttelton Theatre, by the banks of the River Thames, for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets today.

The Misanthrope is a comedy of manners that exposes the contradictions of social etiquette, honesty and hypocrisy. In this contemporary reinterpretation, the central figure Alceste is reimagined as Alice, a novelist who rejects what she sees as hollow expressions of kindness and polite society. As Alice becomes increasingly outspoken, she faces professional backlash and personal isolation, raising questions about integrity, truth and the price of being unvarnished in a world obsessed with image.

Blending sharp dialogue and modern sensibilities with the enduring wit and moral scrutiny of Molière’s original, The Misanthrope explores how honesty sits alongside societal expectations and public debate in contemporary life.

The Misanthrope was written by Molière and first performed in 1666 at the Théâtre du Palais-Royal in Paris. A verse comedy of manners, the original play centres on Alceste, a man who despises social hypocrisy and insists on absolute honesty, even when it isolates him from the society he inhabits.

Unlike many of Molière’s broader farces, The Misanthrope was more restrained in tone and initially divided audiences, who found its moral ambiguity and unsympathetic central character challenging. Over time, however, it has come to be regarded as one of Molière’s greatest works, admired for its psychological depth, sharp satire and enduring relevance.

This National Theatre staging updates that legacy in a fresh voice, using contemporary verse and perspective to explore how authenticity, especially when wielded by a woman in today’s world, collides with societal norms and expectations.

Casting for this production includes:

Sandra Oh is a Canadian-American actor best known for her long-running role in Grey’s Anatomy , which earned multiple awards and nominations, and as the titular role in the critically acclaimed series Killing Eve . Her screen credits also include Sideways, The Princess Diaries, and Quiz Lady . The Misanthrope marks Oh’s National Theatre debut.

The Misanthrope is adapted by Martin Crimp ( Cyrano de Bergerac, Attempts on Her Life ), from the original play by Molière, and is directed by National Theatre Artistic Director Indhu Rubasingham ( The Father and the Assassin, Red Velvet ), with Gitika Buttoo ( The Effect, Consent ) as associate director.

Set and costume design is by Robert Jones ( My Brilliant Friend, The Lehman Trilogy ), with lighting design by Tim Lutkin ( Life of Pi, Starlight Express ). Jeannette Nelson ( War Horse, Hamlet ) serves as voice coach, associate set and costume design is by Ethan Cheek ( The Ocean at the End of the Lane, Pinocchio ), sound design is by Alexander Caplen ( Patriots, Leopoldstadt ), and original music is composed by Anna Meredith ( Connect It, HandsFree ).

Sandra Oh’s National Theatre debut is sure to be one of the hottest theatrical events of this season - so make sure to book early to avoid disappointment. If you enjoy classic comedy sharpened for modern audiences, The Misanthrope offers wit, bite and big ideas in equal measure. Secure your tickets for The Misanthrope at the National Theatre Lyttelton Stage today.

shows.name: The Cherry Orchard
shows.description_short: Conor McPherson’s striking new adaptation of Chekhov’s classic arrives at the Harold Pinter Theatre, starring Kristin Scott Thomas.
shows.description_full:
Ian Rickson directs Conor McPherson’s new adaptation of The Cherry Orchard, which is heading to the West End. A quietly devastating tragicomedy led by Kristin Scott Thomas, it plays at the Harold Pinter Theatre located close to Leicester Square. This play promises a piece of theatre which remains as timeless and resonant as ever.

The Cherry Orchard captures a world in a delicate and inevitable transition. Chekhov’s play follows an aristocratic Russian family who return to their estate, known for its large cherry orchard, only to be crippled by debt and the likelihood that they will lose their home. Although it is a simplistic story, The Cherry Orchard encapsulates a world that is tilting towards revolution in the 20th century.

Written in 1903, The Cherry Orchard was the last play written by Anton Chekhov. He is also known for publishing other outstanding plays like The Seagull, Three Sisters, and Uncle Vanya. It was first staged at the Moscow Art Theatre a year after it was published in 1904.

A plentiful number of productions of The Cherry Orchard have made it to the UK stage over the years, with the first in 1911 at the Aldwych Theatre. The most notable production starred Zoe Wanamaker at the National Theatre in 2011.

Kristin Scott Thomas is a five-time BAFTA Award and Olivier Award nominee. She won the Olivier Award for Best Actress for her performance in The Seagull , which Ian Rickson also directed. Her film and theatre credits include Nowhere Boy , Salmon Fishing in the Yemen , The Audience and Lyonesse .

Conor McPherson has adapted this production of The Cherry Orchard . He has received Tony and Olivier Award nominations for his work, including The Brightening Air, Girl from the North Country and Uncle Vanya .

The production will be directed by Ian Rickson. He has directed numerous productions across the globe, including The Weir , Jerusalem , and London Tide .

Joining him will be set designer Chloe Lamford, lighting designer Bruno Poet, movement director Shelley Maxwell and casting director Amy Ball CDG.

Don’t miss out on the opportunity to see one of the most poignant plays of our time, performed by the multi-award-winning actress Kristin Scott Thomas. Book your tickets to experience this bold new revival of The Cherry Orchard at the Harold Pinter Theatre for a limited time.

shows.name: The Hunger Games: On Stage
shows.description_short: Join Katniss Everdeen as she battles for survival and self-discovery in this immersive adaptation of the smash-hit book and film series.
shows.description_full:
We volunteer as tribute! This thrilling new adaptation of beloved franchise, The Hunger Games , comes to the stage for the first time this autumn. This heart-pounding new play uses “innovative and immersive staging techniques” (Guardian) to transport audiences right into the heart of The Hunger Games at the brand new purpose-built Canary Wharf Theatre, in the heart of London’s Docklands. Witness Katniss Everdeen’s fight for survival as you’ve never seen it before - secure your tickets for The Hunger Games: On Stage today.

It’s the not-so-distant future, and sixteen-year-old Katniss Everdeen lives in the city of Panem, a poor and underprivileged district in a dystopian region of North America. Katniss and her impoverished family just want to get by, following the death of her father several years before. But when the Capitol declares Katniss’ little sister has been chosen as “tribute” for the 74th annual Hunger Games - a fight to the death between teenagers from each district, organised to entertain the citizens of the wealthy and out-of-touch Capitol - Katniss steps up to take her place. What follows is an exhilarating battle for survival and a journey of self-discovery, as Katniss must muster all her courage, defiance and bravery to survive.

This brand new stage adaptation is based on the young adult novel by Suzanne Collins, and the movie adaptation of the same name. The first in a trilogy, The Hunger Games novel was released in 2008, becoming a global smash hit and topping the bestseller charts. The film adaptation, starring Jennifer Lawrence as Katniss, was released in 2012, also to critical acclaim, and was followed by sequels Catching Fire and Mockingjay Parts 1 and 2. The series has since expanded to include two prequel novels, The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes, and Sunrise on the Reaping , which focus on the youths of adult characters from the novels - including Katniss’s mentor Haymitch and the villainous Coriolanus Snow - which have also been adapted for screen.

Mia Carragher ( The Gathering, Let’s Love ) leads the cast as Katniss Everdeen, with Euan Garrett ( Quadrophenia A Mod Ballet, Billy Elliot the Musical ) as Peeta Mellark, and Joshua Lacey ( All The Old Knives, Wicked ) as Haymitch Abernathy.

They are joined by Sophia Ally as Primrose Everdeen/Tribute Heddle and Ruth Everett as Mrs Everdeen. Stavros Demetraki as Caesar Flickerman, Nathan Ives-Moiba as Cinna, Tristan Waterson as Gale Hawthorne and Tamsin Carroll as Effie Trinket.

Other tributes include Aiya Agustin as Tribute Rue, Liana Cottrill as Tribute Clove, Rory Toms as Tribute Fila, Lewis Easter as Tribute Marvel, Marcellus Hill as Tribute Thresh, Jessica Lee as Tribute Tippet, Mariana Lewis as Tribute Glimmer, Felipe Pacheo as Tribute Cato and fight captain, Redmond Rance as Tribute Stele, Mark Samaras as Tribute Drove and Artemis Stamouli as Tribute Fossa and movement captain.

Completing the cast are Imogen Brooke and Alexandra Barredo as ensemble, Felix Garcia Guyer as fight captain and ensemble, with swings Kyerron Dixon-Bassey, Geo Bailey, Nathanael Saleh, Kiera Milward, led by swing captain Matthew Ive.

The Hunger Games: On Stage is adapted by Conor McPherson and directed by Matthew Dunster.

Conor McPherson is an Olivier award-winning, Tony-nominated Irish Playwright. His credits include The Weir, Girl from the North Country, The Brightening Air and Shining City .

Matthew Dunster is an Olivier-nominated British playwright and director. He is the former Associate Director of both the Young Vic and the Globe Theatre. His credits include 2:22 a Ghost Story in the West End, Love and Money at the Royal Exchange and Young Vic Theatre, and Hangmen at the Royal Court, West End and in the US.

This is a fresh thrilling take on The Hunger Games that we all know and love, so make your way down to the Capitol and get ready to cheer on your favourite tribute. This stage adaptation features breathtaking stunts, thrilling illusions and dazzling stagecraft to bring this epic story of courage, defiance and resilience to life. Don’t miss out - secure your tickets for The Hunger Games: On Stage at Canary Wharf Theatre today!

shows.name: I'm Every Woman: The Chaka Khan Musical
shows.description_short: Celebrate the life and music of Chaka Khan in this electrifying new musical, featuring some of her biggest hits.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 14+. Under 16s must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.work_performed_name: I'm Every Woman: The Chaka Khan Musical
shows.description_full:
Get ready to groove as I'm Every Woman: The Chaka Khan Musical comes to Wembley Park Theatre, right next to Wembley Stadium, this summer. This dazzling new jukebox musical celebrates the life and legacy of the ten-time Grammy Award-winning icon, Chaka Khan, in a strictly limited run. Don't miss your chance to witness the story of a music legend come to life on stage - book your tickets today.

I'm Every Woman delves into the extraordinary life of Chaka Khan, tracing her journey from her early days in Chicago to international stardom. The musical explores her time with the funk band Rufus, her solo career, and her collaborations with music legends like Prince, Stevie Wonder, and Joni Mitchell. The show also delves into her activism, personal struggles, and triumphs, offering an intimate look at the woman behind the music.

Audiences will be treated to a soundtrack of Chaka's greatest hits, including "I Feel For You," "Ain't Nobody," "Sweet Thing," and, of course, "I'm Every Woman”. Blending powerful storytelling and iconic music, I'm Every Woman is a celebration of resilience, passion, and the enduring impact of a true musical icon.

Music and lyrics are by Chaka Khan.

The book is by Nia T. Hill. Hill is an award-winning writer, producer, and director whose credits span theatre, television, and film. She produced and toured Tyler Perry’s first musical I Know I’ve Been Changed , and co-founded Strange Fruit Media, winning an NAACP Image Award for Sunday Best and a Grammy Award for the song "Jesus" performed by Le’Andria Johnson. She has served as creative director for Chaka Khan, producing international concerts and new studio work.

Racky Plews directs. Plews is an award-winning creative whose credits include American Idiot , Footloose , and Josephine Baker . She has also choreographed for the BBC’s The Famous Five.

Choreography is by Jade Hackett. Hackett’s credits include Miss Saigon (Sheffield Crucible), Hex (National Theatre), and Get Up Stand Up! The Bob Marley Musical (West End).

Chaka Khan - Alexandra Burke

Since winning the fifth series of X Factor in 2008, Alexandra Burke has hit Number One in the UK Charts and appeared in several stage productions, including The Bodyguard, Sister Act and The Addams Family .

Further casting includes:

Born Yvette Marie Stevens, Chaka Khan is an American singer whose career spans over five decades. She rose to fame in the 1970s as the lead vocalist of the funk band Rufus, delivering hits like "Tell Me Something Good" and "Ain't Nobody." Embarking on a solo career in 1978, she achieved success with songs such as "I'm Every Woman" and "I Feel for You." Known as the "Queen of Funk," Khan has sold approximately 70 million records worldwide and won ten Grammy Awards. In 2023, she was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, recognising her significant contributions to music.

With 22 albums, 25 Billboard-charted songs, and over 70 million records sold, Khan's influence on pop, soul, jazz, and funk is undeniable. I'm Every Woman marks the first time Khan's life will be explored on stage - her passion for civil rights, her struggles with addiction and against a toxic music industry, and her triumphs as a working woman.

Nobody does it better than Chaka Khan! So put on your dancing shoes and shimmy on down to Wembley Park Theatre. You won't want to miss the chance to see the powerful, inspirational and uplifting story of a true music legend as you’ve never seen it before. Book your tickets for I'm Every Woman: The Chaka Khan Musical today.

shows.name: The Nutcracker
shows.description_short: Enjoy a Christmas classic as London’s most popular festive ballet returns for another exhilarating season. Best for age 5+.
shows.typical_age_range: Under 5s not permitted. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Escape into a world of whimsy, magic and incredible dancing this winter as The Nutcracker returns to St Martin's Lane's historic London Coliseum for its annual festive season. The classic ballet has been a staple of Christmas theatre in London for over 50 years, and English National Ballet Artistic Director Aaron S. Watkin’s new version promises to keep the spirit alive. Anything is possible in this enchanting production, so book your tickets early for a spot in the audience this year.

It is a cold and frosty Christmas Eve in Edwardian London, and Clara is unwrapping a special present. As the paper falls off, an enchanted Nutcracker appears and whisks her off on a spellbinding adventure. In this magical new world, Clara meets handsome strangers, comes face-to-face with the terrifying Mouse King, and drifts high above the Land of Snow in a hot air balloon. Exquisite dance, soaring music from Tchaikovsky and gorgeous visual storytelling make The Nutcracker a pure spectacle that will delight all ages, as an array of wonderful characters leap, pirouette and glide across the stage.

This year, enjoy a new re-telling of the classic tale, as world-class dancers and musicians transform the Coliseum into a winter wonderland. English National Ballet’s Artistic Director Aaron S. Watkin and Olivier Award-winner Arielle Smith choreograph a dance extravaganza, with sets and costumes designed by Dick Bird. As Clara absconds from her London home into a technicolour new world populated by the beautiful Sugar Plum Fairy and handsome Cavalier, a rush of excitement and possibility sends a thrill through the auditorium.

Russian composer Tchaikovsky wrote The Nutcracker’s score, and even those newest to ballet and classical music will be familiar with the lively notes of “Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy” and “The Nutcracker Suite”. There is no better place to hear the opening notes and soaring melodies of this world-famous work than at the beautiful London Coliseum. Even five decades on, the dynamic mix of dance and music is still an exhilarating experience for theatregoers, who return time after time to enjoy the enduring magic of this award-winning production.

This magnificent ballet has kept audiences rapt with wonder for generations, and for many, it marks the beginning of the most wonderful time of the year! Avoid disappointment and secure your Nutcracker tickets now; this is a sell-out year on year.

shows.name: Cyrano de Bergerac
shows.description_short: Love, lies, poetry and disguise: Adrian Lester stars as the large-nosed wordsmith in this RSC transfer of Rostand’s romantic drama.
shows.description_full:
Adrian Lester reprises his role as long-nosed wordsmith Cyrano, as the RSC’s acclaimed production of Cyrano de Bergerac transfers to the Noel Coward Theatre, in the heart of Covent Garden. Ashamed of his large nose, soldier and poet Cyrano is drawn into a scheme to help his hapless love rival charm the woman he too is secretly in love with in this “warm and likeable”(Guardian) revival of Edmond Rostand’s romantic play. This production was critically acclaimed when it debuted in Stratford in 2025, and comes to London for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets today.

'No master to serve, no leash to bear. I walk as I please, and I speak as I dare.'

Soldier, poet and philosopher, delightfully witty and deeply romantic, Cyrano de Bergerac is a truly brilliant man. There’s just one small, or large, problem - his remarkable nose.

Wilfully proud, but wracked with doubts over his appearance, Cyrano must watch from afar as the woman he loves - the bold, beautiful, poetry-loving Roxanne - falls for another man. But this suitor, Christian, whilst handsome, does not have a way with words - and he knows this is the only way to truly win Roxanne’s heart. Enter Cyrano, who must help Christian woo the woman he loves from the shadows.

Cyrano de Bergerac was written in 1897 by Edmond Rostand, loosely based on the life of the real 17th-century novelist and playwright, Cyrano de Bergerac. Though the real Cyrano did have a somewhat large nose, this element is generally very exaggerated for performance. The play, which is written entirely in verse, first premiered at the Théâtre de la Porte Saint-Martin, France, and has since had many notable adaptations - including Jamie Lloyd’s stripped back 2019 production starring James McAvoy, a 1970 production translated by Anthony Burgess, and several film adaptations including Netflix movie The Half of It , and Golden Globe-winning musical adaption Cyrano , which starred Peter Dinklage. The play is credited with introducing the word "panache" (meaning a confident and flamboyant style) into the English language.

This Royal Shakespeare Company production premiered at the Swan Theatre, Stratford-upon-Avon, in 2025 to critical acclaim, adapting the original verse in a mixture of rhyming couplets and modern English, and now transfers to London for a limited season.

Casting includes:

Adrian Lester reprises his role as Cyrano from the shows RSC run, which marked his debut with the company. Lester is an Olivier, Evening Standard Theatre Award and a Critics' Circle Theatre Award winner, and Tony nominee. He is best known for his roles in The Sandman, Riviera, Company, The Lehman Trilogy, Henry V , and for playing the titular role in Othello , opposite Rory Kinnear as Iago, at the National Theatre.

Cyrano de Bergerac was originally written by French dramatist and poet Edmond Rostand. The show is directed by Simon Evans ( Staged, The Dazzle, A Day in the Death of Joe Egg ), who also co-adapted the script with playwright and poet Debris Stevenson ( My Brother’s a Genius, Poet in Da Corner ).

Adrian Lester’s “standout” (Guardian) performance as the nosy, love-lorn poet “crackles with an electricity” (Broadway Baby) in this lyrical, romantic tale of love, lies, longing and disguise. Cyrano de Bergerac is full of “emotional authenticity and neat, quipping wit” (Guardian). You’ll fall for this brand new revival, so don’t miss this strictly limited RSC transfer - book tickets for Cyrano de Bergerac at the Noel Coward Theatre today.

shows.name: Hay Fever
shows.description_short: Emily Burns directs Hay Fever, a new production starring Christine Baranski and Richard E. Grant in the West End this autumn.
shows.description_full:
Catch Christine Baranski and Richard E. Grant, two of the biggest stars from stage and screen, in a dazzling new production of Hay Fever this autumn. Noel Coward’s irresistible 1920s comedy, set in an English country house, will play at the Wyndham’s Theatre, just a short walk from the National Gallery in the heart of Central London. Book your tickets for Hay Fever and don’t miss out on director Emily Burns’ take on this irresistibly good show that is only in the West End for a limited time!

Described as a cross between a high farce and a satirical comedy, Hay Fever follows Judith, a retired actor who is idling away with her family at their country home. Her husband, David, is a reclusive novelist, and her children, Sorrel and Simon, are failing to launch their own aspirations.

When each member of the Bliss family invites an unexpected guest to their home for the weekend, the house quickly erupts into unforgettable chaos. Over the course of three acts, the Bliss family seem to manage to bombard their guests with activities and conversation, who are lukewarm to their advances. Judith is the most ridiculous of them all, making wild exaggerations and exclamations and even performing an overdramatic faint. Will the poor guests be able to escape this family of lunatics with their dignity intact?

Hay Fever is one of Noel Coward’s most beloved plays, which first debuted in the West End in 1925. Subsequent revivals on the stage happened over the years across London, including a run at The Old Vic in the 1960s. The play has also been adapted for television over the years. The most recent production of Hay Fever was directed by Lindsay Posner in 2015 at the Duke of York’s Theatre, where it played after a debut at the Theatre Royal Bath.

Christine Baranski will make her West End debut as Judith Bliss in the West End revival of Hay Fever . She is one of the most honoured actresses in entertainment, with several accolades, including an Emmy and two Tony Awards. She is currently starring as Agnes Van Rhijn in the acclaimed HBO series, The Gilded Age . Other credits include Mamma Mia! , Into the Woods , How the Grinch Stole Christmas and Chicago .

Richard E. Grant will star as David Bliss. The award-winning actor has an incredible fleet of work on stage and screen, including Everybody’s Talking About Jamie , Saltburn and Downton Abbey . His role in this revival of Hay Fever at the Wyndham’s Theatre will mark Richard’s return to the West End stage after 20 years.

Further casting for the play is to be announced.

Emily Burns directs the production. An associate of the Bridge Theatre, and previously of the National Theatre, her theatre credits include Measure for Measure , Dear Octopus and The Comeback .

Catch two of the greatest actors of our time, two-time Tony Award-winning actress Christine Baranski and Academy Award nominee Richard E. Grant, as you’ve never seen them before, in what is set to be one of the most talked about productions in the West End this year. Book your tickets for Hay Fever and experience a blissful combination of wit and chaos at the Wyndham’s Theatre!

shows.name: Heathers The Musical
shows.description_short: Join the murderous students of Westerburg High as the WhatsOnStage Award-winning musical, based on the cult film, returns to London.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 14+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
The students of Westerburg High are back in London and more murderous than ever as Heathers comes to The Arts at Marble Arch Theatre, just a short walk from Hyde Park, for a strictly limited season. This deliciously dark musical has been a fan favourite for over a decade, and now theatregoers can say “greetings and salutations” to even more black comedy, murderous plots, and queen bee cliques as Heathers the Musical returns to one of the capital’s most exciting new venues.

Welcome to Westerburg High, where popularity can be a matter of life and death. Social reject Veronica Sawyer is just trying to get by, but when she is unexpectedly taken in by the beautiful but cruel Heathers, her dreams of being popular seem to be coming true. Her newfound status is rocked by rebel newcomer JD, however, and soon Veronica learns that it might hurt to be a nobody, but being somebody could lead to murder…

Wickedly funny and whip-smart, Heathers The Musical is adapted from the 1988 cult film, which famously starred Winona Ryder and Christian Slater. Fans of the film can enjoy its biggest, bloodiest and most hilarious moments reimagined via songs including “Candy Store”, “Seventeen” and “Dead Girl Walking”. Since its UK stage debut in 2018, it has become a sensation amongst young theatregoers and won the fan-voted WhatsOnStage Award for Best New Musical.

Casting is to be announced.

Heathers is adapted for the stage with a book, music and lyrics by Emmy nominee Kevin Murphy ( Desperate Housewives, Reefer Madness! the Musical ), and Lucile Lortel winner Laurence O’Keefe ( Legally Blonde the Musical, Bat Boy: The Musical ). Andy Fickman ( She’s the Man, Reefer Madness! the Musical ) directs, with choreography by Gary Lloyd ( Cruel Intentions the Musical, Sweet Charity ).

Honey, what you waiting for? Don’t be a loser: join the clique that everyone’s talking about with Heathers ! The killer musical will get your heart racing and your feet tapping as chaos descends in the school halls - book tickets for Heathers at the Arts Theatre Marble Arch today!

shows.name: Cirque Alice
shows.description_short: Lewis Carroll’s timeless tale is transformed into a kaleidoscopic adventure from the creators behind Circus 1903. Best for age 5+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Under 12s must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
This Christmas, embark on a unique, kaleidoscopic adventure with some of the world’s greatest performers around the globe. Lewis Carroll’s timeless tale provides the perfect backdrop for this new show, which makes its European premiere this festive season. Playing at the Royal Festival Hall, located on the South Bank of the River Thames, Cirque Alice will dazzle audiences of all ages with a "wondrous evening filled to overflowing with incredible memories that will last a lifetime!" (Australian Arts Review).

Filled with some of the most eccentric and larger-than-life characters, Cirque Alice reimagines the popular classic story by Lewis Carroll. The story follows a girl named Alice who falls down a rabbit hole into a different world where she encounters curious characters like the Mad Hatter and the Queen of Hearts in a quest to get back home.

This new production is brought to life by a collective of aerialists, contortionists, and puppeteers, along with special guest music and comedy artists. Cirque Alice is set to a captivating soundtrack of newly arranged contemporary renditions of some of the world’s favourite classical hits.

Cirque Alice made its debut with sell-out seasons in Australia and Singapore. Brought to life by the same creators behind The Illusionists and Circus 1903 , this production marks the European premiere with a festive season at the Southbank Centre’s Royal Festival Hall.

The Creative Team for Cirque Alice is headed by creative directors Ash Jacks McCready and Kirsty Painter, with choreography by Dane Bates, costumes by Angela Aaron, lighting by Hugo Mercier and musical arrangements by Martin Raabe-Olsen & Marius Christiansen. Roast Productions and Senbla present the show in association with Tim Lawson and Simon Painter.

Take a trip down the rabbit hole into the breathtaking world of Cirque Alice at the Southbank Centre's Royal Festival Hall this Christmas. Perfect for families, festive outings, or special celebrations; book your tickets to see the "wonderland of its own making" (The Fame Reporter) - and don’t be late!

shows.name: Inter Alia
shows.description_short: Rosamund Pike stars in this searing drama about motherhood and masculinity from the award-winning team behind Prima Facie.
shows.description_full:
From the award-winning team behind Prima Facie comes bold new play Inter Alia , transferring to Wyndham’s Theatre, in the heart of Leicester Square, this spring, following a smash hit run at the National Theatre in 2024. A blistering exploration of motherhood, modern masculinity, and the justice system, Inter Alia stars Saltburn and Gone Girl star Rosamund Pike. This production is “simply superb” (Financial Times). Inter Alia transfers to the West End for a strictly limited run, so be sure to grab tickets before they’re gone, girl!

inter alia (in-tur eh-lee-ah) prep. Latin, meaning "among other things."

London Crown Court Judge Jessica Parks is top of her game - considered by some to be a maverick, Jessica challenges and changes the judicial system with each case. At home too, she seems to have it made - feminist, friend and karaoke fiend, Jessica is a loving wife and a supportive parent and balances the juggling act between working mum and eminent judge with ease. But when an earth-shattering event throws this balance off course, Jessica must reconcile her beliefs, and fight to keep her family together.

Inspired by her previous work as a lawyer, playwright Suzie Miller’s plays often centre around social justice and the legal system, including the award-winning, one-woman play Prima Facie. Prima Facie debuted in Miller’s native Australia in 2019 and later transferred to the West End and Broadway, starring Jodie Comer, where it sold out both runs and earned critical acclaim, winning two Olivier Awards and a Tony Award. Inter Alia debuted at the National Theatre in September 2025 to critical acclaim, and now transfers to the West End in March 2026.

Emmy and Golden Globe winner and BAFTA and Academy Award Nominee Rosamund Pike leads the cast as Jessica Parks. Pike is a former Bond Girl and is best known for her roles in Pride and Prejudice, Saltburn, The Wheel of Time , and as the unhinged Amy Dunne in Gone Girl . The National Theatre premiere of Inter Alia marked Pike’s first time treading the boards in over 15 years; previous stage credits include Gas Light at the Old Vic and Tennessee Williams’s Summer and Smoke at the Apollo Theatre.

Joining Pike is Jamie Glover ( Noises Off, Waterloo Road ) as Jessica’s husband Andrew Parks, reprising his role from the National Theatre run.

Cast in the role of Harry, Jessica’s son, is Corma McAlinden. Harry was played by Jasper Talbot ( Broadchurch, Redlands ) in the original production.

Inter Alia is written by playwright Suzie Miller ( Prima Facie, The Mathematics of Longing, Jailbaby ) and directed by Justin Miller ( Prima Facie, Stranger Things: The First Shadow, Kyoto ).

The award-winning creative team also includes set and costume design by Miriam Buether ( The Jungle ), lighting design by Natasha Chivers ( Sylvia ), sound design by Ben and Max Ringham ( A Dolls House ), and video design by Willie Williams ( We Will Rock You ) for Treatment Studio.

If you weren’t lucky enough to catch the National Theatre’s critically acclaimed run of Inter Alia , now you have a second chance to see superstar Rosamund Pike live on stage. This “ searing legal drama“ *Guardian) from one of Britain’s most exciting playwrights transfers to the West End for a strictly limited season in March 2026. It’d be an injustice to miss it - book tickets for Inter Alia at Wyndham’s Theatre today!

shows.name: State Ballet of Georgia: Swan Lake
shows.description_short: The acclaimed Georgian company return with their “immaculate” (Charleston City Paper) production of Tchaikovsky's classic ballet.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Under 5s not permitted. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.work_performed_name: State Ballet of Georgia: Swan Lake
shows.description_full:
The State Ballet of Georgia return to the London Coliseum, in the heart of Leicester Square, with their breathtaking production of Tchaikovsky ballet Swan Lake . The State Ballet of Georgia made history in 2024 when they brought the show to London for the first time in the acclaimed company’s almost two-century-long history, and now this “beautiful and immaculate” (Charleston City Paper) production returns to the UK for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets for the State Ballet of Georgia: Swan Lake today.

When Prince Siegfried falls in love with the beautiful Princess Odette, he doesn’t know her biggest secret - and that he may be her salvation. The Princess has been cursed by the dastardly sorcerer Von Rothbart, and is only human by night; by day, Odette is a swan, and only a vow of true love can restore her humanity permanently. When the sorcerer's daughter, the Black Swan Odile, interferes with a cruel trick, causing Siegfried to mistakenly betray his love, Odette may lose her last chance to become human again, forever…

Swan Lake is brought to life by the company of “versatile and accomplished” dancers (The New York Times), in this full-length production of the classical ballet, featuring sumptuous costumes, stunning sets, and an immaculate corps de swans, set to Tchaikovsky’s timeless score.

The State Ballet of Georgia is the ballet company of the Z. Paliashvili Opera and Ballet State Theatre in Tbilisi, Georgia. Founded in 1852 by Count Mikhail Vorontsov, a Russian governor in Georgia, the ballet was initially host to visiting troupes from St. Petersburg, Moscow, Kiev, and Warsaw. In 1935 the company was formally established as a resident company by dancer and choreographer Vakhtang Chabukiani, designer Simon Virsaladze, and composer Andria Balanchivadze, becoming a premier Soviet company renowned for mixing classical, Russian, and Italian technique.

Georgia-born dancer Nina Ananiashvili has been Artistic Director of the company since 2004. Considered to be one of the world's greatest dancers, Nina Ananiashvili is a former prima ballerina with the Bolshoi Ballet and the American Ballet Theatre.

The company has previously staged the production in London in 2024, and prior to this performed work at the Edinburgh International Festival.

Swan Lake is written by Russian composer Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky. The show is performed by members of the State Ballet of Georgia company, accompanied by the Royal Ballet Sinfonia. The production is under the artistic direction of the company AD Nina Ananiashvili.

Ananiashvili's accolades include a People's Artist of Russia award, both a Georgian Order of Honour and Medal of Honour (Georgia's highest order), and a Presidential Order of Light.

You won't want to miss this elegantly staged, “dazzlingly polished” (Charleston City Paper) Swan Lake , performed by a stunning company who “bring a lot of beauty, heart, and soul to their work” (Georgian Times). With breathtaking costumes, a company of over 70 dancers and a live orchestra performing Tchaikovsky’s iconic score. This acclaimed Georgia ballet company performing in London is a rare treat - secure your tickets to the State Ballet of Georgia: Swan Lake at the London Coliseum today.

shows.name: Paddington The Musical
shows.description_short: Join a little bear for a very big adventure! Paddington finds a new home in London in this brand new musical adaptation. Best for age 6+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 6+. Under 3s and babes in arms not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Get ready for a most wonderful adventure, as Paddington The Musical comes to London’s Savoy Theatre. This heartwarming new musical tells the story of everyone’s favourite bear as you’ve never seen him before. So book your tickets today and join Paddington as he finds a new home in London - just be sure to pack plenty of marmalade sandwiches!

When his Aunt Lucy retires to a Home for Retired Bears, a young spectacled bear travels to London from his homeland of Peru, seeking a new home. With only a hat, coat and a case full of marmalade sandwiches to his name, the bear is lost, afraid, and lonely. When the Brown family meet the cub at Paddington station, with a note that reads “Please look after this bear” pinned to his lapel, they bring him along to their house in Windsor Gardens, despite Mr. Brown’s misgivings.

Though Paddington causes plenty of mishaps and hijinks, he soon settles into his new life in England. But, Paddington discovers that London isn't all polite smiles and cups of tea, and that the Brown family have plenty of problems of their own. When a sinister villain appears, putting Paddington’s life at risk, the Browns must decide just how far they’re willing to go to make this special little bear a part of the family.

First appearing in 1958 in the children's book A Bear Called Paddington by Michael Bond, Paddington has since become one of the most beloved fictional characters in the UK. As well as 29 best-selling books starring the precocious little bear, Paddington has also appeared in commercials, an immersive experience and even in a skit with Queen Elizabeth II as part of the Platinum Party at the Palace. Bond’s books have been adapted into multiple animated TV series, as well as a BAFTA-winning film series starring Ben Whishaw as the voice of Paddington.

This brand new musical is based on Bond’s popular book series and shows the much-loved character as you’ve never seen him before, so don’t miss out - secure your tickets today!

Paddington The Musical has music and lyrics by Tom Fletcher, with a book by Jessica Swale. The show is directed by Luke Sheppard.

A songwriter, vlogger, children’s author and musician, Fletcher is perhaps best known as one of the lead vocalists of pop band McFly. As a songwriter, Fletcher has written 10 number one singles, penning hits for bands including One Direction, Busted, and 5 Seconds of Summer. He has previously written the music and lyrics for adaptations of his children's books The Creakers and The Dinosaur that Pooped.

Jessica Swale is an Olivier Award-winning playwright, director and screenwriter. Her work includes the screenplay for Summerland , the play Blue Stockings at Shakespeare’s Globe, Nell Gwyn at the Globe and in the West End and The Overwhelming at the Royal National Theatre.

Luke Sheppard is an Olivier and Tony Award-nominated director. His previous credits include Starlight Express , In the Heights , Billionaire Boy and My Son’s a Queer (But What Can You Do?).

The musical is adapted from A Bear Called Paddington , the first book in the Paddington series, by Michael Bond (1925-2017). Bond was a popular children's author and screenwriter. As well as penning over 29 Paddington books - the last of which was published posthumously in 2018 - Bond is the author of the Olga da Polga series, the Monsieur Pamplemousse series, Here Comes Thursday and many more. Bond held both an OBE and a CBE for his services to children's literature, and sold over 35 million copies of the Paddington books worldwide.

Main cast:

No! Paddington Bear is played by two people who work together to bring the marmalade-munching bear to life. Arti Shah ( Star Wars, Attack the Block, Guardians of the Galaxy ) is in costume, performing movement and choreography, and James Hameed is the "off-stage performer" providing the voice and remotely controlled facial expressions. The alternate Paddington on-stage performers are Abbie Purvis and Ali Sarebani.

So come and join this little bear on a very big adventure! Paddington The Musical is an uplifting, hilarious and moving new show jam-packed (or should we say marmalade-packed?) with dazzling choreography, catchy songs and plenty of heart. Book tickets for Paddington at the Savoy Theatre today and see this iconic cultural figure like never before!

shows.name: Yamato: Hito no Chikara
shows.description_short: Thunderous drumming meets athletic precision, as the Japanese taiko drummers return with  new show about technology and human connection.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Under 5s not permitted. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
Get ready to feel the rhythm as Yamato: Hito no Chikara comes to London’s Peacock Theatre, in the heart of Holborn. This new physical theatre show, presented by Yamato - the drummers of Japan - confronts a world transformed by technology and asks what remains uniquely human through thunderous taiko drumming and dynamic choreography. Yamato are “energetic, noisy, varied and exhilarating” (Independent) - you won't want to miss this deeply special show.

After successful London seasons in 2019 and 2024, Japanese drumming group Yamato return with a brand new show that explores the essence of human energy and connection. Hito no Chikara translates as “The Power of Human Strength”, and each drum strike resonates with resilience and passion, a celebration of physical and emotional strength in an age shaped by digital technology. Weaving explosive rhythms with moments of delicacy, Hito no Chikara allows you to feel the pulse of humanity through the visceral power of Japanese drumming.

Taiko drumming has deep roots in Japanese history, where large barrel-shaped drums were traditionally used in religious ceremonies, festivals and community gatherings to mark time, signal events and unite people through rhythm. The word taiko simply means “drum”, but the form encompasses a wide range of styles, from ceremonial shrine music to regional folk traditions. Modern ensemble taiko, developed in the 20th century, transformed these traditions into a dynamic performance art, combining powerful, synchronised drumming with choreographed movement and intense physical discipline. Today, taiko is recognised worldwide for its ability to convey energy, unity and human connection through sound.

Yamato’s origins lie in this rich history, and since forming in the 1990s, the company has pushed this heritage into theatrical contexts, crafting showpieces that combine athletic performance with musical precision, bringing their previous show Wings of the Phoenix to London twice to critical acclaim.

Yamato: Hito no Chikara is a company performance rather than a cast-led theatrical production. The ensemble comprises highly trained taiko drummers drawn from the Yamato company, founded in 1993 in Japan’s Kansai region under Artistic Director Masa Ogawa. The show blends traditional wadaiko taiko drumming with contemporary staging to highlight the physicality and communal energy of the performers.

Don’t miss this limited engagement as The Drummers of Japan come to shake London. Yamato’s shows are “Pure energy meets spiritual high” (The Scotsman), and their brand new production celebrates humanity in an age of technological disconnection through traditional music. Feel the rhythm, book your tickets for Yamato: Hito no Chikara at the Peacock Theatre today!

shows.name: Much Ado About Nothing
shows.description_short: Sharp tongues, sparring lovers and mischievous plots abound in this stylish, status-obsessed new staging of Shakespeare’s timeless rom-com
shows.description_full:
Lovers fight and make up, and mischievous plots are unravelled as Shakespeare’s hilarious comedy Much Ado About Nothing returns to Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre on the banks of the Thames. This timeless tale of love conquering all and the iconic battle of wits between Benedick and Beatrice is one of Shakespeare’s best-known works, and this brand-new production transplants the action to a world of late-night parties, style and status. Much Ado is the perfect play to enjoy in London this summer - secure your tickets today.

Years of war are finally over, and the soldiers are standing down. But in image-obsessed Messina, nothing is quite as it seems - and gossip, plotting, and mind-games are soon afoot. Young lovers Claudio and Hero, and sharp-tongued singletons Beatrice and Benedick, are about to discover that their idyllic surroundings are the setting for a series of deceptions that might end in tragedy. And while the war may be over, the battle for true love has just begun...

Shakespeare’s celebration of love and redemption is the ultimate original rom-com! With sharp wit, laugh-out-loud arguments, and heartwarming romance, you'll be swept up in this timeless tale of love in all its forms.

Much Ado About Nothing is one of William Shakespeare’s most popular and frequently performed comedies. Believed to have been written in 1598 or 1599, the play was likely first performed at the newly built Globe Theatre in London. Blending witty romantic sparring with darker themes of deception and honour, the play explores misunderstandings, mistaken identities, and clever wordplay.

Its enduring appeal lies in its balance of sharp humour and emotional depth, particularly in the fiery love-hate dynamic between Beatrice and Benedick, with acclaimed actors such as Kenneth Branagh and Emma Thompson, David Tennant and Catherine Tate, and Vanessa Redgrave and James Earl Jones tackling the roles of the quarrelling lovers.

Much Ado About Nothing was first published in quarto form in 1600, and has remained a staple of the stage ever since, with stagings spanning from David Garrick’s 18th-century productions to notable modern interpretations, including acclaimed film adaptations, including Kenneth Branagh’s 1993 version (which also starred Keanu Reeves and Denzel Washington), and Buffy creator Joss Whedon’s 2012 version.

Casting for this production includes:

Much Ado About Nothing is written by William Shakespeare.

Chelsea Walker directs. Walker is an award-winning director whose credits include the recent stage adaptation of The Da Vinci Code , All’s Well That Ends Well, and Hedda Gabler . Design is by Sami Fendall ( Brigadoon, Hamlet ), with music by composer, multi-instrumentalist and recording artist Angus MacRae ( Mrs Warren’s Profession, Cabaret on Broadway).

Sigh no more, ladies, gents and everyone in between, because you can laugh, swoon and revel in the ultimate battle of wits, romance and redemption with Much Ado About Nothing . Whether you’re a Groundling or sitting on the benches, the irrepressible joy of this ageless tale, told in a sizzling new staging, promises to be the ultimate Shakespeare experience this summer. Book tickets for Much Ado About Nothing at Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre today!

shows.name: Romeo and Juliet
shows.description_short: Robert Icke directs Stranger Things star Sadie Sink and Hamnet's Noah Jupe as the star-crossed lovers in Shakespeare's romantic tragedy.
shows.description_full:
Stranger Things star Sadie Sink makes her West End debut in Romeo and Juliet , directed by revolutionary director Robert Icke. Sink is joined by rising star Noah Jupe as Romeo. This bold new staging of Shakespeare’s timeless romantic tragedy comes to the Harold Pinter Theatre in the heart of Leicester Square and promises to be the hottest show of the season - secure your tickets today!

"Parting is such sweet sorrow."

The two oldest families in Verona, the Capulets and Montagues, have been feuding for an age. So when Juliet, the Capulets' youngest daughter, and Romeo, a Montague, fall for each other at a party during the height of summer, their forbidden love and whirlwind romance spark a series of events that can only end in tragedy…

Romeo and Juliet is one of William Shakespeare’s most performed tragedies. The plot is loosely based on an Italian tale by Matteo Bandello, which is one of many such tragic love stories, and which was translated into verse in 1562 as The Tragical History of Romeus and Juliet by Arthur Brooke, and later in prose in 1567 as part of the story collection Palace of Pleasure by William Painter. Shakespeare’s version of the story was inspired by both translations, but was significantly expanded, adding supporting characters and secondary plots. The play is believed to have been written between 1591 and 1595, as part of the first folio.

Romeo and Juliet has been revived and adapted numerous times, including several times for film with significant adaptations including the 1968 version by Franco Zeffirelli and Baz Luhrmann's 1996 Romeo + Juliet , which starred Clare Danes and Leonardo DiCaprio.

Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe lead the cast as Juliet and Romeo, both making their West End debut in the roles.

Sadie Sink is an American actor best known for her role as Max in Stranger Things . She is a Tony nominee, and was the second youngest woman in history to be nominated for ‘Best Actress’. Her credits include The Whale, Fear Street , Annie and John Proctor is the Villain on Broadway.

Noah Jupe is a British actor best known for his roles in Honey Boy, A Quiet Place Part II, Lady in the Lake for Apple TV, Ford v Ferrari , and most recently Hamnet with Paul Mescal. He is an Independent Spirit nominee.

Robert Icke directs. Icke is a playwright and director, best known for his play The Doctor and his modern adaptation of classics including Mary Stuart starring Juliet Stevenson, Oedipus with Mark Strong and Lesley Manville and 1984 for Headlong theatre company. His directing credits include Manhunt, which he also wrote, for the Royal Court, Hamlet starring Andrew Scott for the Almeida and Henry IV, Parts 1 and 2 starring Ian McKellen. He is a former associate director at Headlong Theatre and the Almeida.

Further creative team members are to be confirmed.

Don't miss Sadie Sink and Noah Jupe as star-crossed lovers in their West End debut, with direction by "one of the most important forces in today’s theatre." (Observer), Robert Icke. It’d be a tragedy to miss it - book your tickets for Romeo and Juliet at the Harold Pinter Theatre today.

shows.name: A Christmas Carol
shows.description_short: The Old Vic’s hit show returns! Immerse yourself in the warmth, cheer, and charity of this timeless Christmas classic. Best for age 8+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 8+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Warm your cockles at Waterloo's historic Old Vic Theatre this festive season as their magical, smash-hit production of A Christmas Carol returns for its tenth year. Charles Dickens’ tale of miserly Scrooge discovering the spirit of the season is “pure joy to behold” (The Telegraph) on stage, in a play that fills the theatre to the brim with goodwill, merriment and the smell of freshly baked mince pies.

Tight-fisted moneylender Ebenezer Scrooge has only one thing on his mind: money, and how to make more of it. Even Christmas Day can’t stop him, a fact his employee, Bob Cratchit, knows only too well when he has to grovel for a day off with his family. But everything changes when Scrooge is visited by the ghost of an old colleague on Christmas Eve. Marley has an ominous warning: before dawn breaks on Christmas morning, he will encounter three more ghosts with a chilling message…

Charles Dickens’s beloved story about a man whose heart thaws as he embraces the spirit of Christmas has been a firm favourite for over 170 years. It is one of the most adapted stories ever told, with everything from musicals to Muppets bringing the cast - including Cratchit, Tiny Tim, and the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present and Yet to Come - to life for several generations.

Step into the Old Vic auditorium and you will be struck by the hustle and bustle of Victorian England, and the smell of oranges and mince pies. This unique in-the-round staging brims with music, merriment and the joys of the most wonderful time of the year. Since premiering in 2017, director Matthew Warchus (Matilda the Musical ) and playwright Jack Thorne’s ( Harry Potter and the Cursed Child ) A Christmas Carol at the Old Vic is now a festive tradition that theatregoers return to again and again.

Casting is to be announced.

Start a new tradition for your family with A Christmas Carol tickets this year. Inside the Old Vic, you’ll feel as if you’ve dropped right into the heart of Victorian London! Find out why this classic tale remains one of the most beloved Christmas stories; this show is a treat that will touch even the frostiest of hearts this winter.

shows.name: Magic Mike Live
shows.description_short: Let your hair down with a night of irresistible, temperature-raising entertainment, inspired by Channing Tatum’s sizzling film franchise.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 18+. Under 18s will not be admitted.
shows.description_full:
Let your hair down and enjoy an unforgettable night out at London’s hottest show, Magic Mike Live .

Packed with sizzling choreography from every angle - especially in the front rows - Magic Mike Live brings together some of the world’s most talented dancers, acrobats and musicians for 90 minutes of temperature-raising, adrenaline-fuelled performance. Liberating and exhilarating, fans of the hit film franchise will have the time of their lives watching the guys strut their stuff live on stage at the Hippodrome Casino in between vibrant Soho and Covent Garden.

This isn’t Chippendales! Get ready to enjoy eye-popping acrobatics, dynamic hip-hop and street dancing, pulse-raising stripteases and lapdances for those who want them, all performed by an incredible cast of multi-talented international dancers and set to live music. You might even be invited onto the stage for an extra-immersive experience if you’re lucky!

Perfect for a girls’ night, birthdays, retirement parties, or just to let your hair down, Magic Mike Live is an unforgettable night of entertainment that will get your pulse racing. It’s a sexy, cheeky, empowering display of fantasy and sexuality. Now a worldwide hit, the show has been entertaining audiences in London since 2018, so book now to find out why everyone loves it!

Conceived and directed by Channing Tatum, Magic Mike Live is inspired by the sensational film and its sequel, and lovingly adapted for the stage by Tatum himself. It is co-directed by Tatum and Alison Faulk, with sensual, powerful choreography by Alison Faulk, Teresa Espinosa and Luke Broadlick.

Although Channing Tatum doesn’t appear on stage, his tantalising vision for the show is apparent from the moment you enter and grab your drinks, right through to the red-hot finale.

Main cast:

Don’t just take our word for it: Magic Mike Live has reviewers hot under the collar and amazed at the talent on display! This “sexy, entertaining” (Voice of London) show isn’t just a run-of-the-mill strip show; it’s got serious talent on display that takes the spirit of the film and turns it into a full 365-degree experience, from the moment you step into the custom-designed auditorium.

Whether you’re booking for a hen or stag do, special occasion, or just want to party with friends, make sure Magic Mike Live tickets are at the top of your list!

faq.answer: No. Magic Mike live on stage involves some clothes being removed and cheeky moments, but it stays above the belt (excuse the pun), with a focus on dance and entertainment. You can learn more about what to expect in our Magic Mike London review.

faq.question: Is there audience interaction?
faq.answer: The performers come out into the audience and will pull people up to dance or interact throughout the show. This is completely optional so if you don’t want to take part, let them know. The front row tends to have more interaction, but no area is left out!

faq.question: Is there a dress code for Magic Mike Live?
faq.answer: There isn’t a smart dress code for the show, and you can wear comfortable clothes. Keep it sensible and don’t wear anything you wouldn’t wear to another theatre show.

faq.question: Are men allowed at Magic Mike?
faq.answer: Magic Mike Live is an inclusive show, and adults (aged 18+) of any gender are welcome to attend. While audiences are often female-heavy, men also come to watch the performers show off their incredible dance talents.

shows.name: Cats
shows.description_short: Regent’s Park artistic director Drew McOnie directs this brand new production of the iconic, award-winning Andrew Lloyd Webber musical.
shows.description_full:
Do you know how to go to the Heaviside Layer? Cats the Musical returns to London for a strictly limited run this summer, before embarking on a UK tour. This brand new staging of Andrew Lloyd Webber’s Tony and Olivier-winning musical about the rhymes and rituals of a group of mischievous cats comes to Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, just a short walk from Baker Street. Don’t miss this purrfect new revival of Cats - secure your ticket today.

Once a year, the slinking stray cats and rebellious dreamers of London gather under the full moon for the Jellicile ball - a special time where one particular puss will be chosen to be reborn. As the cats gather, they sing, dance, celebrate, and tell their "tails". There’s the elderly patriarch Old Deuteronomy, the acrobatic, trouble-making twins Mungojerrie and Rumpleteazer, the devilish Macavity the mystery cat, sweet and quiet white kitten Victoria, and the outcast Grizabella, each with their own story to tell. But who will be chosen to ascend to the Heaviside Layer?

Cats is based on T.S. Eliot’s children’s poetry book Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats. The musical premiered in May 1981, and was a critical success, holding the record for longest-running West End Musical until 2006, when it was surpassed by Les Misérables . The musical has won several Olivier and Tony awards, and has enjoyed many revivals, tours and adaptations internationally, including a film adaptation in 2019 which starred Idris Elba, Judi Dench, Ian McKellen and Jennifer Hudson. This brand new staging features all your favourite songs from the show, including “Memory”, “Jellicle Songs for Jellicle Cats”, “Magical Mr Mistoffelees” and "Skimbleshanks: The Railway Cat".

Who will go to the Jellicle ball? Casting includes:

Cats is directed and choreographed by Drew McOnie. McOnie is an Olivier-nominated choreographer and director. He is the current artistic director of the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, an Old Vic associate artist, and the artistic director of the McOnie dance company. His credits for Regent’s Park include Brigadoon, On the Town , Jesus Christ Superstar , and Carousel . Other credits include King Kong for the Broadway Theatre, Bugsy Malone for the Lyric Hammersmith and UK tour, and The Lorax for the Old Vic Theatre.

Lyrics are based on T.S. Eliot’s Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats. Music is by Andrew Lloyd Webber.

Sir Andrew Lloyd Webber is a British Composter. He is one of only twenty-one people in the world to hold an EGOT, which means he has won (multiple) Emmy, Tony, Oscar and Grammy awards. Lloyd Webber’s other accolades include Olivier, Ivor Novello and Brit Awards, and he also holds a Knighthood for his contributions to the arts. Lloyd Webber is a fellow of the British Academy of Songwriters, Composers, and Authors and an inductee into the Songwriters Hall of Fame. His best-known works include Sunset Boulevard, Starlight Express, Evita and Phantom of the Opera .

You’ll want to live the memories again and again this summer with this pawsome new production of Cats the Musical . Be dazzled by stunning choreography by Regent's Park Artistic Director Drew McOnie, along with all your favourite songs. The iconic Andrew Lloyd Webber musical comes to London for a strictly limited run this summer 2026, so don't miss out - secure your tickets for Cats at the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre now!

shows.name: The Story
shows.description_short: Letitia Wright leads this razor-sharp newsroom thriller about race and reporting - where chasing the truth can cost you everything.
shows.description_full:
Breaking news! The Story , a new play from award-winning playwright Tracey Scott Wilson, makes its UK premiere on the National Theatre’s Olivier Theatre stage, on scenic Bankside. The Story is “a riveting, tough-minded drama about race, reporting and the truth…” (Associated Press) that drops you into a high-pressure newsroom where every second counts and every angle has a price. This thrilling drama is directed by Death of England director Clint Dyer, and stars Black Panther’s Letitia Wright in her National Theatre debut. Secure your tickets for this limited run today.

In a fast-paced newsroom, the story is breaking, and the race to own it is on. When a white teacher is killed in a Black neighbourhood, reporters scramble for the headline that will define the day, and perhaps their careers. But as a controversial lead gathers momentum, an ambitious Black reporter pushes beyond the boundaries set by her editor, forcing the whole team into a pressure-cooker of competing agendas, rivalries and ethical fault lines.

What begins as a fight for a scoop becomes something far more combustible: a tug-of-war between truth and narrative, between what can be proved and what will be believed. The Story asks who gets to shape the public record, and what happens when the “right” version of events is the one that sells.

The Story debuted off-Broadway at The Public Theatre in 2003. This National Theatre production marks the show’s UK premiere, putting Wilson’s writing in conversation with today’s media climate and Britain’s own debates around race, power and public narratives.

Confirmed casting includes:

Further casting details are to be announced.

The Story is written by award-winning playwright Tracey Scott Wilson ( Buzzer, The Good Negro ). Clint Dyer ( Death of England, Othello ) directs. Set design is by Anna Fleischle ( The Lehman Trilogy, Yerma ), with costume design by Natalie Pryce ( Blues for an Alabama Sky, A Streetcar Named Desire ), Lighting design by Paul Anderson ( The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time, Barber Shop Chronicles ), Sound design by Benjamin Grant ( The Motive and the Cue, Leopoldstadt ), and movement direction by Lucie Pankhurst ( Small Island, Dear England ). Video design is by Gino Ricardo Green ( For Black Boys Who Have Considered Suicide… ) and Tal Yarden ( The Lehman Trilogy, Network ), with intimacy direction by Ingrid Mackinnon ( Jerusalem, The Doctor ).

Propulsive, punchy and unafraid to stare down the messy gap between the truth and the story, The Story is edge-of-your-seat drama full of big questions and even bigger stakes. With Marvel star Letitia Wright making her National Theatre debut, The Story is a “slick, smart and engrossing” (Variety) new play that’s sure to be headline news this season. This pulse-racing drama comes to London for a strictly limited run - book your tickets for The Story at The National Theatre's Olivier Theatre today.

shows.name: Into The Woods
shows.description_short: Olivier Award winner Jordan Fein directs this major revival of Sondheim's subversive fairytale musical at the Bridge Theatre.
shows.description_full:
Wishes do come true! Into the Woods comes to the Bridge Theatre at the foot of the iconic Tower Bridge this winter! A cast of storybook characters go hunting for their happily ever after in Sondheim and Lapine’s spellbinding musical, directed by red-hot director of the moment Jordan Fein. Bridge Theatre co-founder Nicholas Hynter described Into The Woods as “one of the great achievements of the American stage” (What’sOnStage) and you won't want to miss this major revival of an iconic show - so find your happy ever after and book now!

“I wish! More than anything”

The Baker and his Wife desperately want a child, Little Red Riding Hood wants to visit her sick grandma, Cinderella wants to attend the ball and Jack (of ‘and the Beanstalk’ fame) wants to save his beloved cow, Milky White, from being taken to market. The cast of fairytale characters venture into the deep dark woods, searching for the solution to their troubles, but when they run afoul of a Wicked Witch, their stories get entangled and new worries emerge.

Can the Witch's curse be broken? Will everyone make it “home before dark”? And what happens after happily ever after?

Full of princes, giants and big bad wolves, heartbreak, hope and magic quests, Into the Woods has become a true staple of musical theatre, and features well-known songs including “First Midnight”, “Agony”, “No One is Alone” and “Giants In the Sky”.

The musical first premiered in 1986 and turned the world of Grimm’s fairytales on its head. Subversive and enchanting, Into the Woods was a smash hit, earning ten nominations, and snatching three trophies, at the 1988 Tony Awards. Into the Woods has had many award-winning revivals and adaptations, including a Disney film starring Meryl Streep, Anna Kendrick and Chris Pine, which was nominated for three Academy Awards and three Golden Globes. This production at the Bridge marks the first major London revival of the musical in almost a decade, so don’t experience the “agony” of missing out - book now!

Main Cast:

Into the Woods at the Bridge Theatre is directed by Olivier Award-winner Jordan Fein, who helmed the five-star sell-out revival of Fiddler on the Roof at the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre in summer 2024, also transferring to the Barbican this spring. Fein’s other credits include Oklahoma! at the Young Vic, Dark Disabled Stories at The Public Theater & The Bushwick Starr and the prologue to Cabaret at the Playhouse. On Into the Woods Fein is reunited with Tony Award-winning designer Tom Scutt, who also worked on Fiddler , with other credits including Little Shop of Horrors at Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, Ben Platt's Radio City Music Hall Concert for Netflix and A Very Expensive Poison at The Old Vic.

Into the Woods has a book by award-winning playwright and librettist James Lapine ( Falsettos, Passion, Little Miss Sunshine ) and music and lyrics by the late great Stephen Sondheim ( Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street, West Side Story, Gypsy ). Lapine and Sondheim previously collaborated on Sunday in the Park with George .

Do you know what’s out there in the woods? Find out and join the fairytale with tickets to this dazzling musical. Into the Woods at the Bridge Theatre has a strictly limited run, so beg, steal or barter your magic beans to get a ticket - or better yet, book today!

shows.name: The Oresteia
shows.description_short: Simon Stone's major modern, visceral production of the Greek tragedy plays at the Bridge Theatre this summer for a limited season.
shows.typical_age_range: Initial age guidance is 15+. This may change as the production develops.
shows.description_full:
The ancient text of Aeschylus’s The Oresteia comes to the Bridge Theatre in a radical reimagining by the acclaimed Australian theatre, opera and film director Simon Stone. Situated near Tower Bridge, this play will be significantly and cathartically relevant for audiences heading to experience this classic work, which will be transformed into something new this summer.

This new production of The Oresteia at the Bridge Theatre follows a family haunted by their role in an unjust war. As the painful burden of inherited trauma and inter-generational conflict becomes more intense, the family spirals into a powerful vortex of violence.

Based on a trilogy of Greek tragedies written by Aeschylus in the 5th century BC, The Oresteia has been adapted multiple times into operas and plays, including a rap and hip hop-inspired show by French rapper D’ de Kabal in 2014.

The Oresteia was first staged in London in 1981 by Sir Peter Hall, who directed Tony Harrison’s adaptation at the National Theatre. A subsequent revival at the same theatre took place in 1999. The most notable production was by Robert Icke in 2015 at the Almeida Theatre, where it was a sold-out hit and was transferred in the same year to the Trafalgar Theatre. At this time, there was also another production of it playing at Shakespeare’s Globe in the same year.

The Oresteia will be written and directed by Simon Stone. An Australian director and writer, he has worked on productions across the world. His London debut was in 2016 when he directed a new adaptation of Euripides’ Medea at the Young Vic and starred Billie Piper in the title role. Other credits on the stage include Phaedra, Innocence, and the critically acclaimed production of The Lady from the Sea at the Bridge Theatre in 2025.

This includes set designer Lizzie Clachan, costume designer Mel Page, music by Stefan Gregory, lighting designer Nick Schlieper, and casting director Jessica Ronane CDG.

T he Oresteia will take a timeless story and transform it into a modern and visceral play for modern audiences. If you want to experience groundbreaking work from one of the best theatre directors of today, make sure you don’t miss out on a visit to the Bridge Theatre this summer.

shows.name: The Car Man
shows.description_short: Matthew Bourne's sizzling take on Bizet's Carmen, set in 1960s small-town USA, returns to London.
shows.description_full:
Buckle up at Sadler’s Wells, in the heart of Islington, as Matthew Bourne’s multi-award-winning The Car Man returns for a strictly limited run. Based on Bizet’s Carmen, The Car Man is a dance-thriller spectacular which transports the classic opera’s story to a grease-slicked garage and diner in small town USA. This red-hot dance show is “sizzling and fizzing with lust” (Telegraph), and makes for unmissable theatre - secure your tickets today.

In a greasy 1960s diner, one small town’s dreams are torn apart when a handsome stranger arrives, and the townspeople soon descend into a frenzied spiral of greed, desire, backstabbing and revenge.

Matthew Bourne’s The Car Man premiered in 2000 at Plymouth’s Theatre Royal and is one of his most distinctive and provocative dance-theatre productions. Loosely inspired by Bizet’s Carmen and drawing narrative influence from James M. Cain’s novel The Postman Always Rings Twice , it reimagines the action of the sultry opera in small-town America. With a moody adapted score, the show blends passion, betrayal, and murder in a gripping noir-style tale. Over the years, it has been revived multiple times, including a major production in 2015 and a film version released by Sky Arts in 2022. In 2000, the show won the Evening Standard Award for best “Musical Event” and was nominated for a Laurence Olivier Award.

The Car Man is performed by the company of New Adventures. Direction and choreography are by acclaimed choreographer and New Adventures founder Matthew Bourne ( Swan Lake, The Red Shoes ), with a dramatic, cinematic score adapted and arranged by Terry Davies ( Frankenstein, The Wind in the Willows ) based on Bizet’s Carmen . The production also features set and costume design by Lez Brotherston ( Sleeping Beauty, The Red Shoes ), lighting by Chris Davey ( Nutcracker!, Romeo and Juliet ), and sound design by Paul Groothuis ( Oliver!, Follies ).

This incredible production is Bizet’s Carmen as you’ve never seen it before. Allow yourself to be swept into the world of The Car Man , where romance and revenge collide. This award-winning show from dance titan Matthew Bourne is “gripping, gasp-making, edge of your seat stuff” (Daily Mail) and returns for a strictly limited run - don’t miss out, book your tickets for The Car Man at Sadler’s Wells today.

shows.name: An Ideal Husband
shows.description_short: Political, public and private personas are all at odds in this bold new staging of Wilde’s witty, enduringly relevant comic melodrama.
shows.description_full:
This vibrant new production of Oscar Wilde’s enduring and hilarious play, An Ideal Husband , comes to the Lyric Hammersmith in West London. This comedic melodrama about reputation and blackmail is as relevant as ever, and this brand new production by Lyric Associate Nicholai La Barrie showcases Wilde’s witty word-play and exploration of the dark underbelly of politics and public personas in a bold new staging. Secure your tickets today!

“Questions are never indiscreet. Answers sometimes are.”

Politician Sir Robert Chiltern is on the rise. A man of impeccable taste, he appears to have it all - a loving wife, magnificent clothes, a wonderful house and most importantly, a spotless reputation. That is, until the meddling Lady Cheverley enters the scene. An old school rival of Robert’s wife, and the widow of one of Robert’s old colleagues, Lady Cheverley has a long-held grudge, a flair for the dramatic and a whole load of secrets ready to spill. With the threat of scandal on the horizon, just how far will Robert go to keep his reputation unblemished?

An Ideal Husband first premiered in 1895 at the Theatre Royal Haymarket. The show was critically acclaimed and continues to be popular today. It has since received many revivals and adaptations, with notable productions including the 2018 Vaudeville Theatre staging starring Father-son duo Edward and Freddie Fox, and the 1999 film adaptation starring Julianne Moore, Cate Blanchett and Rupert Everett.

Casting for An Ideal Husband includes:

The Lyric’s Associate Director Nicholai La Barrie directs. La Barrie is a British writer, actor and director. Previously, La Barrie was a MOBO fellow and a resident director on Tina - The Tina Turner Musical . His credits include Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York) in the West End, Romeo and Juliet at the Royal Exchange, Manchester, and God of Carnage at the Lyric Hammersmith.

An Ideal Husband is written by Oscar Wilde (1854 -1900). Wilde was an Irish author, poet, and playwright, often regarded as a key figure in the aestheticism movement. Wilde is widely considered to be one of the most well-known and greatest writers of the Victorian Era, best known for his witty word-play and satirical epigrams. Wilde’s most famous works include his plays The Importance of Being Earnest, Salome, Lady Windermere's Fan and A Woman of No Importance , and his Gothic horror novel The Picture of Dorian Gray .

Corruption, blackmail and societal expectations collide in this exhilarating new staging of one of Wilde’s wittiest and most beloved plays. Don’t miss this fresh new take on the classic comedy of manners from one of London’s hottest up-and-coming directors. Secure your tickets for An Ideal Husband at the Lyric Hammersmith today!
