shows.name: Trainspotting the Musical
shows.description_short: This brand new musical adapts the generation-defining novel by Irvine Welsh, featuring electrifying hits from the film and original songs.
shows.description_full:
Trainspotting the Musical makes its world premiere this July at the Theatre Royal Haymarket, just a short walk from Piccadilly Circus. This brand new musical adapts Irvine Welsh’s novel, about a group of friends struggling with addiction in 1990s Edinburgh. The musical has a book by Welsh himself, and features original songs co-written by Welsh and dance legend Steve Mac, along with iconic tracks from the 1996 hit film adaptation, which starred Ewan McGregor. Trainspotting comes to London for a strictly limited run this summer - so choose life, choose tickets, and book today.

Leith, Edinburgh, in the 1990s: cynical Renton and his friends Sick Boy, Begbie, Spud, Tommy, and Kelly are trying to choose life - but life doesn't seem to want to choose them. Struggling with heroin addiction and economic deprivation, the gang faces the trials and tribulations of getting clean, love, sex and relationships, and earning some cash, as they try to find their place in a society that has pegged them as outcasts.

Darkly funny, life-affirming, and deeply nostalgic, this genre-defying musical is Trainspotting as you’ve never seen it before, featuring your favourite tunes from the film alongside brand-new songs.

Trainspotting the Musica l is based on the book, and its film adaptation, of the same name. Published in 1993, Trainspotting was the debut novel by Scottish author Irvine Welsh, written in Scots and Scottish English. Trainspotting quickly became a cult hit and was shortlisted for the 1993 Booker Prize. Three sequel novels and a prequel have also been released.

A film adaptation was made in 1996, directed by Danny Boyle, which starred Ewan McGregor, Johnny Lee Miller and Robert Carlise. The film was a critical and commercial success; the highest-grossing film of the year, Trainspotting earned an Academy Award nomination and won BAFTAs for Best Adapted Screenplay and Best Actor. Often considered to be one of the top films of the 90s, in 2004 Trainspotting was voted the best Scottish film of all time in a public poll, and was ranked tenth in the BFI’s list of the Top 100 British films of the 20th century. A sequel, T2 Trainspotting , was released in 2017.

The novel has previously been adapted for the stage both as a play in the mid-90s and as an immersive theatre show, Trainspotting Live , in 2013, which was also a critical success.

Confirmed casting includes:

Trainspotting the Musical is written by Irvine Welsh, adapted from his own novel of the same name. Music and Lyrics are co-written by Welsh and dance music pioneer Stephen McGuinness, aka Steve Mac (remixes on work by artists including Michael Jackson, Barry White, Amy Winehouse, INXS). The show is directed and developed by Caroline Jay Ranger ( Only Fools and Horses the musical, Fawlty Towers Live ).

Welsh is a Scottish author, screenwriter and playwright, best known for his novels, frequently written in an Edinburgh dialect Scots and set in his native Scotland, which depict themes of working-class life, drug addiction, and social alienation. Notable works include Trainspotting sequels/prequels Porno and Skagboys , as well as his novels and short story collections Glue , The Acid House , and Filth , which was also adapted into a film starring James McAvoy.

Thirty years on, the story that defined a generation returns as you’ve never seen it before. You won't want to miss this defiant, provocative and truly unforgettable new musical as it makes its world premiere in London before embarking on a nationwide tour. So join the outsiders, feel the rush, and choose life - book your tickets for Trainspotting the Musical at the Theatre Royal Haymarket today.

shows.name: Bill Bailey: Vaudevillean
shows.description_short: Strictly winner and oddball comedian Bill Bailey returns to the West End with a musical comedy show celebrating the golden age of variety.
shows.description_full:
Comedy legend Bill Bailey returns to the West End with his brand-new live show Bill Bailey: Vaudevillean at the Theatre Royal Haymarket, just off vibrant Picadilly. This limited stand-up musical comedy residency brings Bailey’s unique blend of musical virtuosity, surreal humour and storytelling to one of London’s historic theatres. Inspired by the rich tradition of vaudeville entertainment, the show celebrates the performers who once dominated pre-radio and pre-television stages with comedy, music and speciality acts - with a typically modern Bill Bailey twist. The run follows his previous sold-out engagement at the venue with Thoughtifier and arrives after a major UK arena tour of the same show.

Before streaming, before television and even before radio, vaudeville was the prime-time entertainment of its day, with performers combining comedy, music and eccentric speciality acts in lively variety shows that captivated audiences across theatres and music halls.

In Vaudevillean , Bill Bailey revisits that vibrant tradition, blending stand-up, original music and offbeat observations about modern life. Bailey, a multi-instrumentalist known for his ability to move effortlessly between instruments and musical styles, mixes witty storytelling with playful musical interludes and unexpected cultural references in this wonderfully wacky celebration of the art form.

Bill Bailey began performing stand-up in the 1990s, quickly gaining recognition for his imaginative mixture of music and comedy. His early solo show Bill Bailey’s Cosmic Jam helped establish his distinctive style and led to a television special broadcast on Channel 4.

Over the decades, he has become a regular presence on British television, while his live tours consistently sell out across the UK and internationally. Bailey’s shows often combine philosophical musings, cultural satire and impressive musicianship, making him one of the most distinctive performers in contemporary comedy. With Vaudevillean, Bailey continues that tradition while paying homage to the theatrical variety performers who shaped modern entertainment. The show will tour the UK before landing in London’s West End for a limited run.

Vaudevillean is written and performed by Bill Bailey ( Black Books, Never Mind the Buzzcocks ). One of the UK’s most distinctive comedians, Bailey is celebrated for blending stand-up with musical performance. His television appearances include Black Books, QI and Have I Got News for You , and he won BBCs Strictly Come Dancing in 2020.

Blending stand-up comedy, off-the-wall musical performances and affectionate nods to classic variety entertainment, this hilarious show is a lively celebration of performance in all its forms as Bill Bailey’s distinctive style brings the spirit of old-school vaudeville into the modern West End. This witty and unpredictable show from one of Britain’s most beloved and original comedians comes to London for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets for Bill Bailey: Vaudevillean at the Theaytre Royal Haymarket today!

shows.name: Glengarry Glen Ross
shows.description_short: This revival of Mamet’s explosive, Pulitzer-winning play, about four real-estate agents competing for sales, features an all-female cast.
shows.description_full:
A major revival of Pulitzer Prize-winning play Glengarry Glen Ross comes to the Old Vic Theatre, just by Waterloo, this summer. David Mamet's blistering modern classic delves into the corrupt, capitalistic world of four ruthless real estate agents, who are willing to do anything to make a sale, living by the mantra “Always be closing!”. Directed by Tony winner Patrick Marber, this production marks the play's first major revival in London in almost a decade, and changes the game with an all-female cast. You won't want to miss it!

The unseen owners of a Chicago real estate company declare a sales contest: the highest-earning seller will snag the grand prize, a Cadillac, and the glory, while second place will earn a set of steak knives - but the two worst performers will be fired effective immediately. Over the course of two days, four corrupt estate agents battle it out to be top dog, willing to do whatever it takes to win.

When newcomer and upstart, the ruthless Ricky Roma, takes the lead, the other agents - fallen-from-grace former top earner Shelley Levene, timid and unassuming George Aaronow and the loudmouthed, envious Moss - scramble to take the top spot. But when a robbery at the agency adds extra pressure, the office soon becomes a boiling pot of backstabbing, betrayal and bribery.

The exciting all-female cast for this revival includes:

Glengarry Glen Ross premiered at London’s National Theatre in 1983. The show was a critical success, and opened on Broadway the following year, earning four Tony nominations, a Drama Desk Award and the Pulitzer Prize for drama. A film adaptation with an expanded script by Mamet was made in 1992, starring Al Pacino, Jack Lemmon and Alec Baldwin, which was nominated for an Academy Award and a Golden Globe. The show has been revived countless times over the years, most recently in a 2025 Tony-nominated production starring Succession's Kieran Culkin, but this brand new Old Vic production flips the script on its head, featuring an all-female cast for the very first time.

Glengarry Glen Ross is written by Pulitzer and Tony-winning playwright David Mamet. Renowned for his scintillating, snappily realist dialogue and sometimes controversial characters, Mamet’s best-known works include Oleanna, Speed-The-Plough, Bitter Wheat and Race . Mamet is a founding member of the acclaimed Atlanta Theatre Company and an inductee of the American Theatre Hall of Fame.

Patrick Marber directs. Marber is a playwright and director, whose credits include Leopoldstadt, Don Juan in Soho, Travesties and The Producers . Marber also directed the recent Broadway revival of Glengarry Glen Ross in 2025. Marber’s many accolades for his writing and directing work include Tony, Olivier and BAFTA awards.

The Old Vic makes history with this all-female premiere of one of the great American modern classics. With an explosive script, an award-winning creative team, and a boundary-breaking cast, Glengarry Glen Ross is not to be missed. So make sure you close on this deal - book tickets for Glengarry Glen Ross at The Old Vic Theatre today!

shows.name: Burlesque The Musical
shows.description_short: Be seduced by this sizzling musical about a small-town girl who finds her voice at a glitzy Burlesque bar, based on the campy 2010 film.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 12+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Don your feather boa and grab the glitter as Burlesque The Musical slinks back into London! Based on the campy cult favourite movie about a small-town girl with a big voice, this musical thoroughly seduced audiences in 2025 and returns to the brand new, purpose-built Arts at Marble Arch Theatre, just a short walk from Hyde Park, for an encore this September. Burlesque is a “dazzling triumph” (Matinee Radio) and has a strictly limited run at the Savoy Theatre, so snap up your tickets today!

Aspiring singer Ali is “too much” for her quiet midwestern town, where she spends her days waitressing in her late mother's cafe. When Ali discovers she was adopted as a child, she leaves her humdrum home for the bright lights of New York City, hoping to find her long-lost birth mother. Drawn into the dazzling underworld of music, dance and seduction at a historic burlesque club, Ali not only finds her mother, Tess, who owns the club; she ends up finding herself, too. Amongst the misfits and dreamers of the club, Ali finally feels like she belongs, and sparks fly when she meets kindhearted barman Jackson. But when Tess’s vindictive ex Vince threatens to close down the club, Ali must fight for her new home. Will she be able to raise her voice to save Burlesque?

Gritty, glitzy and glamorous, Burlesque The Musical is a sizzling story of self-discovery, based on the hit 2010 film, which starred Cher and Christina Aguilera. This fresh new adaptation features all your favourite songs from the movie, with tunes by Christina Aguilera, Sia and Diane Warren, alongside new songs by Todrick Hall and Jess Folley. Burlesque is “big, in-your-face, blockbusting entertainment" (Reviews Hub) - book for this raunchy, riotous romp today!

The musical premiered at the Manchester Opera House in 2024 before transferring to the Theatre Royal, Glasgow. The show transferred to London’s Savoy Theatre in summer 2025, playing to sold-out audiences and earning a WhatsOnStage Award. The show now returns to the custom-built Arts Theatre at Marble Arch, and is the first production to open the semi-permanent venue.

Casting is to be announced for the 2026 production of Burlesque The Musical.

The film’s creator Steve Antin adapts his own work for the stage, writing the book for Burlesque , with additional material by Kate Wetherhead ( The Devil Wears Prada, Legally Blonde ). Antin’s previous credits include Gloria, Proud Mary, and Chasing Papi .

Music and Lyrics are by Christina Aguilera (who is also an executive producer for the show), Sia and Diane Warren, with additional songs by Jess Folley ( Les Miserables, X Factor – The Band) and Todrick Hall ( Midnight, Kinky Boots ).

So “Wagon Wheel Watusi” on down to Marble Arch and put some sparkle into your life with tickets to Burlesque The Musical at the Arts Theatre at Marble Arch. “ Burlesque is a musical that really does have it all” (Broadway World) - so what are you waiting for? Book this sexy, sassy and sequin-filled show today!

shows.name: Teeth 'N' Smiles
shows.description_short: Rebbeca Lucy Taylor, aka Self Esteem, stars in this explosive revival of David Hare's anarchic play about a failing rock’n’roll band.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 14+. Under 16s must be seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Grab your tickets for the hottest gig in town, as Rebecca Taylor aka Self Esteem returns to the West End this Spring. Taylor stars as Maggie in Teeth 'N' Smiles , a major revival of the classic David Hare musical play, about a rockstar trying to keep her band together at a tough gig, staged in celebration of the show's 50th anniversary. Teeth 'N' Smiles comes to the Duke of York's Theatre, in the heart of Leicester Square, this spring for a limited time only. Don't miss out - book your tickets today!

It’s 1969, and in a dilapidated basement bar at Jesus College, Cambridge, a rock and roll band prepares to play for a university ball.

While her bandmates may be skint, disillusioned, and feel they’ve sold out by performing at the stuffy university, all lead singer Maggie wants to do is perform… and drink. But tensions are high as Maggie quarrels with both the band’s controlling manager, Saffian and her ex-lover, songwriter Arthur, who is grappling with his lingering feelings for Maggie, as well as a blooming attraction to Laura, the band’s new assistant. As the band’s dreams of stardom slowly collapse, Maggie is determined to go on with the show. Fuelled by alcohol, rage and rock’n’roll, as the night draws on, Maggie struggles to keep her grip on the gig, and on reality…

Teeth 'N' Smiles first premiered at The Royal Court Theatre in 1975, starring Helen Mirren as Maggie. A subversive, rock’n’roll smash hit, during the show’s initial run at the Royal Court, The Who drummer Keith Moon arrived intoxicated at the stage door to lavish praise on Mirren - before trying to join the cast on stage, and being stopped by the theatre’s management!

This anarchic, trailblazing play about counterculture and artistic integrity returns to London 50 years on, in a landmark new production with updated music from Self Esteem.

Rebecca Lucy Taylor stars as Maggie.

Taylor is best known for her work as a musician and songwriter under the stage name Self Esteem. Formerly part of indie duo Slow Club, Taylor began performing solo in 2019 and has since released three critically acclaimed, best-selling albums, winning an Ivor Novello and Attitude Award and receiving nominations for the Mercury, NME and Brit Awards. Taylor also composed the music for Olivier-winning play Prima Facie , starring Jodie Comer.

Taylor is also an accomplished actress, with credits including roles on I Hate Suzie with Billy Piper, in SKY TV series Smothered, and as Sally Bowles in Cabaret at the Kit Kat Club opposite Jake Shears. In early 2025, Taylor also performed in a staged version of her album, A Complicated Woman , at the Duke of York’s Theatre, which she also devised.

Further casting includes:

Teeth 'N' Smiles is written by David Hare, with music and lyrics by Nick Bicât and Tony Bicât and additional new music and lyrics by Self Esteem. Daniel Raggett directs.

Daniel Raggett is an Evening Standard Theatre Award-nominated film and theatre director. His credits include Brace Brace at the Royal Court, The Vortex at Chichester Festival Theatre, Anna X at the Harold Pinter Theatre, an Olivier-nominated production of Accidental Death of an Anarchist at the Theatre Royal Haymarket and Sheffield Theatres, and short film Hermit , starring Emma Corin. Ragett has also worked as an associate director to Ivo Van Hove on several productions, including Network at the National, Mary Stuart in the West End, and West Side Story on Broadway.

Sir David Hare is an award-winning playwright, screenwriter and director. He is an Olivier and BAFTA winner, and a Tony, Golden Globe, and Academy Award nominee. His most notable works include feature films The Hours , starring Meryl Streep and Julianne Moore, and The Reader , starring Kate Winslet and Ralph Fiennes, and plays My Zinc Bed, Skylight, The Vertical Hour and Straight Line Crazy .

Teeth 'N' Smiles was a trailblazing play upon its premiere and now celebrates its 50th anniversary with this bold, raucous and explosive new production featuring rock’n’roll star Rebecca Lucy Taylor, aka Self Esteem. You won't want to miss it - so what are you waiting for? Get the band back together and book for Teeth 'N' Smiles at the Duke of York Theatre today!

shows.name: Death Note: The Musical
shows.description_short: This musical adaptation of the cult manga is a thrilling game of cat and mouse between an eccentric detective and an otherworldly killer.
shows.description_full:
Death Note: The Musical makes its UK premiere at the Barbican Theatre, just a short walk from Moorgate, this summer. Based on the bestselling Japanese manga, this stage adaptation transforms the cult phenomenon psychological thriller into a high-intensity musical. When high school student Light discovers an otherworldly notebook that will kill anyone whose name is written in its pages, he sets about ridding the world of evildoers - until quirky detective L enters the scene, seeking to discover who exactly is behind the murders. What ensues is a tense game of cat and mouse, in this “deliciously dark masterpiece” (Stage2Page), coming to London for a strictly limited run.

When brilliant but disillusioned student Light Yagami discovers a mysterious notebook that grants him the power to kill anyone whose name he writes in it, his world changes instantly. The notebook belongs to Ryuk, a bored Shinigami, or death god, who drops it into the human world for his own amusement.

Determined to rid society of criminals, Light adopts the alias “Kira” and begins delivering his own twisted idea of justice, until his actions attract the attention of an enigmatic and eccentric detective known only as L. As the two engage in a tense battle of intellect and morality, the line between hero and villain swiftly blurs.

Blending supernatural elements with a gripping psychological duel, Death Note: The Musical explores power, corruption and the consequences of absolute justice, set to a stirring, contemporary score.

Death Note began as a manga series serialised between 2003 and 2006, quickly becoming a worldwide success. It has since inspired an equally acclaimed anime adaptation, live-action films, novels and video games, cementing its place as a modern pop culture phenomenon.

The musical adaptation premiered in Tokyo in 2015 and has since been staged in South Korea and in concert versions internationally, including two limited-run, sell-out concert stagings at the London Palladium and Lyric Theatre in 2023. The full production now makes its UK premiere at the Barbican Theatre, continuing the story’s global journey from page to stage.

Whose name will be written in the Death Note? Casting for this production is to be announced.

Death Note: The Musical features a book by Ivan Menchell ( Bonnie & Clyde, The Cemetery Club ), with music by Frank Wildhorn ( Jekyll & Hyde, The Scarlet Pimpernel ) and lyrics by Jack Murphy ( Wonderland, The Civil War ). The original manga was written by Tsugumi Ohba and illustrated by Takeshi Obata.

Further creative team members are to be confirmed.

Death Note is a “unique and exquisite musical” (West End Best Friend); you won't want to miss this cult phenomenon, fully realised in London for the very first time. Filled with moral complexity and an electric score, Death Note: The Musical is “to die for” (All That Dazzles). The battle of wits between Light and L will have you on the edge of your seat, and is sure to be in high demand, as the previous concert stagings sold out. Secure your tickets to Death Note: The Musical at the Barbican Theatre today.

shows.name: Billy Elliot
shows.description_short: A boy in a mining town pursues his dream of becoming a ballet dancer in this Olivier and Tony-winning musical with songs by Elton John.
shows.description_full:
Olivier and Tony Award-winning musical Billy Elliot leaps back into the West End at the Adelphi Theatre, on the Strand. Based on the beloved film about a young boy from a working class North East town, who goes against his masculine mining community to pursue a love of ballet, this “triumphant” (Daily Telegraph) musical features songs by legendary songwriter Elton John. Billy Elliot returns to London for a strictly limited run from February 2027 - so go on, Billy, book your tickets today!

Set in a North East mining town during the 1984-85 miners’ strike, Billy Elliot follows eleven-year-old Billy, who stumbles into a ballet class on his way to boxing lessons. Captivated by dance, Billy discovers a passion that sets him apart from expectations placed upon him by his family and community.

As tensions rise in the town, Billy secretly pursues his dream, guided by his formidable dance teacher, Mrs Wilkinson. Against a backdrop of political unrest, Billy’s journey becomes a moving story about self-expression and the courage to challenge stereotypes. Billy Elliot balances intimate family drama with large-scale ensemble numbers exploring themes of identity, resilience and ambition, with an unforgettable score, including “Electricity”, “Expressing Yourself” “Merry Christmas Maggie Thatcher” and “The Letter”.

Billy Elliot began life as a film written by Lee Hall and directed by Stephen Daldry, which was released in 2000 and received critical acclaim for its portrayal of working-class Britain and childhood aspiration. Its success led to the musical adaptation, which premiered at London’s Victoria Palace Theatre in 2005.

The musical went on to win 10 Olivier Awards, including Best New Musical, and later earned 10 Tony Awards following its Broadway transfer. Over its original West End run, from 2005–2016, the production became one of London’s longest-running musicals and launched the careers of numerous young performers in the title role. Known for its demanding choreography and emotionally charged storytelling, Billy Elliot has been performed internationally and remains a defining British musical of the modern era. This huge revival starts out in the North East - at the Sunderland Empire - and embarks on a landmark UK tour before pirouetting its way into the West End for a strictly limited run.

Casting for this production is to be announced.

Billy Elliot features music by legendary EGOT singer-songwriter Elton John ( The Lion King, Aida ) and a book and lyrics by Lee Hall ( Rocketman, Spoonface Steinberg ), the screenwriter of the film. The original production was directed by the film's director Stephen Daldry ( The Hours, The Crown ) with choreography by Peter Darling ( Matilda the Musical, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory ).

Further creative team members for this revival are to be confirmed.

Billy Elliot is “pure magic” (Sunday Express), so feel the electricity and get yourself down to the Adelphi Theatre in 2027 for a moving, uplifting musical with songs by the iconic Elton John. Book your tickets for Billy Elliot at the Adelphi Theatre today - it’ll be proper canny!

shows.name: La Fille mal gardee
shows.description_short: A young woman in love must outwit her mother’s plans in Frederick Ashton’s beloved pastoral comic ballet, full of humour and charm.
shows.description_full:
Take a trip to the countryside with La fille mal gardee at the Royal Opera House, in the heart of Covent Garden. Frederick Ashton’s beloved ballet, complete with mischievous antics, maypole dances and a cheerful score, is ideal for both families and long‑time ballet lovers and returns to London for a strictly limited run this spring.

Set in a rural village, the story follows Lise, the spirited daughter of Widow Simone, who is in love with the young farmer Colas. Simone, however, has other plans and hopes to marry Lise to the foolish Alain, son of a prosperous landowner. Through secret meetings and clever schemes, Lise and Colas work to outwit Simone’s plans and secure their own happy ending. A rural tale of love and family meddling, the ballet combines classical technique with bucolic humour, incorporating folk‑dance elements such as maypole and clog dances alongside vivacious ensemble work.

La fille mal gardee (French for The Wayward Daughter ) is a ballet in two acts choreographed by Sir Frederick Ashton for The Royal Ballet and first staged at the Royal Opera House in 1960. Ashton’s version revived the 18th‑century pastoral comedy originally created by Jean Dauberval and inspired by a 1765 painting by Pierre-Antoine Baudouin. Ashton’s version has since become a signature comedy in the Royal Ballet’s repertory.

The score, rooted in Ferdinand Hérold’s original 1828 music and fashioned into a new orchestral tapestry by Lanchbery, supports Ashton’s light‑hearted choreography, balancing lyrical tenderness with sprightly antics. Highlights often include the pas de ruban, Widow Simone’s clog dance and the maypole sequence, which together reflect the ballet’s pastoral charm. This production’s colourful design and inventive choreography have made La fille mal gardee a favourite not just in London but with ballet companies worldwide, celebrated for its blend of wit, grace and narrative clarity.

Casting varies by performance date, but principal dancers set to appear include:

La fille mal gardee is choreographed by Sir Frederick Ashton ( The Dream, Cinderella, Symphonic Variations ), with music by Ferdinand Hérold, arranged and orchestrated by John Lanchbery ( The Tales of Beatrix Potter, Coppélia ). The scenario is based on the original ballet by Jean Dauberval ( Psyché et l'Amour ).

Design is by Osbert Lancaster ( Coppélia, Pineapple Poll ), and lighting is by John B. Read ( Song of the Earth, Manon ).

Sir Frederick Ashton’s feel‑good ballet pairs humour with classical virtuosity as this acclaimed production returns to its Covent Garden home. With its memorable choreography, delightful costumes and engaging story, La fille mal gardee is an ideal introduction to ballet or a rewarding return for seasoned fans. Book your tickets for La fille mal gardee at the Royal Opera House today for a summer of sunshine, wit and romance.

shows.name: Dark of the Moon
shows.description_short: A supernatural folk musical about love, prejudice and a dangerous bargain between a human girl and a witch-boy in the Appalachian Mountains.
shows.description_full:
A haunting tale of love and prejudice comes to life in Dark of the Moon , playing at the Charing Cross Theatre, just off the Strand. Based on a 1940s play about a witch-boy and a human who fall in love, this new musical reimagines a classic Appalachian folktale through a distinctive score, blending country, bluegrass and rock’n’roll. This production comes to London for a strictly limited run - secure your tickets today!

In a quiet Appalachian town, superstition and tradition shape daily life. High in the nearby Smoky Mountains, however, another world exists - a mysterious realm inhabited by witches and warlocks whose powers influence the land below. When John, a curious witch-boy, falls in love with Barbara, a human girl from the town, their relationship challenges the boundaries between these two worlds. Determined to live among humans, John makes a dangerous bargain that allows him to become mortal, but only if Barbara remains faithful. As suspicion spreads through the town and tensions rise between the earthly and supernatural communities, the couple’s love is tested by prejudice, fear and the consequences of the pact made under the dark of the moon. Blending folklore with emotional storytelling, this new musical explores whether love can survive when two opposing worlds collide.

Dark of the Moon is based on the play of the same name by Howard Richardson and William Berney, which was in turn inspired by the traditional folk ballad “Barbara Allen”, a centuries-old song that appears in British and American folk traditions. Richardson and Berney’s drama transformed the ballad into a supernatural tale set in the Appalachian Mountains, blending folklore, romance, and social commentary. Premiering on Broadway in 1945 and in London in 1948, the play became known for combining fantasy and realism and exploring themes of prejudice within a small community. Despite the story’s rich folklore and atmospheric setting, the play is rarely revived - although it does feature as a 'play within a play' in Stranger Things: The First Shadow .

While the original play does include songs, this musical version expands the story through a score influenced by Appalachian musical traditions, bringing the tension between the human and supernatural worlds to life through music as well as drama. The musical had its developmental debut at the Rubicon Theatre in California in 2023, and now makes its UK premiere at The Charing Cross Theatre for a strictly limited run.

Casting for this production is to be confirmed.

Dark of the Moon features a book by Emmy nominee Jonathan Prince ( American Dreams, Melrose Place ), with music and lyrics by Grammy winning song writers Lindy Robbins ( Birds of Prey, Descendants ), Dave Bassett (Elle King’s “Ex’s & Oh’s”, Vance Joy’s “Riptide”) and Steve Robson (James Blunt’s “You’re Beautiful”, One Direction’s “Story of My Life”). The musical is based on the play by Howard D. Richardson and William Berney.

The production is directed by Georgie Rankcom ( Ride, Anyone Can Whistle ), with choreography by Jane McMurtrie ( Lizzie, Spring Awakening ). Brad Haak ( Come From Away, School of Rock ) serves as Musical Supervisor, with Matt Herbert ( Matilda the Musical, Heathers ) as Music Director and orchestrations by Dillon Kondor ( Six, Be More Chill ).

Production design is by Libby Todd ( Ride, The Secret Diary of Adrian Mole Aged 13¾ ), with lighting design by Jonathan Chan ( Ride, The Time Traveller’s Wife ) and sound design by Andrew Johnson ( Heathers, Jerry Springer: The Opera ).

Dark of the Moon blends romance, folklore and music with supernatural drama and a sultry, Appalachian-inspired soundtrack. You won't want to miss this atmospheric new musical from an award-winning creative team. Let yourself be spellbound - secure your tickets for Dark of the Moon at the Charing Cross Theatre today.

shows.description_short: Get ready to learn... Blippi is back in London with another interactive live show for families! Best for age 2+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 2+. Under 16s must be accompanied by an adult.
shows.description_full:
A cherished children’s character worldwide, Blippi is back with a brand-new interactive show in 2026! Playing at the West End’s newest venue, The Arts at Marble Arch, located close to Hyde Park, experience the ultimate educational adventure this summer! Grab your orange glasses and embrace your inner explorer with Be More Blippi , jam-packed with high-energy music and plenty of surprises for children aged 2+. Secure your tickets today!

Blippi and Meekah invite families to bring their children to explore the world around them more curiously with this brand-new show. Step inside an interactive adventure which features some surprises alongside fan-favourite songs like “Monster Truck” and “Dino Dance”, and “The Excavator Song”.

Blippi is an American educational YouTube channel that rocketed to global popularity since its first episode aired in 2014. His 2023 show, Blippi the Musical , was based on the original YouTube series written by Steve John, and it was nominated for an Olivier Award. Blippi On Tour has delighted millions of children worldwide, playing in countries like North America, Mexico, Africa and the UK!

His last show in London, Blippi: The Wonderful World Tour , ran for a limited time at the Harold Pinter Theatre in 2024.

Clayton Grimm is best known for portraying the character of Blippi since 2019. He has credits in theatre and voice acting. Clayton previously played The Flash in the DC Justice League tour. This run in the West End will offer audiences a rare opportunity to see Clayton perform live, as well as appearing as Blippi at this year’s Camp Bestival.

This summer, make a date with this energetic children’s production that is “sure to uplift both kids and adults alike” during its limited engagement in the heart of London. If you’re looking for a fantastic addition to your summer holiday plans this year, don’t miss out on the opportunity to play and learn with Blippi himself! Book your tickets for Be Like Blippi at Arts at Marble Arch today!

shows.name: The Jungle Book
shows.description_short: Step into a roarsome new play adaptation of the famous children’s story The Jungle Book at the National Theatre. Best for age 7+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 7+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
A new production of The Jungle Book is coming to the iconic National Theatre this autumn, located on the South Bank of the River Thames. Adapted by Indian playwright Anupama Chandrasekhar, the beloved tale is reimagined through innovative puppetry and stunning visuals – secure your tickets today.

The Jungle Book centres on Mowgli, a human boy who is raised by wolves after being separated from his parents. He’s mentored by a panther called Bagheera and a bear called Baloo, who teach him the laws and dangers of navigating the jungle. Whilst there’s a central fight between Mowgli and Shere Khan, the tiger, this is a timeless story about family, identity, courage, and the conflict between nature and humanity.

Hiran Abeysekera leads this new production of The Jungle Book as Mowgli. His television credits include Russell T. Davies’ A Midsummer Night’s Dream and Find Me in Paris , but he is best known for his performance in Lolita Chakrabarti’s adaptation of Life of Pi . The production, which made its debut at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield in 2019 and went on to have a West End transfer at Wyndham’s Theatre, earned him an Olivier Award for Best Actor in a Play for his performance as Pi Patel. Hiran also recently starred in the title role of Hamlet at the National Theatre.

The rest of the cast includes:

Indhu Rubasingham, the current Artistic Director of the National Theatre, directs this new production adapted by Anupama Chandrasekhar. She worked on The Father and the Assassin at the National, which won a nomination for Best Play at the Evening Standard Theatre Awards.

The production will feature puppetry by Nick Barnes and Finn Caldwell, set and costume design by Rajha Shakiry, movement direction by Finn Caldwell, lighting design by Oliver Fenwick and sound design by Benjamin Grant. Composer Fernado Velázquez will also be involved in The Jungle Book .

This major new adaptation of a treasured children’s story will soar on the Olivier Theatre stage at the National this Autumn. Be swept up by the magic of Rudyard Kipling's tale and the innovative puppetry that awaits you in the heart of the jungle.

shows.name: John Proctor is the Villain
shows.description_short: This moving, bitingly funny coming-of-age reimagining of Arthur Miller's The Crucible embarks on a West End run at Wyndham's Theatre.
shows.description_full:
Tony-nominated play John Proctor is the Villain transfers to the West End in 2027, following its sell-out debut in London at the Royal Court Theatre in Spring 2026. This bitingly witty coming-of-age dramady turns theatrical classic The Crucible on its head, examining what it means to be a teenage girl in a post #MeToo world, and who gets to decide who is the hero of the stories we tell. John Proctor is the Villain will “engage and electrify” (Guardian). Secure your tickets for the strictly limited run at Wyndham’s Theatre, located close to the electric atmosphere of Leicester Square, today.

In a rural Georgia town in 2018, a high-school honours English class sit down to study Arthur Miller’s The Crucible - but the teens are more preoccupied with sex, gossip, who’s making out with who, and conducting ‘witch hunts’ of their own than with a stuffy old play about the Salem witch trials.

As they try to figure out their place in a post-#MeToo world, a group of girls faces pushback from their conservative school administrators when they try to found a feminist club, and as they delve further into The Crucible, they start to question everything they previously thought about the play. Is its flawed yet celebrated protagonist, John Proctor, truly the hero their teacher purports him to be?

Both a response to and re-examination of The Crucible, John Proctor is the Villain flips the script on an American classic with a fresh new perspective that asks who the hero really is - “after watching it, you will never view ‘The Crucible’ in the same way again.” (Washington Post).

John Proctor is the Villain premiered in Washington DC in 2022. Following a further successful professional staging in Boston, the show made its Broadway debut in 2025, starring Stranger Things star Sadie Sink, and took an unusual path to New York - previously having been licensed for almost 100 non-professional productions, at high schools and colleges beforehand. The show’s Broadway premiere was a smash hit and earned seven Tony nominations. In 2025, a film adaptation, co-produced by Sadie Sink and Tina Fey, was greenlit, with Bellflower adapting her own script.

The production, which made its European premiere at the Royal Court Theatre in Spring 2026, sold out its entire run. The upcoming West End transfer to Wyndham’s Theatre is a result of the high demand and popularity of this show!

Casting for this production is to be announced.

“A fantastically cathartic play” (The Independent), “Absolutely nails the timeless fury of female adolescence” (The Telegraph), “The most validating theatrical experience a young woman can have” (A Young(ish) Perspective): critics adore the power of John Proctor is the Villain and the news of a West End transfer is exactly what this show deserves. If you’re looking for a piece of theatre that packs a punch, this five-star triumph is exactly that.

John Proctor is the Villain is written by American playwright and educator Kimberley Belflower. Bellflower is a Tony nominee and Drama Desk winner. Her other credits include Teen Girl FANtasies, Gondal , and The Sky Game. The 2025 production of John Proctor is the Villain , marking her Broadway debut, and now the play receives its West End premiere.

Tony winner Danya Taymor directs, fresh from a Tony-nominated run directing the show on Broadway. Taymor is an American director, and her credits include The Outsiders the Musical, Daddy, Familiar and Pass Over .

An American classic gets seen in a fresh new light in this feel-good, funny and moving play by one of America’s hottest young playwrights. It’s “absolutely essential” (WhatsOnStage) to book your tickets for John Proctor is the Villain and to get into “this utterly galvanising room” (BroadwayWorld) in 2027!

shows.name: A Midsummer Night's Dream
shows.description_short: Shakespeare’s enchanting comedy returns to Regent’s Park with music, mischief and magic under the stars.
shows.description_full:
Prepare to be enchanted as A Midsummer Night’s Dream comes to the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre, in the heart of glorious Regent’s Park, this summer. Shakespeare’s most beloved comedy, about the tangled love triangle between four mortals, complicated by the interference of mischievous fairies, comes alive in a gorgeous outdoor staging. Blending hilarity, folk‑infused music, and the natural beauty of London’s iconic parkland, A Midsummer Night’s Dream is a play that truly casts a spell - secure your tickets today.

A Midsummer Night’s Dream is a riotous tale of love, transformation, and magic that unfolds in an enchanted forest where anything can, and will, happen. Four young Athenians flee into the woods to escape social constraints, only to find themselves at the mercy of mischievous fairies and errant love potions. At the same time, the fairy king and queen are dealing with romantic troubles of their own, while a band of rustic actors rehearse a hilariously earnest play in a delightful metatheatrical twist. This new Regent’s Park production infuses Shakespeare’s classic comedy with folk‑inspired melodies; the open‑air setting amplifies the play’s themes of nature, illusion, and the joyfully unpredictable experience of falling in and out of love.

Since its first recorded performance in the late 16th century, A Midsummer Night’s Dream has been one of Shakespeare’s most frequently staged and visually expressive plays. Scholars believe it was written around 1595–1596, likely to be performed at a court wedding. Early stagings would have used minimal scenery, relying on the language and movement to evoke the magical forest setting.

A Midsummer Night’s Dream has long been associated with open‑air performance, its forested spell perfectly matched to the natural world. This production leans into that tradition, with live music, laughter, moonlight, and merry enchantment under the open sky.

Casting for this production includes:

The 2026 production of A Midsummer Night’s Dream at Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre is directed by Atri Banerjee ( The Glass Menagerie, Julius Caesar ). Original music is composed by Maimuna Memon ( Portia Coughlan, The Grapes of Wrath ).

Naomi Dawson ( Romeo and Juliet, Look Back in Anger ) is the set designer, with costume design by Tómas Palmer ( Run Sister Run, Gruesome Playground Injuries ). Lighting design is by Joshua Pharo ( Kenrex, Noughts and Croses ), with sound design by Max Pappenheim ( Twelfth Night, A Raisin in the Sun ) and movement direction by Anjali Mehra ( Jesus Christ Superstar, Marriage Material ).

If you’ve ever dreamed of Shakespeare where the rustle of leaves becomes part of the soundscape and moonlight co‑stars with the players, this summer’s A Midsummer Night’s Dream promises precisely that. Let Shakespeare’s raucous comedy wash through you beneath the stars - book your tickets for A Midsummer Night’s Dream at the Regent’s Park Open Air Theatre today

shows.name: Mother Courage and Her Children
shows.description_short: Brecht’s epic anti‑war drama makes its Globe premiere, starring Michelle Terry. With live music, biting humour and fierce humanity.
shows.description_full:
Witness history this summer as Mother Courage and Her Children comes to Shakespeare’s Globe, on vibrant Bankside. This visceral staging of Bertolt Brecht’s timeless epic about survival, commerce and the cost of conflict marks the first time this revolutionary playwright’s work has been staged at the Globe Theatre. This ambitious staging of one of Brecht’s masterwork play invites audiences into a world where war is not just a backdrop but the engine of life itself - secure your tickets for this remarkable production today.

Following the devastation of the Thirty Years’ War, Mother Courage hauls her war‑wagon through a landscape of conflict, selling supplies to soldiers and trying to keep her three children safe, even as the violent world around her exacts a devastating toll. Mother Courage will do whatever it takes to survive - but in the business of war, everything has a cost. Is she willing to pay the price?

This Globe production interrogates the brutal economics of conflict with live music, urgent performances and Brecht’s epic theatrical style, asking audiences to consider not just the horrors of war but the systems that perpetuate it. With humour, resilience and startling emotional depth, this staging brings Brecht’s incisive storytelling vividly to life beneath the London sky.

Written by Bertolt Brecht in 1939, Mother Courage and Her Children first debuted in 1941 in Zurich. Several productions have been staged since, many of which were directed by Brecht himself. In 1961, several years after Brecht's death, the play was adapted as a German film, Mutter Courage und ihre Kinder, starring Helene Weigel, Brecht's widow.

Considered a cornerstone of Brecht’s epic theatre, the play uses techniques that encourage reflection over mere emotional identification, blending music, direct address and episodic scenes to challenge audiences to think as much as feel. Mother Courage has remained an enduring indictment of war and profiteering, celebrated for its political force and theatrical innovation, and this blistering new staging marks a historic occasion for the Globe, as this production becomes the first ever staging of Brecht’s masterwork on its stage.

Michelle Terry stars as Mother Courage. Terry is an actor, writer and the Artistic Director of Shakespeare’s Globe. She has performed leading roles in numerous Globe productions, including Hamlet, The Merry Wives of Windsor , and As You Like It , as well as many roles at other prestigious venues, including the National Theatre, RSC, and Royal Court.

Further casting includes:

Mother Courage and Her Children is written by Bertolt Brecht, with significant contributions by Margarete Steffin. Brecht (1898–1956) was a German playwright, poet and director. His most influential works include The Threepenny Opera with composer Kurt Weill, The Caucasian Chalk Circle , and The Life of Galileo . Brecht’s techniques, including direct address, visible staging, and songs, reshaped 20th-century theatre and continue to influence political and socially engaged performance today.

Bruntwood Prize winner and Succession writer Anna Jordan provides the English translation. Her other credits include Yen , Lost Atoms, Becoming Elizabeth, One Day for Netflix and Killing Eve .

The production is directed by Globe Associate Artist Elle While ( A Midsummer Night’s Dream, Hamlet ). Music is composed by James Maloney ( Much Ado About Nothing, A Very Very Very Dark Matter ), with set and costume design by takis ( Wicked, A Midsummer Night's Dream ).

Witness a Globe Theatre first as Mother Courage and Her Children storms the yard with humanity, protest and unflinching theatrical power. Whether you’re drawn by Brecht’s epic vision, Michelle Terry’s commanding performance, or the Globe’s iconic setting, this production promises to resonate deeply. Don’t miss this chance to experience one of theatre’s greatest works at one of London’s most remarkable venues - book your tickets for Mother Courage and Her Children at Shakespeare’s Globe!

shows.name: Showstopper! The Improvised Musical
shows.description_short: You give a place, time and situation and they create a brand-new musical! Showstopper is perfect for theatre fans who enjoy the unexpected.
shows.typical_age_range: Recommended 12+. No Under 4’s. Under 16’s to be accompanied by an adult 18 years or over.
shows.description_full:
Be in the audience of a brand new musical every night as improv troupe The Showstoppers return for a Monday residency at the Cambridge Theatre in Seven Dials. Showstopper The Improvised Musical is a hilarious and brilliant display of quick-thinking creativity - so step aside Lloyd Webber, there’s some new musical theatre impresarios in town!

A top producer needs your help. He’s been asked to present a brand new musical - a sure-fire West End hit - but he’s stuck for ideas. Every night he takes prompts for name, setting, music genre and more from the audience, so you might find your suggestion brought to life live on stage.

From Phantom of the Operating Table to Sex, Drugs and Sausage Roll , every night is a premiere with the Showstopper team, and none of them know what they’ll be performing until the suggestions start rolling in.

This wacky and brilliant show, steered each night by a hugely talented team of actors, musicians and techies, premiered at Edinburgh Fringe Festival before transferring to the West End for a series of residencies. In 2016 it won the Olivier Award for Best Entertainment and Family Show, and has remained a fast favourite ever since.

Crowds rush to see what the team have got hidden up their sleeves every Monday night… and if there’s any end to their astounding improv skills, they’ve shown no signs of it yet!

The Showstopper troupe is headed up by creators and performers Adam Meggido and Dylan Emery, who devised and premiered the show back in 2008.

The cast on any given night includes Ruth Bratt, Justin Brett, Matt Cavendish, Jonathan Ainscough, Pippa Evans, Susan Harrison, Joshua C Jackson, Ali James, Sean McCann, Philip Pellew, Ethan Pascal Peters, Martha Pothen, Andrew Pugsley, Lauren Shearing, Joy Tan, Lucy Trodd and Heather Urquhart.

Showstopper also features a group of musicians who perform live every night - a crucial role for any musical, and you will be amazed by how quickly they can come up with melodies for the cast to sing along to.

Where else can you enjoy a musical debut every night?! Showstopper The Improvised Musical is the perfect combination of comedy and theatre and the joy is in not knowing what to expect. Andrew Lloyd Webber tunes in a bakery, jazz-hands on a space station… book Showstopper tickets for a unique evening of musical theatre that might even inspire you to write your own hit.

shows.name: Horrible Histories - Barmy Britain
shows.description_short: Enjoy history with all the nasty bits left in at this brilliant kids’ show! From Boudicca to Queen Victoria, best for ages 5+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 5+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Forget boring lessons about tedious Tudors and stuffy Saxons: Horrible Histories is back in the West End this summer with Barmy Britain ! This family show from the award-winning team behind Awful Egyptians and Ruthless Romans promises gruesomely good historical facts and hair-raising figures from the olden days. School’s out, but learning for fun is definitely in at the Apollo Theatre, near Tottenham Court Road.

Horrible Histories: Barmy Britain invites the whole family to experience history with all the nasty bits left in! From battling legendary warrior queen Boudicca and escaping dastardly graverobbers Burke and Hare, to a quick stop to party with Queen Victoria (you will be amused!), this is a riotous chance to get up close and personal with history’s most exciting moments and most eccentric people.

Following up on previous Barmy Britain productions, this is the ultimate pick ‘n’ mix of figures and events from the past. You might have heard of some, but you won’t know the full story, and this is a fantastic way to get kids interested in learning.

The best-selling Horrible Histories books were created by Terry Deary and inspired a hit television series full of catchy songs including “King of Bling” and “The Monarch’s Song”. They’ve since expanded to the big screen, with Horrible Histories: Rotten Romans invading cinemas.

On stage, shows including Groovy Greeks , Terrible Tudors and Ruthless Romans have played up and down the UK, created by multi-award-nominated children’s theatre creators Birmingham Stage Company. Artistic Director Neal Foster founded the company over three decades ago, and their hit productions include Awful Auntie , Gangsta Granny and Barmy Britain parts one to five!

Get ready to dodge the plague, catch the Gunpowder Plotters, stand and deliver to Dick Turpin… and have a wild ride through hundreds of years of brilliantly bonkers British history! Tickets for Horrible Histories: Barmy Britain - The Best Bits at Apollo Theatre are the ultimate treat for kids and big kids, aged 5 to 105.

shows.name: Hot Mess
shows.description_short: Inspired by the relationship between Earth and Humanity, Hot Mess is the ultimate rom-com musical for any theatre fan in 2026.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 12+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult, and may be refused entry otherwise.
shows.description_full:
Jack Godfrey and Ellie Coote’s “blazing musical about Earth and humanity’s toxic love affair” (The Guardian) returns to the stage following its triumphant debut in Birmingham, Edinburgh and London in 2025. Take your chance on a hopeful new musical and experience a fresh perspective on the climate crisis at The Other Palace, located just a short walk away from Buckingham Palace.

Told through the infectious narrative of a rom-com, Hot Mess explores the relationship between Earth and Humanity. Earth has been waiting for over a billion years for someone to come along that she can relate to, and when she meets Human, she thinks her luck has finally changed. But things start to become toxic between the pair when Human begins to take her for granted by not clearing up his mess, and begins to develop an interest in other planetary bodies. Hot Mess is a musical that ultimately shows the climate crisis as a relationship on the brink of collapse.

Previously entitled This is a Love Story , the production was both performed on the stage at the Dundee Rep and live-streamed in 2021. After developing the productions and creating new songs, the show was given a new title of Hot Mess and was commissioned by Vicky Graham Productions in its debut at the Birmingham Hippodrome.

Hot Mess went on to perform at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe in 2025, starring Danielle Steers as Earth and Tobias Turley as Humanity. It was hugely popular and ultimately made its first transfer to London, where it played at the Southwark Playhouse Elephant. It is the first production to transfer to the city from Birmingham’s in-house New Musical Theatre Department, launched in 2023.

Ellie Coote co-wrote and directed Hot Mess . Her credits include 42 Balloons , which has performed in the UK as well as toured to Chicago. Music and lyrics are by Jack Godfrey. He was the winner of the Best Composer, Lyricist, or Book Writer at The Stage Debut Awards in 2024 for 42 Balloons .

Hot Mess will make you excitedly reconsider your relationship with Earth, backed by the punchiest new music available for musical theatre fans! Book your tickets to experience this exceptionally brilliant new musical launch into the hearts of new audiences this year.

shows.name: My Son’s a Queer (But What Can You Do?)
shows.description_short: The joyful show about growing up queer with a supportive family, which has captured hearts worldwide, returns to the West End.
shows.work_performed_name: My Son’s a Queer (But What Can You Do?)
shows.description_full:
Known for blending queer joy with theatrical nostalgia, Rob Madge’s My Son’s A Queer (But What Can You Do?) heads to the Apollo Theatre, near Piccadilly Circus tube station. Get ready to participate in the ultimate Disney parade and experience a heartfelt story about growing up, identity, and unconditional support. Purchase your tickets now for the “Triumphant and transformative” (The Stage) Olivier Award-nominated show on its welcome return to the West End – just don’t forget to pack your tissues!

Armed with costumes, props, and wigs, 12-year-old Rob Madge creates a Disney parade in their living room. Rob confidently plays characters like Mary Poppins, Ariel, Mickey Mouse, and Belle, even though their parents struggle to keep up. Despite their challenges, Rob's parents' unwavering support highlights the importance of encouragement. Through a treasure trove of home videos and theatrical magic, My Son’s a Queer is a sparkling show that captured the hearts of people worldwide with its gorgeous celebration of the parents who simply say “yes”.

My Son’s A Queer (But What Can You Do?) makes its stage debut at the Turbine Theatre in 2021, where it won the WhatsOnStage Award for Best Off-West End. It went on to play at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe in 2021, before having two West End runs at the Garrick and Ambassadors Theatre.

Its transfer to Broadway in 2024 was initially cancelled, with a UK tour taking its place. The rescheduled season at the New York City Center took place in 2025.

Rob Madge stars in this one-man autobiographical show, My Son’s A Queer (But What Can You Do?) . Their theatre credits include Acorn Antiques , Cabaret and Mary Poppins . They have become a seasoned performer in the London Palladium pantomime over the last couple of years and will be starring in this year’s production of Sleeping Beauty.

Madge has also started writing some new productions, including Charley’s Aunt at the Watermill Theatre and Bank of Dave – The Musical , which premieres with a run at The Lowry, Salford and Curve Theatre, Leicester in 2026.

Written by Rob Madge, the show features music composed by Pippa Cleary and lyrics by Madge and Cleary. Pippa Cleary is an acclaimed musical theatre composer, with credits including The Great British Bake Off Musical and T he Secret Diary of Adrian Mole Aged 13¾ The Musical. She’s working with Rob Madge on Bank of Dave – The Musical.

My Son’s A Queer (But What Can You Do?) is a lively show that provides a memorable “insight into the lives of those who commit to defying society’s ‘norms’ and make the world a better place for it” (Musical Theatre Review). Get your tickets for My Son’s A Queer (But What Can You Do?) at the Apollo Theatre and enjoy this special, uplifting theatrical experience firsthand!

shows.name: The Tiger Who Came To Tea
shows.description_short: Join the tea-guzzling tiger this summer, in this Olivier Award-nominated family show that is now a West End favourite! Best for age 3+.
shows.typical_age_range: Ages 3+. Children under 16 must be accompanied by and sat next to an adult.
shows.work_performed_name: The Tiger Who Came To Tea
shows.description_full:
Who’s that prowling into the West End for another summer of fun? It’s Olivier Award-nominated hit show The Tiger Who Came To Tea , a fantastic story for little ones based on Judith Kerr’s picture book! This boisterous, upbeat play with music is back at Theatre Royal Haymarket near Piccadilly Circus for another year this summer and is the perfect introduction to theatre for your youngest family members.

Sophie and her mummy are about to sit down for their tea when they suddenly hear a loud knock at the door. Who could it be? Surely not a large… stripy… hungry tiger?! They invite him in, but is there enough food for such an unexpected guest?

Packed with colourful characters and costumes, fun sing-a-longs, lots of surprises, and an engaging story that we all know and love, this stage adaptation is magical, roar-some fun for the whole family.

The Tiger Who Came To Tea on stage is a popular show in the West End each summer, bringing the beloved picture book to life with incredible skill. It is adapted by a team of fantastic children’s theatre creatives, including director David Wood OBE, whose other credits include The Witches and Meg and Mog.

The Tiger Who Came To Tea has been adapted from Judith Kerr’s classic book by David Wood OBE. He’s written and directed several plays and musicals for children, including The Gingerbread Man , The Witches and Babe the Sheep Pig .

The creative team includes designer Susie Caulcutt, associate director/choreographer Emma Clayton, music arranger and supervisor Peter Pontzen, magical illusions by Scott Penrose, lighting designer Tony Simpson and sound designer Shock Productions.

Mischief and mayhem in The Tiger Who Came To Tea has been making children laugh and smile for decades, and this irresistible stage show in the West End is the perfect treat that grown-ups will adore too. Invite yourself to tea this summer - book tickets for The Tiger Who Came To Tea at the Theatre Royal Haymarket!

shows.name: Austentatious
shows.description_short: The Jane Austen-inspired improv troupe pulls a fantastic new show out from under their bonnets every night, based on your suggestions.
shows.description_full:
It is a truth universally acknowledged that an improv troupe with a taste for Jane Austen must be in want of an audience… Improv fans and Austenites rejoice as the riotous Austentatious play returns to The Strand at the Vaudeville Theatre. Enjoy one of the best nights out in the West End, thanks to a talented team who create a brand new story every night. Hold onto your bonnets and get ready for wit, romance, laughs and more!

How many novels did Jane Austen write? Get ready to discover a few new stories as the team behind Austentatious on stage take your title suggestions and turn them into fantastic period romps. From Mansfield Shark to The Longbourne Identity via The Empire Line Strikes Back , this sharp, smart and sometimes chaotic show is a good-natured tribute to the beloved Pride and Prejudice author. Expect full period dress, fulsome laughter, and a few surprises that will have you blushing more than Mr Darcy’s fan club!

Austentatious has a long and respectable history, starting back in 2011. It entered society - or to you and I, the theatre scene - in London before travelling up to Edinburgh, where it received rave reviews and played to sold-out crowds. Since 2017 it has held Monday night residencies at various West End theatres, and it is the talk of all the most influential newspapers: The Times called it a “potboiler of a parody, joyously performed” in their five-star review.

Austentatious is currently performed by a cast of six, including original members and creators Joseph Morpurgo and Rachel Parris ( Late Night Mash ). Other members of the company include award-winning comedian, actress and Griefcast podcast host Cariad Lloyd, Graham Dickson, Amy Cooke-Hodgson, Charlotte Gittins, and Daniel Nills Roberts.

The group pull on their petticoats, dust off their cravats and practice their stiff upper lip before every performance to bring you the very best madcap improv you could ask for.

Austentatious is the perfect night out whether you’re a die-hard Austenite or only caught the end of Pride and Prejudice on television. This “pop-up book of creative genius” (Broadway Baby), full of whip-smart jokes and silly plots that are guaranteed to put a smile on your face. Book now to enjoy one of the most eligible shows in London!

shows.name: Samson et Dalila
shows.description_short: Saint‑Saëns’s grand biblical opera of love, betrayal and strength returns to London in Richard Jones’s vibrant staging.
shows.description_full:
Richard Jones’ acclaimed staging of Samson et Dalila returns to the Royal Opera House, in the heart of Covent Garden. This dramatic operatic retelling of the biblical tale, in which Samson’s remarkable strength is tested by love, faith and loyalty, stars Aigul Akhmetshina and SeokJong Baek as the troubled lovers. You won't want to miss it - secure your tickets today

Set against the backdrop of the Israelite struggle against Philistine oppression, the opera unfolds Samson’s tragic arc from heroic leader to a man undone by passion. Samson, a leader of the Hebrews with superhuman strength, falls in love with Dalila, a Philistine woman who leads him into a web of seduction and betrayal. Dalila works with the Philistine High Priest to discover the secret of Samson’s strength; when it is revealed that his power lies in his hair, he is captured and blinded. In the opera’s climax, Samson regains his strength through prayer and brings down the temple of Dagon upon himself and his enemies.

Saint‑Saëns’s score blends operatic drama with choral writing that reflects the work’s oratorio origins, featuring lyrical solos, expressive ensembles and the celebrated Bacchanale in Act III.

Samson et Dalila , based on the biblical story of Samson and Delilah from the Book of Judges, was composed by Camille Saint‑Saëns in the late 1860s and first performed in 1877 in Weimar, where it was conducted by Franz Liszt. It remains the composer’s most performed opera, with its blend of dramatic narrative, choral writing and expressive arias, particularly “Mon cœur s’ouvre à ta voix”, a staple for mezzo‑sopranos worldwide.

While originally conceived with oratorio elements, Saint‑Saëns and librettist Ferdinand Lemaire expanded the work into a full dramatic opera, focusing on the psychological interplay between devotion, desire and betrayal.

This Royal Opera House production by Richard Jones blends contemporary theatrical insight with the opera’s powerful score and epic themes, making it a compelling experience for traditional opera audiences and newcomers alike.

Samson et Dalila at the Royal Opera House is directed by Richard Jones ( Peter Grimes, The Queen of Spades ). Alex Soddy ( Elektra, Animal Farm ) Conducts

Set design is by Hyemi Shin ( The Cunning Little Vixen, I Puritani, A Midsummer Night’s Dream ), and costumes are designed by Nicky Gillibrand ( Billy Budd, The Magic Flute, Hansel and Gretel ). Lighting design is by Andreas Fuchs ( Jenůfa, La Clemenza di Tito ), and movement direction is by Lucy Burge ( Peter Grimes, La traviata, The Cunning Little Vixen ). The opera is composed by Camille Saint‑Saëns ( The Carnival of the Animals, Organ Symphony No. 3, Danse macabre ) with a French libretto by Ferdinand Lemaire.

Casting varies by performance date. Principal cast includes:

This vivid staging from one of the UK's top opera directors, Olivier winner Richard Jones, blends intense emotion, striking music and dramatic storytelling. From the seductive interplay between its leads to the overwhelming force of its choruses and orchestral writing, Saint‑Saëns opera stands as a cornerstone of the repertory. Book your tickets to Samson et Dalila at the Royal Opera House today.

shows.name: As You Like It
shows.description_short: Escape into the Forest of Arden with this revelatory and joyous festival-themed staging of Shakespeare’s beloved pastoral comedy.
shows.description_full:
All of London's a stage this summer as As You Like It returns to Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre, on Bankside. Escape the confines of the city and revel in the forest with this hilarious play about two young women who flee their stifling lives in court and head into the woods, seeking self-discovery. This bold new adaptation of Shakespeare’s witty, gender-bending comedy simmers with lust, live music and festival spirit, and celebrates freedom, friendship and the redemptive power of love. Secure your tickets today!

Rosalind and Celia have escaped the restrictions of the court and are searching for Rosalind’s father, the exiled Duke. Orlando has fallen head over heels and wants the whole world to know about it. And, deep in the Forest of Arden, unexpected meetings, long-awaited reunions and incredible transformations are about to take place…

As You Like It is an exuberant celebration of love as friends, cousins, couples and parents come together under the liberating canopy of the forest. It is one of Shakespeare’s best-known and best-loved comedies, and has been performed all over the world and reinterpreted in myriad ways since it was written over 400 years ago.

As You Like It is one of William Shakespeare’s most beloved pastoral comedies, believed to have been written in 1599 or early 1600. It was first published in the First Folio in 1623, but no earlier quarto version survives. The play is thought to have premiered at the Globe Theatre, captivating Elizabethan audiences with its mix of romance, wit, and disguise. Based on Thomas Lodge’s prose romance Rosalynde , Shakespeare’s version transforms the story with greater emotional depth and clever wordplay. Set in the idyllic Forest of Arden, it explores themes of love, gender, exile, and nature versus society.

As You Like It remains a popular choice for modern productions, thanks to its engaging heroine and witty philosophical musings, including the famous “All the world’s a stage” monologue. Its playful subversion of gender roles and celebration of love in many forms continue to resonate with contemporary audiences, and this brand new production transplants the action to a vibrant, festival-themed forest where lust, love and self-discovery are waiting just around the corner or... tree!

Confirmed cast includes:

Further casting is to be announced.

As You Like It is written by William Shakespeare.

This production is co‑directed by Associate Artistic Director of Shakespeare’s Globe and former Artistic Director of the Lyric Hammersmith, Sean Holmes ( Romeo and Juliet, Pinocchio ), and writer-director Charlie Josephine ( Pinocchio, Cowbois ), who also stars as Orlando. Design is by Paul Wills ( The Comedy of Errors, The Tempest ) with further creative team to be confirmed.

You won't just like it - you’ll love it! As You Like It is a playful, joyous story, and one of Shakespeare’s most popular comedies - and this sexy, spirited and bold new production is more liberating than ever before. Journey into the Forest of Arden and delight in what - and who - you find: secure your tickets for As You Like It at Shakespeare's Globe Theatre today.

shows.name: The Enormous Crocodile
shows.description_short: Gobble up a fantastic family musical this summer! Roald Dahl’s book is turned into a snappy show with songs and puppets. Best for age 3+.
shows.typical_age_range: Best for age 3+. Under 16s must be accompanied and seated next to an adult.
shows.description_full:
Get ready for a snappy, toe-tapping treat as The Enormous Crocodile slithers back to London this summer, at the Lyric Hammersmith in the heart of Hammersmith! This wickedly funny musical, based on Roald Dahl’s classic tale, brings the jungle to life with a menagerie of puppets, catchy tunes, and one very hungry crocodile on the hunt. Can the other animals stop his secret plans and clever tricks? Developed by an award-winning team, this lively family show is “inventive, jaunty and entertaining” (West End Best Friend) and is packed with adventure, laughs, and larger-than-life fun!

Deep in the jungle, the Enormous Crocodile is on the prowl, and he’s got a very particular craving… juicy little children! Armed with “secret plans and clever tricks,” this beastly reptile slithers his way through the jungle, determined to snap up a tasty treat. But the other animals have had enough of his greedy ways, and with a bit of teamwork (and a lot of courage), they just might be able to stop him before it’s too late!

This lively musical adaptation of Roald Dahl’s classic book is packed with cheeky humour, thrilling adventure, and toe-tapping tunes. With dazzling puppetry and a jungle bursting with character, it’s a riotous celebration of wit, friendship, and standing up to bullies - perfect for little explorers and their grown-ups alike!

Casting for this production is to be announced.

This mischievous musical is brought to life by an award-winning creative team. Suhayla El-Bushra ( The Ramayana, Hollyoaks ) adapts Roald Dahl’s classic tale with a script full of humour and heart, providing book and lyrics, while Ahmed Abdullahi Gallab, known for his musical work as Sinkane, provides the show’s infectious melodies alongside additional music and lyrics by Tom Brady ( Romeo and Juliet, Lord of the Rings ).

The production is developed and directed by Emily Lim ( Another Fine Mess ), with co-direction and inventive puppetry design by Toby Olié ( Life of Pi, Pinocchio ). Set and costume design is by Fly Davis ( Caroline, or Change, Standing at the Sky’s Edge ), with puppetry co-design and supervision by Daisy Beattie ( Ocean at the End of the Lane ). With this top-tier team at the helm, The Enormous Crocodile is packed with creative flair, jungle magic, and larger-than-life fun!

The Enormous Crocodile is a wickedly fun adventure for the whole family. Roald Dahl’s classic story bursts to life in the open air, making this the perfect summer treat for little theatre fans. Don’t miss your chance to see this “fun, vibrant and interactive” (Afridiziak Theatre News) mischievous musical - book your tickets for The Enormous Crocodile at the Lyric Hammersmith Theatre now, before they’re gobbled up!

shows.name: Cinderella: The Pantomime
shows.description_short: Expect show-stopping songs and side-splitting comedy at Lyric Hammersmith’s pantomime - a delight for the whole family. Best for age 6+.
shows.description_full:
You shall go to the panto this festive season as Lyric Hammersmith’s iconic festive treat is back. Jam-packed with sass, comedy, and catchy pop songs - as well as the unforgettable audience participation - this is a fabulous alternative to traditional pantomime for everyone in the family to enjoy. Secure your tickets to Cinderella at the Lyric Hammersmith today!

Don’t forget to wear your best and head to the biggest party of the season! Brought to the stage in a West London setting, the treasured rags-to-riches story of Cinderella has been loved for generations. Follow Cinders, who lives under the thumb of her selfish stepmother and her two stepsisters, who constantly make life difficult for her. When news of a ball comes to life, Cinders is determined to make it there with the help of her fairy godmother!

This year’s festive offering of Cinderella promises show-stopping songs, laugh-out-loud comedy and the signature twist of a Lyric Hammersmith pantomime!

Lyric Hammersmith has been producing pantomimes in West London since the 19th century, where they are known for their contemporary spin on classic stories. After a long hiatus, the theatre’s former director, Sean Holmes, brought family pantomimes back to the stage with a production of Jack and the Beanstalk in 2008.

The last time Cinderella was staged at Lyric Hammersmith was in 2003, when it was written by Vikki Stone and directed by Tonderai Munyevu.

Casting for this production is to be announced.

Cinderella at Lyric Hammersmith will be written by Joel Horwood and directed by Robin Belfield.

Joel Horwood is a British playwright. He has written pantomimes at the Lyric Hammersmith previously, including Dick Whittington and Jack and the Beanstalk . His other theatre credits include The Ocean at the End of the Lane , I Want My Hat Back , and The Little Match Girl .

Robin Belfield is an Associate Director for The Unicorn Theatre. His directing credits include Princess Essex, The Twits, Charlotte’s Web , and Mother Goose .

If you’re looking to experience a pantomime “as sweet and warm as a hot chocolate” (The Reviews Hub), Lyric Hammersmith is the place to be this year. Cinderella is the perfect fit for festive family entertainment - book your tickets today!

shows.name: Tao of Glass
shows.description_short: Award-winning collaborators Philip Glass and Phelim McDermott come together in this moving tapestry of music and Taoist Wisdom.
shows.description_full:
Boundary-pushing new show Tao of Glass makes its West End Premiere this summer at Soho Place, just off Tottenham Court Road. This groundbreaking collaboration between composer Phillip Glass and performer-director, Improbable’s Phelim McDermott, combines brand new music with Taoist wisdom in a “truly magical journey” (WhatsOnStage) that meditates on life and loss. Don’t miss it - secure your tickets today.

Where does inspiration truly come from? Sparked by a dream, this innovative new show explores this question, merging ten brand-new pieces of music from Grammy and Academy Award-nominated composer Philip Glass, with Taoist wisdom, meditating on loss, life and meaning. Part performance, part concert, this remarkable production weaves a mesmerising tapestry of music and storytelling, in the most personal collaboration yet from long-standing creative colleagues Philip Glass and Phelim McDermott.

Tao of Glass premiered at the acclaimed Manchester International Arts Festival in 2019, to critical acclaim, as a co-commission between Factory International, Manchester, Improbable, Perth Festival, Ruhrfestspiele Recklinghausen, Hong Kong New Vision Arts Festival and Carolina Performing Arts – University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill in association with Naomi Milgrom AO.

Tao of Glass is the latest collaboration between Philip Glass, who composes music for the piece, and Phelim McDermott, who writes, co-directs and performs. The pair have previously worked together on Opera productions in London, New York and internationally.

Philip Glass is widely regarded as one of the most influential composers of the late 20th Century. The founder of the Philip Glass ensemble, his work spans opera, musical theatre, film scores and chamber and orchestral pieces. His credits include scores for The Hours, Notes on a Scandal, and The Truman Show , as well as operas Einstein on the Beach, and The Quiet American . Glass’s numerous accolades include a BAFTA Award, a Drama Desk Award, and a Golden Globe, nominations for three Academy Awards, four Grammy Awards, and a Primetime Emmy Award, as well as the Ordre des Arts et des Lettres, the National Medal of Arts, Kennedy Center Honors, a Grammy Trustees Award and a Lifetime Achievement from the World Soundtrack Academy.

Phelim McDermott is a performer-director. A founding member and co-artistic director of award-winning theatre group Improbable, McDermott is an Olivier Award winner. His credits include Satyagraha, Akhnaten, and The Perfect American with Glass, Shockheaded Peter for the West Yorkshire Playhouse, Leeds, and the Lyric Hammersmith, The Enchanted Island for the Metropolitan Opera, Così fan tutte for ENO and My Neighbour Totoro at the RSC, Barbican Centre and in the West End.

Kirsty Housley ( Drive Your Plow Over The Bones Of The Dead ) is co-director, with design by Fly Davis ( Caroline or Change ), lighting design by Colin Grenfell (Medea), sound design by Giles Thomas ( Master Harold and the Boys ) and musical direction by Chris Vatalaro. Little Angel Theatre co-founder Lyndie Wright ( The Magic Flute ) is the puppet designer and puppet maker

Tao of Glass is produced by Factory International, Improbable and Nica Burns.

This innovative show interweaves Taoist wisdom with the music of legendary composer Philip Glass in a boundary-pushing, deeply moving performance. “Every so often your eyes will fill with tears, for there is only so much that the human heart can hold within it” (Guardian) - don’t miss this remarkable piece of theatre, secure your tickets for Tao of Glass at Soho Place today.
